# 00 Current Work

Dashboard route rename checkpoint on 2026-07-15: the main management dashboard route was moved from `/dashboard` to `/dashboard-overview`, while `/dashboard` now remains only as a compatibility redirect to the new route. What is what: `Dashboard Overview` is the user-facing page name and should match the route users navigate to, while the underlying API contract stays `GET /api/dashboard`. Why it stays this way: the old short route had drifted away from the actual page naming and was easy to confuse with other dashboard surfaces; keeping a redirect preserves old bookmarks and internal links during the transition. Navigation, permission code, formulas, API payloads, schema, and business data were not changed.

Owner Daily dashboard runtime follow-up on 2026-07-15: `/owner-daily` was loading again but still felt slow because it does not have a separate read model; `GET /api/owner-daily` re-exports `GET /api/dashboard` and therefore runs the same `buildMainDashboards()` batch as the full dashboard page. What is what: Owner Daily is only a filtered dashboard view, not an isolated API route. Why it had to be fixed this way: the earlier pool-stability checkpoint on 2026-07-07 documented that the dashboard batch must respect low Prisma/Supabase pool limits, but `apps/next/src/lib/server/main-dashboards.ts` had regressed so `runReadBatch()` still executed the large read set via `Promise.all(...)`. The helper now executes the batch sequentially again, preserving the same formulas and payload while reducing pool contention for `/dashboard` and `/owner-daily` together. APIs, permissions, schema, and business data were not changed.

SIT database refresh checkpoint on 2026-07-15: the new SIT Supabase project `vbjlkxbytccklhqvxjuu` was refreshed from the active dev-target project `fhglqymcdmrgbsbadnwr`. What is what: SIT is the database behind the `devkub1/ns-dev` Vercel project and is separate from the older customer UAT projects. The refresh backed up the pre-restore SIT state under `/tmp/ns-erp-dev-to-sit-20260715-034341`, restored dev application schemas `public`, `maintenance`, and `supabase_migrations`, and replaced the required Supabase Auth identity rows (`auth.users`, `auth.identities`) so app-user foreign keys and login identity data match dev. Why it has to be like this: the SIT app had already been redeployed with SIT env, but its database did not yet contain the active application schema/data; cloning dev into SIT makes code, runtime env, application schema, business data, migration history, and auth identities point at one consistent integration snapshot. Post-refresh reconciliation matched dev exactly for the checked database set: 202 tables and 67,008 rows across application schemas plus auth users/identities, with 0 row-count differences. The 6 not-yet-validated check constraints in SIT match dev exactly and are not a restore-created drift.

SIT login/runtime env repair checkpoint on 2026-07-15: `POST /api/auth/login-complete` on `https://ns-erp-sit.vercel.app` failed after the database refresh even though Supabase password sign-in succeeded. What is what: the browser login first authenticates with Supabase Auth, then the Next.js route uses Prisma/pg to read `app_users`, update `last_login_at`, and write the auth audit event. Why it had to be fixed this way: the restore was intentionally run without ownership/privileges, so Supabase Data API roles needed grants restored on `public`; Vercel also needed the app-specific runtime keys `DATABASE_URL`, `DATABASE_PRISMA_URL`, and `DATABASE_RUNTIME_URL`, not only `POSTGRES_*`. The Prisma/pg runtime additionally required `uselibpqcompat=true&sslmode=require` on the Supabase pooler connection string to avoid `SELF_SIGNED_CERT_IN_CHAIN`. After restoring grants, updating SIT env, and redeploying `devkub1/ns-dev`, browser smoke verification confirmed Supabase token response `200`, `/api/auth/login-complete` response `200`, and redirect to `/`.

Customer UAT auth-helper repair checkpoint on 2026-07-14: customer UAT login failures at `https://ns-erp-uat.vercel.app/` were traced to a schema gap in the active UAT Supabase project `ekeomeumqjvbhgwyaqwe`, not to a missing env file. What is what: `apps/next/.env.uat.local` is only a local ignored reference file for UAT credentials and is not read automatically by Vercel; the live app still depends on the deployed Vercel env plus the UAT database schema. The runtime proxy calls `public.current_app_user_access_context()` after Supabase session validation, but UAT had `has_app_permission()` while missing `current_app_user_access_context()`, so authenticated requests failed with `ตรวจสอบบัญชีผู้ใช้งานไม่สำเร็จ`. The missing migration `20260712120000_add_current_app_user_access_context.sql` was applied directly to the UAT database and recorded in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`; post-fix verification confirmed both helper functions exist, the migration history row exists, Supabase password sign-in for `watcharathat@gmail.com` succeeds, and authenticated RPC `current_app_user_access_context` now returns `app_user_id=1` with `must_change_password=false`. Why it stays this way: auth/session env can be correct while login still fails if the helper-function contract between proxy and DB drifts, so UAT verification must always include both deployed env and required auth helper migrations.

Customer UAT full-refresh checkpoint on 2026-07-14: after fixing the helper/schema drift, customer UAT was intentionally overwritten from the current dev integration state, including code, schema, business data, and business storage files. What is what: the customer-facing UAT runtime is the separate Git repo `uat-origin` plus Supabase project `ekeomeumqjvbhgwyaqwe`; it is not just `new-origin/uat`, and it must be refreshed on both the Git/deploy side and the live database/storage side when the user asks for a full UAT reset. Why it has to be like this: comparing only repo code was insufficient because earlier failures were caused by database/schema/data drift. The 2026-07-14 refresh therefore force-promoted `new-origin/dev` into `uat-origin/main` and `uat-origin/uat`, aligned the runtime schema to dev, replaced UAT application data from dev for `public`, `maintenance`, application-owned `auth`, and `supabase_migrations` tables, and synced the business storage buckets `product-images` and `weight-ticket-pdfs`. Verification after the refresh confirmed core business table counts match dev, storage object counts match dev (`product-images=124`, `weight-ticket-pdfs=402`), and direct Supabase password sign-in for `watcharathat@gmail.com` still succeeds on UAT. Remaining count differences were only verification-generated runtime log tables, not business-state tables.

Topbar theme surface checkpoint on 2026-07-14: AppShell Topbar now uses a white surface with light slate text, controls, and search-result panel in light mode; in dark mode its background directly uses the same `--ns-dark-surface-soft` workspace surface instead of a separate navy band. What is what: Topbar is the global navigation/action chrome and its search result panel belongs to that same surface. Why it stays this way: the global chrome must not introduce an extra dark surface above the workspace, while the thin divider still preserves the boundary. Navigation, search behavior, theme state, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Theme toggle switch checkpoint on 2026-07-14: `ThemeModeToggle` now uses the TweakCN-style accessible switch geometry (`h-6 w-11`) instead of the previous standalone icon. What is what: the thumb shows Sun in light mode and Moon in dark mode, while `role="switch"`, `aria-checked`, and the thumb translation communicate the same state to keyboard and assistive-technology users. Its light state uses a soft neutral track with white thumb; dark state uses a white track with `#171717` thumb. Why it stays this way: the persistent track makes the current global theme readable before interaction without adding semantic colors or a submenu; the existing one-click theme change and circular View Transition remain unchanged. Theme persistence, routes, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Active-field baseline checkpoint on 2026-07-14: native inputs, selects, textareas, and shared comboboxes now use one central TweakCN active-field contract. What is what: focus is the temporary editing state for every editable form control, including Topbar menu search, not a page-specific visual feature. Why it stays this way: every field keeps its existing theme background and idle border, while both themes use only the neutral `#737373` focus border plus translucent 3px ring; this replaces the former Topbar-only exception and prevents blue or green focus from being confused with a selected/success state. Form values, validation, search behavior, results, navigation, theme persistence, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Profit & Cost Top 10 header-surface checkpoint on 2026-07-14: the three Top 10 panel headers on `/profit-cost-analysis` now use the same table-header surface as the report table. What is what: the Top 10 title strip is a structural header, while the card body remains a report-summary surface. Why it stays this way: matching the table-header surface groups the page's reporting blocks without adding another competing dark shade. Top 10 data, ordering, expand/collapse behavior, filters, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Theme toggle view-transition checkpoint on 2026-07-14: the top-bar theme toggle retains the existing `next-themes` state and now animates the new theme as a circular reveal from the icon's position. What is what: the icon remains an immediate light/dark command, while the visual reveal communicates the global theme change without a secondary menu. Why it stays this way: the transition makes a page-wide setting change legible without adding a heavier control; browsers without View Transition support and users who prefer reduced motion receive the same immediate, accessible theme change with no animation. Theme persistence, system-theme handling, routes, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Profit & Cost Analysis desktop-table typography checkpoint on 2026-07-14: `/profit-cost-analysis` now loads `Sarabun` for only its desktop product and report tables. What is what: column headers use 13.5px `medium` (500), ordinary text and numeric values use 13.5px `normal` (400), and decision values such as GP retain `medium`; mobile cards, KPI cards, and filter controls keep the existing Noto Sans Thai treatment. Why it stays this way: the dense desktop report table is scanned across many columns, so Sarabun's lighter Thai letterforms and reduced repeated bold/semibold text improve readability while preserving emphasis for financially important values. Table data, sort/filter/pagination behavior, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Pagination page-size standardization checkpoint on 2026-07-14: all active Next.js pagination page-size controls now use the shared `PageSizeDropdown` instead of native `<select>`, searchable comboboxes, or local menus. What is what: page size is a fixed single-choice pagination setting, while business lookup fields remain their own dropdown or searchable controls. Why it stays this way: a single shared menu keeps its trigger, option text, dark-mode contrast, hover state, and no-checkmark behavior consistent everywhere, without changing each page's allowed sizes or reset-to-first-page behavior. Its focus ring is neutral slate rather than the generic green action focus, so a currently focused pagination setting is not mistaken for a success/selected state. Pagination state, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Git synchronization completion checkpoint on 2026-07-14: local source head `30812836` was composed with fetched `new-origin/dev` head `2c33a5b5` through merge commit `545b742`, then with the two teammate commits that arrived during validation through merge commit `eca7589c`. The semantic resolution kept the accepted local Sales Plan/Sales Commission filter layout, responsive cards, Excel exports, table sizing, LINE PB/SB/PMT/RCP behavior, Sidebar, payment, receipt, and table checkpoints while retaining incoming Sales Plan persistence, customer/product pickers, Pending controls, pagination, PO Sell linkage, ADV/VAT logic, Stock Ledger grouping, and the latest customer payload/LME-field changes. A normal non-force push advanced `new-origin/dev` from `bc494d89` to application head `eca7589c`; post-push `ls-remote`, fetch, and divergence checks matched with `ahead 0 / behind 0`. Fresh validation passed: targeted merge lint, workspace type-check, full Vitest `70/70`, full workspace lint with zero errors and one pre-existing warning in `scripts/qa-thai-font.tsx`, npm audit with zero vulnerabilities, two production builds generating all 306 pages, conflict-marker and `git diff --check` scans, and the local HTTPS/login/RSC guard across 106 routes and 213 checks with zero failures. This documentation-only follow-up records the verified application merge and does not change runtime code.

Semantic merge guard confirmed by the user on 2026-07-13: when another developer changes a page that already contains accepted local UX/UI work, synchronization must preserve both sides instead of replacing the whole file. For the current `dev` divergence, keep the local Sales Plan/Sales Commission filters, tables, responsive cards, export behavior, and other accepted UI in `MainSalesControlClients.tsx`, then integrate the remote Live LME fetch/save, provider, read-only API-field, and PO Sell customer-preservation behavior. Preserve every local checkpoint in this handoff while accepting the remote stale-marker deletion. Before any push, prove the three local LINE commits and all accepted local UI are still present, run targeted lint/type/tests, and perform rendered regression checks on every overlapping page. No push is authorized merely because Git reports a clean textual merge.

Sales Plan add-form popup checkpoint on 2026-07-11: `/sales-plan` now opens `+ เพิ่มแผน` as a dark-header modal instead of expanding the form above the planning table. The same product, channel, customer, quantity, LME, calculated-summary, reset, validation, and draft-row behavior remains intact; `ยกเลิก` closes the popup and `เพิ่มเข้าตาราง` adds the draft then closes it. What is what: the table remains the planning workspace, while the modal is the focused entry surface for one draft plan. Why it stays this way: moving the wide entry form out of the page preserves the table context and prevents the page from jumping when a user starts a new plan. This is UI placement only; plan calculations, API contracts, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Dark dropdown menu contrast checkpoint on 2026-07-14: shared `DropdownMenuContent` and checkbox items now expose semantic slots with explicit dark-mode foreground/background rules. What is what: page-size menus and other portal dropdown menus render outside their local page surface, while checked/highlighted rows are interactive states inside that menu. Why it stays this way: portal menus must not depend on a parent text color that may be absent in dark mode; every option remains readable and the selected page size stays visibly distinct. Dropdown behavior, pagination, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Page-size dropdown selection checkpoint on 2026-07-14: shared `PageSizeDropdown` now uses ordinary single-choice menu items rather than checkbox items. What is what: page size has exactly one current value, unlike a multi-select filter. Why it stays this way: the trigger already states the current page size, so the popup stays a plain option list with only hover/focus feedback; a checkmark or persistent selected row incorrectly suggests a second selection model. Pagination count, navigation, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Profit & Cost page-size control follow-up on 2026-07-14: the product table now uses the shared `PageSizeDropdown` rather than `SearchCombobox`. What is what: product category/supplier/customer controls remain searchable because they select from business master data; page size is a small fixed pagination setting. Why it stays this way: a fixed set of row counts needs an ordinary dropdown, not a search field. Product data, sorting, page navigation, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Purchase Bills segmented-filter color checkpoint on 2026-07-14: `/purchase/bills` now uses the shared `SegmentedFilterButton` for document type and status filters. Active selections use a brighter slate surface and border; inactive choices are transparent with muted text and a light hover surface. What is what: these are true filters, not tabs or actions. Why it stays this way: the selected condition must be visible immediately in dark mode without making every possible condition read as selected. Filter behavior, multi-select status semantics, document data, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Dark-mode table surface checkpoint on 2026-07-14: shared table body and shell use the same `#111a28` base as the workspace, while table headers use the lighter table-surface `#1b2635`. What is what: the body remains part of the page reading plane and the header is the structural grouping plane. Why it stays this way: a continuous workspace/table background reduces competing dark-blue surfaces, while the slightly lighter header preserves column scanning without restoring prominent borders. Table data, sort/filter/pagination behavior, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Profit & Cost Analysis KPI consolidation checkpoint on 2026-07-14: `เจ้าหนี้คงเหลือ` (AP) and `ลูกหนี้คงเหลือ` (AR) are now displayed as two values inside one shared breakdown KPI card. What is what: AP and AR remain independent balances and retain their supplier/customer counts; the card only combines their visual container. Why it stays this way: both values are outstanding-counterparty balances in the same reporting scope, so two large adjacent cards repeat visual structure without adding a separate decision. Read model, calculations, filters, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Sales Plan LME cf form follow-up on 2026-07-11: the `+ เพิ่มแผน` popup now includes required `LME cf (USD/MT)`. Selecting a product pre-fills it from that product group's current LME reference, while allowing a plan-specific edit; the plan price, row LME value, and preview card use this entered LME cf. What is what: LME Reference Pricing remains the page-wide default, while LME cf is the quoted LME reference used by an individual draft plan. Why it stays this way: each customer plan may need an explicit LME reference without silently changing the global LME configuration. This remains draft-only UI calculation behavior; API, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Sales Plan draft-form simplification on 2026-07-11: the `กำไรคาดการณ์` preview and its client-only draft calculations were removed from the `+ เพิ่มแผน` popup. What is what: the popup now previews only the selected metal group, total weight, LME cf/FX, and the derived selling price. Why it stays this way: the entry flow captures a sell plan and quoted LME reference, while profitability analysis remains available in the separate product analysis/report surface. API, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Sales Plan product picker follow-up on 2026-07-11: the `สินค้า` control in the `+ เพิ่มแผน` popup now reads the active eligible products supplied by the product master instead of the page's product-analysis rows. It is a searchable combobox that matches product code, product name, or metal group. What is what: Product Master is the selectable product source, while product analysis remains the report surface. Why it stays this way: a plan must be creatable for every eligible active product, even when it has no current stock/analysis row. This is a read-source and form-control change only; plan persistence, API permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Sales Plan customer picker follow-up on 2026-07-11: the `ลูกค้า` control in the `+ เพิ่มแผน` popup now reads active customers from Customer Master. It is a searchable combobox that matches customer code or name, and the draft row retains the selected customer code and name. What is what: Customer Master is the only customer selection source for a sales plan. Why it stays this way: a sales plan must identify an existing customer consistently instead of retaining untraceable free text. This is a draft-form source/validation change only; plan persistence, API permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Sales Plan analysis tabs follow-up on 2026-07-11: the executive `วิเคราะห์แผนขาย vs สต๊อกว่างขาย` and `สต๊อกว่างขายคงเหลือหลังหักล็อกราคา` tables are now presented as mutually exclusive line tabs. What is what: the first tab supports pricing/management decisions; the second tab verifies the saleable stock remaining in the selected month after locked quantities. Why it stays this way: these are alternative views of the same planning scope, so showing one table at a time reduces repeated rows and makes the selected decision context explicit. Table data, formulas, filters, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Sales Plan pending-sale table follow-up on 2026-07-11: the `ตารางรอขาย` now shows the product-level decision data from the sales-planning read model in the reference layout: pending-sale quantity, Pool cost, best proposal price, LME %, projected profit/Margin, real pending sale, locked sale, open PO Buy, and stock. What is what: Pool values describe the matched Cost Pool inventory; real pending sale is the available balance after incoming PO Buy and locked PO Sell. Why it stays this way: the table must let users compare the sell proposal and margin against the actual available balance without manually reconciling several reports. Data sources, formulas, filters, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Sales Plan pending-sale proposal source follow-up on 2026-07-11: `ราคาเสนอที่ดีสุด`, `% LME`, projected profit, and Margin in `ตารางรอขาย` now derive only from the highest priced matching row currently present in the sales-plan table. Products without a plan show `-` for these proposal-dependent values. What is what: global LME Reference Pricing is a planning default; a sales-plan row is the product-specific commercial proposal. Why it stays this way: the pending-sale report must not imply a sell offer exists before a planner has entered one. Stock/PO balances, filters, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Sales Plan pending-sale ordering follow-up on 2026-07-11: `ตารางรอขาย` now lists products with an entered sales-plan price and LME % first, followed by products without a plan in descending pending-sale quantity. What is what: rows with plan data are actionable commercial proposals, while the remaining rows are inventory awaiting a proposal. Why it stays this way: decision-ready items should be immediately visible at the top of the report without hiding unplanned stock. Data sources, formulas, APIs, permissions, schema, and database state are unchanged.

Purchase Payments checkpoint on 2026-07-13: `/purchase/payments` now treats the payment tab as an approved-PMA work queue and the history tab as immutable PMT/PMA audit history. What is what: queue KPI cards show only pending value and oldest document age; history KPI cards show paid amount, bank fee, and net cash out, while the compact list summary owns PMT paid/cancelled counts and optional voided-PMA count. Desktop combines bank and account into one destination column with horizontal overflow and a sticky action column; mobile cards expose explicit `ทำจ่าย`/`ยกเลิก` buttons and no card-wide click target; history cards/tables expose a keyboard-accessible `ดูรายละเอียด` button. PMT history can no longer be edited: corrections require cancel/reversal, a new approval, and a new PMT, whose outward document number is issued only by the server. One PMT may combine PMAs only when each PMA recipient still matches its own source and all PMA recipient, normalized payment method, bank, and digit-only account snapshots agree; legacy numeric recipient IDs and `โอนเงิน` are normalized to current supplier/payment-method master values, and only cash may omit bank/account. Why it stays this way: PMT is a posted payment snapshot, so mutating it or accepting a client-selected document number would break bank/accounting audit facts, while comparing each PMA to its source prevents source changes from silently redirecting an approved payment. Fresh validation passed: focused destination tests 14/14, focused ESLint, full workspace lint with 0 errors and one pre-existing `scripts/qa-thai-font.tsx` warning, Prisma generation plus TypeScript type-check, full Vitest 10 files/38 tests, production build with 306 generated pages using `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096` and `CIRCLE_NODE_TOTAL=2`, and scoped `git diff --check`. Browser/DOM UAT was not run because this was an implementation-only request. No database schema or data was changed, and no commit/push was attempted from the dirty divergent `dev` worktree.

Purchase Payments table-design follow-up on 2026-07-14: the `/purchase/payments` payment queue now follows the strict combined filter/table baseline without inventing a false status lifecycle. What is what: the search remains the top-row queue search, while the lower `ประเภทเอกสาร` segmented control filters only the four real PMA source types (`purchase_bill`, `advance_payment`, `expense`, `petty_advance_return`) before pagination; clear and tab switching reset it to `ทุกประเภท`. The desktop table now relies on the shared `Table` shell for its single border and horizontal overflow layer, and `อายุเอกสาร (วัน)` reserves enough width for the full one-line business label while the final sticky action column keeps the approved auto-stretch behavior. Why it stays this way: the queue contains only approved unpaid PMAs, so source type is a truthful operational filter while a generic status filter would misrepresent the workflow; one shared table shell also prevents duplicate borders and competing scroll containers. Fresh validation passed: payment-queue design contract check, focused ESLint, full Vitest 16 files/65 tests, full workspace lint with zero errors and one pre-existing `scripts/qa-thai-font.tsx` warning, Prisma generation plus TypeScript type-check, npm audit with zero vulnerabilities, and a constrained-worker production build generating all 306 pages. Browser/DOM UAT was not run because the user requested implementation rather than testing. No API, payment calculation, permission, database schema/data, commit, or push changed in this follow-up.

PMT LINE checkpoint on 2026-07-13: a successful new payment from `/purchase/payments` now enqueues and executes LINE source `purchase_payment` only after the PMT transaction commits. What is what: outward `PMT` document number is the notification identity; one PMT may aggregate several `payments` rows, PMA allocations, and company-account splits; `/admin/line-settings` exposes `PMT` beside PB/SB so its group can be selected independently. The Flex card shows status/date/branch, payee and approved destination, PMA/source documents, payout accounts, paid/discount/WHT/fee/net amounts, note, and a history link, while masking destination accounts to the final four digits and omitting internal IDs/tax/contact data. Why it stays this way: the payment transaction is the accounting source of truth, LINE is a post-commit side effect, and explicit fail-closed `PMT` routing prevents financial data from falling into a default or unrelated group. A LINE failure never rolls back a valid PMT and remains visible in the shared job/attempt retry history. Dev-target now has an independent `PMT -> ทดสอบ` rule with stop-after-match; real resolver verification returned that group only. Fresh validation passed: PMT-targeted Vitest 11/11, full Vitest 13 files/46 tests, targeted ESLint, full workspace lint with 0 errors and one pre-existing `qa-thai-font.tsx` warning, Prisma generation plus TypeScript type-check, npm audit with 0 vulnerabilities, production build with 306 generated pages using a 4 GB heap and constrained workers, a real read-only PMT aggregate/Flex privacy check, and official LINE push validation HTTP 200 for a capped six-PMA/six-account payload. The real Flex payload was 4,870 bytes, payout-account splits reconciled to net cash out, the full destination account was absent, and the last four digits plus history link were present. Browser/DOM UAT was not run. Live delivery of `PMT012607-0007` to `ทดสอบ` reached `sent`; the screenshot follow-up settled on bubble size `mega`, gave labels a 3:4 share, enabled label wrapping, and the revised card was accepted by LINE.

Sidebar wording checkpoint on 2026-07-13: restored the 49 customer-recognized English/bilingual Sidebar labels from the committed baseline while keeping the newer Thai-first `pageTitle` values inside each page. What is what: `navigationSections.label` and `navigationItems.label` own the compact navigation wording; `pageTitle` owns the working-page title after navigation. Why it stays this way: official module names such as Dashboard & Reports, Tracking 360°, Dual Costing, and Finance / Accounting are stable product vocabulary and should remain familiar in the Sidebar, while Thai-first wording continues inside the operational surfaces. Routes, permissions, menu inventory, icons, and the single-open accordion behavior are unchanged.

Sidebar copy-fit follow-up on 2026-07-13: audited all 127 rendered label slots (section, item, and child labels) against the expanded 256px Sidebar geometry and the active Noto Sans Thai 13.5px font. Removed redundant parenthetical suffixes from the three screenshot-identified entries (`Owner Daily Control`, `Daily Report`, and `Analytics Dashboard`) and from the four additional measured overflows (`ใบสำคัญรับเงิน`, `อนุมัติจ่ายเงิน`, `ข้อมูลย้อนหลัง ม.ค.-เม.ย. 2026`, and `Transaction Ledger`). The complete wording remains in `pageTitle` where context is useful. No route, permission, ordering, icon, or accordion behavior changed.

Sidebar accordion checkpoint on 2026-07-13: per the user's clarification, the desktop/mobile Side menu again keeps only one top-level category expanded at a time. What is what: `expandedSection` stores one category or `null`, the category containing the current route opens automatically, and opening another category closes the previous category while nested child menus remain single-open. Why it stays this way: the navigation should behave as a compact accordion instead of accumulating several long open sections; this decision supersedes the earlier 2026-07-11 dual-default-section checkpoint without changing routes, labels, permissions, or menu inventory.

Local login schema-drift recovery checkpoint on 2026-07-13: password authentication was succeeding, but the post-authentication `POST /api/auth/login-complete` contract could not complete. A full restart first cleared a stale Turbopack route registration (`404`), then exposed the durable failure as Prisma `P2022`: `public.app_users.account_status` was missing from dev-target even though the active Prisma schema and committed lifecycle migration required it. Migration `20260712153000_add_app_user_lifecycle.sql` was applied only to dev-target `fhglqymcdmrgbsbadnwr` and recorded in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`; all 8 lifecycle columns now exist, all 21 current application users were reconciled to `account_status = active`, and there are zero null statuses or `account_status`/`active` mismatches. What is what: Supabase Auth still owns credentials/session, `app_users.last_login_at` records the application login, and `account_status` owns the application-account lifecycle. Why it stays this way: schema drift must be repaired at the database/migration source of truth rather than hidden with a runtime fallback. No application source code changed. Fresh local guard verification logged in on attempt 1 and passed 106 pages plus 106 RSC requests (213 checks total) with zero login fallback, HTTPS-login, SSL, RSC, or route failures.

Stock Convert detail-modal alignment follow-up on 2026-07-12: the `/stock/convert` allocation-detail surface now uses the shared Radix `Dialog`/`DialogContent` contract instead of a custom fixed overlay, centers on desktop, becomes full-screen with one body scroll on mobile, and preserves one dark-header action row with no duplicate close control. Login contract hardening checkpoint on 2026-07-12: the active email/password login now validates the linked active application user through `POST /api/auth/login-complete` before navigation, records the successful login audit/`last_login_at`, and signs out the local Supabase session when the application-user contract fails. Migration `20260712120000_add_current_app_user_access_context.sql` adds `current_app_user_access_context()` as the proxy's single RLS-safe source for active app-user id and forced-password-change state; direct `app_users` reads are not used as a fallback. Proxy account/permission RPC failures now return HTTP 500, while AppShell distinguishes 401 redirect from explicit 403/500/network errors instead of showing a fabricated empty role. What is what: Supabase Auth verifies credentials and owns the session; `app_users` owns active business identity; roles/permissions own application access. Why it stays this way: successful credentials alone must not admit an inactive/unlinked business user, and infrastructure errors must not be misreported as missing roles or ordinary access denial. The additive migration was applied to dev-target `fhglqymcdmrgbsbadnwr` and recorded in migration history. Browser smoke passed invalid-password copy, login completion, `/api/auth/me` role/permission payload, protected landing, logout, and post-logout 401. Type-check, production build, and `git diff --check` passed; lint has only the existing unrelated `qa-thai-font.tsx` warning.

What is what: the duplicate flat column headers `Qty` and `฿/กก.` are renamed to `Qty (ออก)`, `฿/กก. (ออก)`, `Qty (เข้า)`, and `฿/กก. (เข้า)` respectively, and `Loss` is renamed to `Loss (สูญเสีย)` with a right-aligned header (`headerClassName: 'text-right'`); the create action button `+ ปรับเกรดใหม่` is moved to the first filter row and the empty second filter row is completely hidden; columns in all three modes are expanded with wider defaults and min-widths; the double-margin gap under the filter toolbar is resolved by removing page-local `mb-4` margins; and the three pagination bars now include `px-1 py-1` padding and omit explicit `font-normal` font weights to match the reference page height. Why it stays this way: suffixes prevent ambiguity of identical labels; moving the create button and hiding the empty row prevents wasted space; wider columns prevent SKUs/branches truncation; removing `mb-4` avoids double-margin rendering; and pagination padding matches the exact vertical spacing coordinate of the canonical reference. This is client-side layout, presentation, and spacing normalization only; the API, calculations, DB schema, and state remain unchanged. Validation passed with Next.js type-check and a full production build with 306 generated pages.

Weight-ticket action-overlap checkpoint on 2026-07-12: the desktop `/daily/weight-ticket-list` action column now has a 390px default/minimum width, so the valid five-button action set stays inside its own cell instead of overflowing left across `อัปเดตล่าสุด`. Local Dev safe-sync checkpoint on 2026-07-11: fetched `new-origin/dev` at `12482dab` and merged it into local `dev` as `f217727f` while preserving the existing four local commits and all tracked/untracked working changes. What is what: the only Git content conflict was this checkpoint document, whose local dashboard/Owner Daily entries and incoming WTI/WTO/Auth entries were both retained; the incoming login credential-removal contract was combined with the local safe-session auto-redirect; and the local permission backfill migration moved to `20260712000000_grant_weight_ticket_permission_to_admin_owner.sql` because incoming Dev owns version `20260711103000` with `retire_admin_department`. The first full guard exposed a stale local Next dev route state where both system-manual child routes returned 404 despite valid source/manifests/build output; a scoped two-route repro confirmed it, restarting only this repo's Next dev process restored both routes to 200 without code changes, and the final full guard passed.

Sidebar Daily visibility checkpoint on 2026-07-11: the desktop sidebar now keeps both the active page section and `รายการประจำวัน` expanded by default instead of showing only one open section. What is what: `/daily/weight-ticket-list` remains the canonical list for WTI/WTO, and `/daily/weight-ticket-dashboard` is its Daily dashboard; both require `daily.weight_tickets.view`. Why it stays this way: after the permission-model merge, users could access the route but the single-section accordion hid the Daily section while they were on pages such as `/owner-daily`, making “รายการใบรับ-ส่งของ” look removed from the app. Browser QA confirmed the sidebar link `📋รายการใบรับ-ส่งของ` is rendered from `/owner-daily` and clicking it opens `/daily/weight-ticket-list`.

Admin permission backfill checkpoint on 2026-07-11: local `dev` was merged with `new-origin/dev` commit `84f1bce0` to pick up the new data-driven user role and permission management work. What is what: protected routes now use persisted `app_permissions` through `has_app_permission`; the previous admin/owner bypass is gone, so an authenticated admin without a specific catalog grant is correctly treated as unauthorized. Why it stays this way: authorization must stay data-driven, but admin/owner were originally defined as all-access roles and later-added permissions were not automatically backfilled into `app_role_permissions`. Migration `20260712000000_grant_weight_ticket_permission_to_admin_owner.sql` now backfills all active catalog permissions to active `admin` and `owner` role templates idempotently, including `daily.weight_tickets.view`, `production.orders.view`, and `production.reports.view`; its version follows the incoming 2026-07-11 batch because `20260711103000` is owned by `retire_admin_department` on `new-origin/dev`. The dev-target database was patched with this migration SQL and verified: admin/owner each have 33 active permissions. Browser QA confirmed login, `/api/auth/me` 200 with `daily.weight_tickets.view`, and click navigation from `/daily/weight-ticket-dashboard` to `/daily/weight-ticket-list` on `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, and `192.168.1.73`; route guards then passed 106 RSC checks and 213 full page checks with zero login/HTTPS/RSC failures.

Local auth/navigation recovery checkpoint on 2026-07-11: local dev navigation no longer drops authenticated users back to `/login` when opening the app through `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, the Tailscale host, or the current Wi-Fi host `192.168.1.73`. What is what: Next dev treats each host as a separate origin, Supabase auth cookies/storage are origin-scoped, and Next's dev HMR endpoint blocks unlisted origins by default; when the browser was opened through the Wi-Fi IP, hydration/dev resources were blocked, the login form could submit without establishing the app session, and protected Daily routes such as `/daily/weight-ticket-list` correctly fell back to `/login`. Why it stays this way: local QA and customer walk-throughs may use either `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, Tailscale, or the machine Wi-Fi address, so the dev origin allowlist must cover the hosts actually used for testing while production CSP hardening remains production-only. Post-fix checks confirmed login, `/api/auth/me` 200, RSC fetch 200, and visible click navigation from `/daily/weight-ticket-dashboard` to `/daily/weight-ticket-list` on `http://localhost:3000`, `http://127.0.0.1:3000`, and `http://192.168.1.73:3000`; a full authenticated navigation guard on `localhost` covered 106 routes with 213 checks and zero login/HTTPS/RSC target failures, and the Wi-Fi origin guard covered 106 RSC checks with zero failures.

Local 127.0.0.1 auth/navigation checkpoint on 2026-07-11: local Next dev now allows `127.0.0.1` as a dev origin through `allowedDevOrigins`. What is what: `localhost:3000` and `127.0.0.1:3000` are different browser origins with separate auth storage/cookies, and Next dev previously rejected the `127.0.0.1` HMR origin, causing the login page to reload without creating Supabase auth cookies; then protected RSC requests such as `/daily/weight-ticket-list?_rsc=...` correctly returned `307 /login` because the server saw no session. Why it stays this way: users may open either host while testing locally, and both must hydrate/login before protected route navigation can be trusted. Post-fix browser checks confirmed both `http://localhost:3000` and `http://127.0.0.1:3000` can log in, create Supabase auth cookies, return `200` from `/api/auth/me`, return `200 text/x-component` from `/daily/weight-ticket-list?_rsc=...`, and click the visible sidebar link from `/daily/weight-ticket-dashboard` to `/daily/weight-ticket-list` without `/login`, `https://localhost:3000/login`, `ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR`, or `Failed to fetch RSC payload`.

All-navigation HTTPS/RSC sweep checkpoint on 2026-07-11: authenticated browser QA swept all 106 routes extracted from active `navigationItems` on `http://localhost:3000`. What is what: the first sweep loaded each route as a normal browser page after login; the second sweep issued same-session RSC-style fetches (`RSC: 1`) for each route to cover the Next client-navigation path that previously produced `Failed to fetch RSC payload`. Why it stays this way: the original issue was a global local-dev CSP/redirect interaction, so every navigable page must be checked for the same HTTPS-login/RSC failure pattern, not just `/daily/weight-ticket-list`. Both sweeps passed with `targetFailureCount: 0`: no route redirected to `https://localhost:3000/login`, no `ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR`, no `Failed to fetch RSC payload`, no authenticated route fell back to `/login`, and every route returned HTTP/RSC 200. Reports were written under `reports/runtime/route-sweep-2026-07-10T21-28-37-814Z.json` and `reports/runtime/rsc-sweep-2026-07-10T21-35-50-849Z.json`. No additional application code, permissions, formulas, database schema, or DB state changed in this sweep.

Local HTTPS redirect checkpoint on 2026-07-11: local Next dev no longer sends the CSP `upgrade-insecure-requests` directive outside production. What is what: `http://localhost:3000` is the local development origin, `/login` is the public auth entry, and `/daily/weight-ticket-list` is a protected Daily route that redirects anonymous users to login or loads for authenticated users with `daily.weight_tickets.view`. Why it stays this way: the production CSP hardening should remain enabled, but local HTTP development must not instruct Chrome to upgrade same-origin login/navigation requests to `https://localhost:3000`, because the dev server does not serve TLS and that creates `ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR` plus Next RSC fallback noise during sidebar navigation. Post-fix checks confirmed the dev CSP omits the directive, anonymous protected-route redirect stays on HTTP, authenticated navigation to `/daily/weight-ticket-list` stays on `http://localhost:3000/daily/weight-ticket-list`, and no `https://localhost:3000/login` or RSC payload failure was seen.

Local website smoke test checkpoint on 2026-07-11: the active Next app was restarted locally at `http://localhost:3000` after a stale/hung Next dev process on port 3000 was stopped. What is what: `/api/health` is the bootstrap health probe; `/login` is the public auth entry; `/api/auth/me` without a session correctly returns `401`; protected pages redirect anonymous users; authenticated smoke coverage loaded `/owner-daily`, `/dashboard`, `/daily/weight-ticket-list`, `/purchase/bills`, `/sales/bills`, `/stock/balance`, `/finance/ar`, and `/finance-accounting/cash-flow-analysis` with HTTP 200, plus a mobile `/dashboard` check. Why it stays this way: local QA must use `http://localhost:3000`, because `http://127.0.0.1:3000` triggers Next dev cross-origin HMR blocking and can create false browser errors/hydration false negatives. Runtime smoke did not show blocking browser console errors or 4xx/5xx API responses on the authenticated route set, but workspace readiness is not normal because `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false` and `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next` both fail on stale `app_users.username` assumptions after the schema/client removed that field. `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next` passed with one existing warning in `scripts/qa-thai-font.tsx`. This was QA/reporting only; no application code, formulas, permissions, database schema, or DB state was changed.

Cash Flow Analysis redesign checkpoint on 2026-07-11: `/finance-accounting/cash-flow-analysis` now follows the active neutral ERP design baseline across desktop/mobile and light/dark themes. The page uses shared `KpiCard` cards, a compact desktop filter row, a mobile summary strip plus shared `MobileFilterSheet`, a diverging profit-versus-operating-cash chart, cash/AR/stock working-capital comparison, responsive current/7-day/30-day cash projection, OD/liquidity risk, Thai-first insight panels, and the standard detail table surface. Mobile KPI text can wrap without clipping, the two mobile date fields stay on one row, the apply action uses `ใช้ตัวกรอง`, and the mobile projection uses its own aspect-correct SVG geometry instead of stretching the desktop chart. What is what: the KPI row shows the current filtered decision signals; profit versus cash explains accrual-to-cash conversion; cash/AR/stock shows where working capital is held; projection and OD panels show near-term liquidity risk; insights explain notable conditions; and the detail table preserves exact source metrics. Why it stays this way: the page must support quick liquidity decisions without repeated cards, distorted charts, mixed terminology, stale figures under new filter labels, or hidden mobile text. Successful data is now associated with the exact request URL, displayed periods come from the successful response filters, refetch/error states hide stale figures, and visible Thai table labels sort by their rendered wording. This is presentation and client-state correctness only; `GET /api/finance-accounting/cash-flow-analysis`, server calculations, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed, and `/finance-accounting/cf-forecast-calendar`, `/sales-plan`, and advance-payment/refund flows were not changed. Validation passed: read-only fixture browser QA for Desktop/Mobile × Light/Dark, mobile Filter Sheet and bottom-nav clearance, KPI clipping, projection aspect ratio, loading/refetch/error/response-period states, zero console/page/failed-network errors in normal cases, workspace lint with one existing unrelated warning, type-check, production build with 302 routes, and scoped `git diff --check`; the temporary local QA public-path allowance was removed before closeout.

Local API route-registry recovery on 2026-07-10: the running Next dev server temporarily returned HTML `404` for both `GET /api/auth/me` and `GET /api/dual-costing/compare-margin` even though both tracked route handlers still existed, both routes were present in the generated app-path manifests, and the retired root `[...slug]` catch-all remained deleted. This isolated the incident to stale/transient local Next dev route state rather than the Compare Margin query, Supabase data, permissions, or either API implementation. The local dev server was restarted without changing application code. Three consecutive post-restart probes returned `200` for `/api/health` and the expected unauthenticated `401` JSON for both protected routes, proving they resolve as API handlers instead of `404`; the subsequent existing-session page load returned `200` for `/dual-costing/compare-margin`, `/api/auth/me`, and `/api/dual-costing/compare-margin`. What is what: `401` without a session is the correct protected-route response; `200` with the existing authenticated session proves the business page can load; `404` for these existing handlers is a broken local route registry. Why it stays this way: runtime recovery must not be disguised as a business-query fallback or compatibility branch. No API contract, formula, permission, database schema, or DB state was changed.


Dashboard Overview KPI delta checkpoint on 2026-07-10: `/dashboard` KPI cards now support stock-style delta badges under/next to the main value. The active six dashboard KPIs receive real comparison values from `GET /api/dashboard`: Revenue, Expenses, Net Profit, Cash Balance, AR, and AP are compared with the previous equivalent date range, then rendered through the shared `KpiCard` `delta` prop as a small up/down/flat icon plus percent. What is what: the large number remains the current filtered KPI, and the small delta badge shows change versus the comparable previous period. Why it stays this way: the user wants a stock-like change signal without adding fake data, sparklines, or duplicated KPI cards; color follows business meaning, so Expenses/AP rising is negative while Revenue/Cash/Net Profit rising is positive.

Owner Daily compact control checkpoint on 2026-07-10: `/owner-daily` now follows the current owner-control design baseline. The page starts with a neutral date filter row, then a compact cash-plan panel showing available cash, expected in, expected out, and projected gap without duplicating those same values as separate KPI cards. Cash-plan sub values are inline stat columns, not nested cards. Actual activity and pending work now render as heading + one-layer KPI/stat grids instead of card-in-card sections, and the detail-heavy due AR, due AP, loan, expense, and pending surfaces sit behind shared line tabs so the page no longer stacks a wall of tables or squeezes AR/AP side by side. Table headers are Thai-first (`ครบกำหนด` instead of `Due`), decorative emoji/giant success icons and disabled placeholder actions were removed from this flow, and loan rows now show installment plus due date in the detail column. What is what: cash plan is the morning liquidity forecast, actual activity is today's posted movement, and line tabs choose the active follow-up surface. Why it stays this way: owner review should scan the cash decision first, then choose the exception list to act on, without repeated KPI values, nested cards, or cramped tables. This is UI presentation only; `GET /api/owner-daily`, formulas, source tables, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.
WTI/WTO numeric and relational hardening checkpoint on 2026-07-11: migration `20260711190000_harden_weight_ticket_numeric_and_relations.sql` standardizes WTI/WTO weight and derived allocation quantities as `numeric(18,2)` while deliberately preserving unrestricted precision for unit-cost and value snapshots. The API schema rejects weight inputs beyond two decimal places, calculation boundaries round line/header/summary weights consistently, type-specific lifecycle and supplier/customer party-shape checks are enforced, self-referencing parent/impurity line numbers now use deferred composite foreign keys, and nonnegative/positive quantity checks protect persisted facts. View `weight_ticket_reconciliation_issues` reports header-to-summary weight drift and summary-to-line bridge-count drift. A rollback validation against Dev normalized the single historical floating artifact (`WTI012606-0005`) and returned zero reconciliation issues. What is what: weights and operational quantities are two-decimal business measurements; unit costs and monetary snapshots remain higher-precision accounting facts. Why it stays this way: it removes JavaScript floating artifacts without sacrificing weighted-average cost accuracy or allowing document aggregates and line relationships to drift silently.

WTO confirmation-owned stock reservation checkpoint on 2026-07-11: saving or editing a `draft` WTO now writes only the document, lines, summaries, and audit status; it neither validates current stock availability nor creates `stock_holds`. The first confirm action validates stock using the latest ledger/active-hold state, creates active pending-out holds, snapshots cost, and changes the document to `delivered` in one database transaction. If stock is no longer sufficient, confirmation fails without changing the document or reserving a partial quantity. Migration `20260711173000_move_wto_stock_hold_to_confirmation.sql` releases legacy active holds attached to draft WTO documents without deleting their history. What is what: draft is a correctable outbound plan, while confirmation is the business commitment that reserves stock. Why it stays this way: an unconfirmed document must not reduce available stock or block another confirmed shipment.

WTI/WTO no-fallback hardening checkpoint on 2026-07-11: weight-ticket creation and mapping no longer invent a `00` branch document code, `draft` status, `none` deduction mode, or `-` weighing-staff name when source data is missing. Branch document codes must be exactly two digits, the authenticated employee must have a display name, and persisted status/deduction values are read directly from their database contracts. Migration `20260711170000_require_weight_ticket_entered_by.sql` makes `weight_tickets.entered_by` required and rejects blank/`-` values. What is what: these fields identify the document sequence, lifecycle, deduction calculation, and responsible employee. Why it stays this way: substituting plausible defaults would conceal invalid master or transaction data and could produce misleading operational documents.

WTI draft lifecycle checkpoint on 2026-07-11: newly saved inbound weight tickets now persist as `draft`, matching the existing WTO review-before-confirm pattern. A draft WTI cannot be selected for a Purchase Bill; the list action `ยืนยันรับของ` changes it to `received`, appends the normal confirmation timeline/audit event, and then makes it eligible for Purchase Bill use. Editing a confirmed ticket preserves its current lifecycle status instead of deriving a new initial status. Existing WTI records remain unchanged. What is what: save records the field document, confirmation accepts the inbound receipt, and Purchase Bill remains the stock-in owner. Why it stays this way: receiving staff need a correctable saved draft before the document becomes an accounting source, without moving stock at WTI confirmation or altering WTO cost-snapshot behavior.

New UAT Supabase clone checkpoint on 2026-07-11: the active Vercel UAT project at `https://ns-erp-uat.vercel.app` now points its Production Supabase and database environment to project `ekeomeumqjvbhgwyaqwe`. The empty UAT database was backed up locally, then received the current dev-target `public` schema/data, `auth.users`, `auth.identities`, and Supabase migration history. Exact reconciliation found no per-table row-count differences: dev and UAT both have 140 public tables and 32,737 public rows, 44 Auth users, 43 Auth identities, 117 public RLS policies, 90 RLS-enabled tables, 15 public routines, and 106 migration-history rows. What is what: dev remains the implementation database, while the new project is the isolated customer UAT database and Auth boundary. Why it stays this way: UAT must reproduce approved dev behavior without sharing the live dev database. This clone does not include Supabase Storage buckets or binary objects; those remain a separate migration/verification batch before PDF/image-dependent UAT flows are accepted.

WTI/WTO UAT schema checkpoint on 2026-07-11: created scoped backup `/tmp/ns-erp-uat-wti-wto-pre-migrate-20260711-175615.dump`, then applied migrations `20260711173000`, `20260711190000`, and `20260711200000` to UAT project `ekeomeumqjvbhgwyaqwe` in one transaction. Migration history contains all three versions. Five legacy active holds on draft WTO documents were released; post-checks show zero draft active holds and zero rows in `weight_ticket_reconciliation_issues`. Weight precision is `numeric(18,2)`. The header Warehouse FK introduced here was later identified as the wrong business dimension and is removed by corrective migration `20260711213000`.

WTI/WTO Dev/UAT release closeout on 2026-07-11: applied the same three WTI/WTO migrations to dev-target `fhglqymcdmrgbsbadnwr` after backup `/tmp/ns-erp-dev-wti-wto-pre-migrate-20260711-110248.dump`; Dev reports all three migration versions, zero draft active holds, and zero reconciliation issues. Provisioned UAT Storage bucket `weight-ticket-pdfs` with the documented public 10 MB PDF/JPEG/PNG/WebP contract and passed upload/read/delete checks. Promoted commit `e0e70892` from `new-origin/uat` to the separate UAT production repository `uat-origin/main`; GitHub Vercel status completed successfully and `https://ns-erp-uat.vercel.app/` plus `/api/health` returned HTTP 200. The local UAT Vercel token is invalid, but GitHub automatic deployment remains operational. The temporary WTI Warehouse mapping issue view is removed by corrective migration `20260711213000` because WTI header Godown is not a stock Warehouse.

WTI/WTO Godown/Warehouse correction on 2026-07-11: the user clarified that `โกดัง` and stock `คลัง` are separate business dimensions. Both WTI and WTO require a physical Godown description at the document header, while WTO additionally requires the actual stock `warehouse_id` on each product line. Corrective migration `20260711213000_remove_wti_header_warehouse_reference.sql` removed the incorrectly introduced header Warehouse FK. Migration `20260711223000_require_weight_ticket_godown.sql` renames the legacy header column from `warehouse_name` to required `godown_name` and preserves `weight_ticket_lines.warehouse_id` for WTO stock relations and holds. Follow-up migration `20260711230000_rename_line_template_godown_key.sql` changes persisted LINE template fields from `warehouseName` to `godownName`; the LINE renderer now reads the header property directly without a Warehouse-to-Godown mapping. What is what: Godown is the physical operating place recorded on either weight-ticket header; Warehouse is the stock accounting bucket selected per WTO line. Why it has to be like this: using Warehouse naming, relations, or template keys for Godown conflates a free operational location with the RM/FG stock dimension and makes the notification contract misleading.

WTI/WTO LINE manual-share correction on 2026-07-11: the notify endpoint now applies branch scope through `branches.code`, matching the list/detail contract. An empty scope means access to every branch and therefore adds no branch predicate; a non-empty scope filters by branch business code. Previously the endpoint always emitted `branch_id IN []` for all-branch users and also treated branch codes as numeric database IDs, causing valid WTI/WTO documents to return not found when sharing to the primary LINE group. Why it has to be like this: authorization scope is expressed as branch codes throughout the weight-ticket flow and must not be reinterpreted as internal IDs.

Login credential/fallback removal checkpoint on 2026-07-11: removed development/test email and password prefill from `/login`, removed the real-account email placeholder, and stopped reading `NEXT_PUBLIC_TEST_LOGIN_*` / `DEV_LOGIN_*` credentials at runtime. Server authorization now accepts only the direct Supabase Auth ID link in `app_users.auth_user_id`; it no longer falls back to email matching, legacy `user_profiles.role`, or Auth metadata roles. Active-user audit found 10 Dev/UAT `app_users` rows without Auth users; those rows could not log in previously because no matching `auth.users` account exists and remain pending invite/account creation. What is what: Supabase `auth.users` authenticates credentials, while `app_users` owns ERP status, roles, branches, and permissions. Why it has to be like this: credential prefill can expose secrets to browsers, and role fallback can grant access outside the configured permission source of truth.

Email-only user identity checkpoint on 2026-07-10: the active app now has one account identifier, email. Migration `20260710133000_remove_app_user_username.sql` was applied to dev-target and removes `app_users.username`, its case-insensitive unique index, and the retired `lookup_app_login_email(text)` function. `/admin/users` no longer collects, lists, sorts, or searches username; create/update validation, login, and self-service reset now require email only. What is what: `app_users.email` identifies the login account, while display name identifies the person in the UI. Why it stays this way: a separate username duplicated identity data and was not used by the intended sign-in flow. Historical audit/activity snapshot columns named `actor_username` remain for traceability; new activity writes the actor email to those compatibility columns. Permissions, branch scope, user roles, Supabase Auth user IDs, and business transaction behavior are unchanged.

Derived user display-name follow-up on 2026-07-10: `/admin/users` no longer asks an administrator to enter a separate user/display name. The API derives `app_users.display_name` from name prefix, first name, and last name on every create/update. Existing records with incomplete structured names remain editable; their display name falls back to email until their profile name is completed. What is what: email identifies the login account; prefix/first/last name identify the person; display name is the system presentation value derived from those fields. Why it stays this way: manual display-name entry duplicates the person identity fields and can drift from them. Role, branch, department, permissions, and Supabase Auth behavior are unchanged.

User prefix dropdown follow-up on 2026-07-10: `/admin/users` now uses a fixed name-prefix dropdown with `นาย`, `นาง`, `นางสาว`, and `คุณ` (plus no prefix). The API enforces the same set. What is what: prefix is a bounded part of the employee profile and participates in the derived display name. Why it stays this way: a fixed option set prevents spelling variants from creating inconsistent employee names. Existing dev-target values already match the allowed set.

Admin Users department/role separation checkpoint on 2026-07-10: `/admin/users` now separates organizational ฝ่าย from permission role. The existing `departments` master is linked to users through `app_users.department_id`, with migration `20260710112000_add_app_user_department.sql` applied to dev-target and 15 current users backfilled from clear legacy role mappings. The admin users API now returns `departments` and each user's `departmentId/department`, requires an active department selection on every create/update, and stores department assignment on `app_users` only. The Users page now supports Role, ฝ่าย, สาขา, and สถานะ filters, shows ฝ่าย as its own table/mobile-card field, and has a required ฝ่าย selector with no “ไม่ระบุฝ่าย” choice. What is what: ฝ่าย is the employee's organization unit; Role remains the permission bundle; Branch remains data-scope access. Why it stays this way: using Role for organization units makes permission roles multiply and hides the difference between job affiliation and system authorization. Runtime role permission grants, branch access writes, Supabase Auth invite/reset behavior, and business transaction flows were not changed.

Terminology follow-up on 2026-07-10: active user-facing wording for the existing `departments` master is now `ฝ่าย`. `/master-data/departments`, its navigation label, validation/API errors, and the fixed-asset form/filter use this vocabulary consistently. What is what: `departments` and `department_id` remain technical names and still represent the employee or asset's organization unit. Why it stays this way: changing only the user-facing term clarifies the organization model without a risky schema/API rename or any effect on assigned users, permissions, or transaction data.

Permission model checkpoint on 2026-07-11: the user-management surface now treats `app_roles` as user-defined work-function templates, not fixed job titles. Migration `20260711090000_add_direct_user_permission_overrides.sql` adds `app_user_permission_overrides` with `allow`/`deny` effects and updates the Supabase permission helper to calculate effective catalog permissions as active role-template grants plus direct allows minus direct denies. `/admin/users` now returns the active permission catalog, template permissions, and user overrides; its user form lets an administrator set page/action permission overrides. `/admin/roles` and `/admin/roles/[id]` create and update work-function templates and their permission sets. The old role-code bypass and legacy `user_profiles` fallback have been removed from proxy, server auth context, and login landing routing. What is what: department remains the required organization unit, work functions are reusable permission templates, and direct overrides are an explicit exception for one user. Why it stays this way: access is determined only by persisted permissions and can therefore differ page-by-page between people in the same department. Existing catalog permissions remain the current enforcement boundary; more granular catalog/API entries are a separate controlled expansion rather than an unsafe free-text permission feature.

Department-role access design decision on 2026-07-11: the approved department master has four departments: `DEP-001 บริหาร`, `DEP-002 บัญชีและการเงิน`, `DEP-003 ประสานงาน`, and `DEP-004 ผลิต`. `ผู้ดูแลระบบ` is a Role, not a department. The approved category baseline is: บริหาร sees all categories; ผลิต sees ผลิต/Stock/ส่งของ; บัญชีและการเงิน sees ประจำวัน/Stock/Dual Costing/Tracking 360; and ประสานงาน sees Stock/ประจำวัน/Dual Costing/วางแผนการขาย. The standard role vocabulary is `เจ้าหน้าที่`, `หัวหน้างาน`, `ผู้บริหาร`, and `ผู้ดูแลระบบ`; these are job/authority levels, not department names. A person may work in more than one operating scope, so the target authorization model is one primary department on the employee profile plus multiple department-role assignments. Each department-role receives page/action grants within its allowed categories, and the user's effective access is the union across active assignments plus direct allows minus direct denies. Side-menu categories appear only when at least one page inside is permitted. The deployed schema still uses `app_users.department_id` and global `app_user_roles`, so a dedicated access-preserving migration is required before multi-department assignments become runtime enforcement.

Latest employee-directory and department checkpoint on 2026-07-11: migration `20260711123000_sync_latest_employee_directory.sql` replaces that earlier organization baseline with four active departments: `DEP-001 บริหาร`, `DEP-002 บัญชีและการเงิน`, `DEP-003 ประสานงาน`, and `DEP-004 ผลิต`. `DEP-005 คัดแยก` is merged into production and deleted. The approved Side menu category scope is: บริหาร sees all categories; บัญชีและการเงิน sees ประจำวัน/Stock/Dual Costing/Tracking 360; ประสานงาน sees Stock/ประจำวัน/Dual Costing/วางแผนการขาย; and ผลิต sees ผลิต/Stock/ส่งของ. The migration synchronizes 21 active employee records, including `peach@admin.com` as `ผู้ดูแลระบบ`, adds `guntapit@metalcom.co.th`, removes `ns-kwan@nsscrap.com` and `ns-or@nsscrap.com`, updates the submitted names, assigns one standard job function per employee, and keeps all listed employees unrestricted across branches. Department category scope is a design boundary; runtime access continues to come only from persisted role/page permissions.

Employee directory checkpoint on 2026-07-11: migration `20260711110000_sync_employee_directory.sql` synchronizes the user-provided employee directory to `app_users`: structured first/last name without a prefix, primary department, and all-branch scope (no `app_user_branch_access` rows). The batch updates existing records by case-insensitive email and creates `photsathon.spd1@gmail.com` as an app-user directory row without an Auth account, ready for the normal invite flow. It intentionally does not change existing roles, role permissions, direct overrides, or Auth identities; those require the next department-role permission batch.

Employee role normalization checkpoint on 2026-07-11: migration `20260711113000_normalize_employee_roles.sql` resets the submitted employee directory's `หน้าที่งาน` to the standard roles shown in `/admin/users`: 16 users are `เจ้าหน้าที่` and 4 users are `ผู้ดูแลระบบ`. The migration deletes those users' legacy role assignments and direct permission overrides. `เจ้าหน้าที่` begins with no permission grant; `ผู้ดูแลระบบ` receives every active catalog permission through persisted `app_role_permissions`. No legacy role, direct override, code fallback, or implicit role-code bypass remains for the submitted employees. The next batch configures page/action access for each `ฝ่าย + Role` profile.

Employee work-function selector follow-up on 2026-07-11: `/admin/users` now records exactly one `หน้าที่งาน` through a required dropdown, rather than allowing multiple checkbox selections. Migration `20260711120000_add_employee_role_marker.sql` adds the persisted `app_roles.is_employee_role` marker and makes only `เจ้าหน้าที่`, `หัวหน้างาน`, `ผู้บริหาร`, and `ผู้ดูแลระบบ` selectable in the employee directory; any other `app_roles` row remains a reusable permission template and is not a job choice. The create/update APIs enforce one active marked role. What is what: `หน้าที่งาน` is the employee's one current job/authority level, while role templates outside this marker remain configuration artifacts. Why it stays this way: a staff directory needs one clear job label, and selecting several arbitrary permission templates makes the label and approval chain ambiguous.

Admin Users filter checkpoint on 2026-07-10: `/admin/users` now has client-side filters for Role, branch, and user status in the existing filter card. What is what: search still scans user identity/contact/role/branch text, Role filters by assigned app role, branch filters by branch access while treating users with no branch restriction as all-branch users, and status filters active vs closed users. Why it stays this way: the API already returns users, roles, and branches for this management surface, so filtering can stay local without changing the permission-protected read contract or adding database query behavior for a small admin list. Runtime user create/edit/status APIs, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Calendar dark-mode color follow-up on 2026-07-10: the shared calendar popover now uses slate selection, range, and focus colors instead of bright blue in dark mode. What is what: this is the common date-picker calendar surface used across filters/forms, so selected days, range highlights, and keyboard focus rings now match the rest of the dark ERP shell. Why it stays this way: the blue state styling looked out of place against the system's dark neutral UI and made date-picker overlays read like an unrelated theme. This is shared UI styling only; date values, filtering behavior, APIs, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

App shell bar alignment follow-up on 2026-07-10: the shared `AppShell` now uses the same 72px header height for the left brand/sidebar bar and the main topbar so the dark shell and page header align cleanly on desktop instead of looking like mismatched bars. The breadcrumb strip no longer draws its own bottom border, so the shell keeps one clear divider at the topbar only. What is what: the sidebar brand bar identifies the app, the topbar carries the current page title/actions, and the breadcrumb is a secondary navigation aid rather than a second framed header. Why it stays this way: ERP shell chrome should look structurally even and should not introduce extra horizontal rules that make the top area feel split into too many layers. This is UI-shell layout only; routes, auth, page data, APIs, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Dependency security cleanup on 2026-07-10: effective dependency overrides now live in the repository root `package.json`, because npm workspace installs only enforce root-level overrides. Prisma's transitive development helper is overridden from `@prisma/dev@0.24.3` to patched `0.24.14`, which removes Hono and the vulnerable `@hono/node-server@1.19.11` chain, while PostCSS resolves to the patched root version. The stale `apps/next/package-lock.json` was removed so the root workspace `package-lock.json` is the single npm lockfile; that nested lock still referenced the already-replaced vulnerable `xlsx@0.18.5` and an old `hono@4.12.19`, causing obsolete High findings. `read-excel-file` is pinned to `9.2.0` because the newer `9.3.0` package imports `saxen` at runtime but publishes it only as a development dependency, which breaks a clean Turbopack build. Hono is not imported by the active app and no longer appears in the resolved dependency tree. This checkpoint changes dependency resolution and security scan accuracy only; application behavior, APIs, database schema, and business flows are unchanged.

Security report follow-up on 2026-07-10: report `20260710-085002-14d36a` was against commit `1538614a` and still found stale dependency data from the tracked `apps/next/pnpm-lock.yaml`; that unused PNPM lockfile has now been removed so the npm workspace root `package-lock.json` is the only lock source. The tracked false-positive secret findings were also reduced: LINE webhook test data now uses a clearly fake reply token, Tax WHT table resize keys use shorter non-secret identifiers, and the legacy UI audit HTML has its old Supabase publishable key redacted. A root `.dockerignore` now excludes local env files, dependency folders, and build output from Docker/scan contexts. The remaining `.env.local` findings in that report came from `/repo/.env.local`, which is not tracked by git; the scan runner must scan a clean checkout or explicitly exclude local `.env*` files so machine-local secrets are not copied into the scanned image. This is security hygiene and scan accuracy only; runtime business behavior is unchanged.

Sales Commission table filter/pagination checkpoint on 2026-07-10: `/sales-commission` now keeps pagination/filter behavior aligned with the active table design. The overview Sales summary table and each selected-sales drilldown tab use the same pagination row (`พบทั้งหมด`, page size, `ก่อนหน้า`, `หน้า X / Y`, `ถัดไป`), reset-column-width actions live in the toolbar area, and the summary export button uses the baseline `ส่งออก Excel` treatment. Drilldown filters are tab-specific: category tabs search category names, Supplier tab searches supplier names, and item detail searches bill/supplier/product plus segmented commission status. The table surface order is intentionally toolbar/filter first, pagination second, table third. Selecting a salesperson also resets the viewport to the top of the drilldown on mobile so the selected-sales context and back/export actions are visible before KPI/table content. What is what: line tabs choose the verification table, the tab toolbar filters only the active table, and pagination controls the active rows. Why it stays this way: table controls must stay attached to the data surface they affect and must not create duplicated counts, floating reset buttons, irrelevant filters, or mobile detail screens that start mid-content.

Sales Commission drilldown back-action checkpoint on 2026-07-10: `/sales-commission` selected-sales drilldown now places `กลับหน้าหลัก` as a standalone top-left navigation action above the selected-sales header card instead of grouping it with the `ส่งออก Excel` report action. The selected-sales header card now carries the salesperson context and export action only. What is what: back exits the detail view, export acts on the report data. Why it stays this way: navigation and report actions should not look like peer actions inside the same card when they have different UX meaning. This is UI placement only; runtime API behavior, commission formulas, filters, export data, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.

Shared KPI marker cleanup on 2026-07-10: `apps/next/src/components/ui/KpiCard.tsx` no longer renders a default `●` marker inside a left circular icon shell when a page does not pass a real icon. Existing meaningful icons passed by page code still render. What is what: KPI color now comes from label/value tone unless a meaningful icon is explicitly provided. Why it stays this way: decorative dot markers looked like unexplained status indicators and created the vertical artifact the user flagged on `/sales-commission`. This is design-system presentation only; KPI values, formulas, API behavior, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.

Sales Commission drilldown visual alignment on 2026-07-10: `/sales-commission` selected-sales drilldown was tightened after rendered review. The detail KPI area now has 8 balanced cards in one 4+4 desktop grid, including `ค่าคอมเดือนนี้`; the back action uses the compact `rounded-md h-9` button baseline with an icon; the four verification table panels now use neutral white headers instead of dark/emoji `Table` headers; and visible Supplier labels in the drilldown tables/search were normalized to Thai `ผู้ขาย`. What is what: the drilldown remains the same read-only verification surface with 4 tables and the same export/read model. Why it stays this way: the data was correct, but the detail presentation needed to match the current table/card/button/Thai-first design rules.

Sales Commission KPI completion on 2026-07-10: `/sales-commission` overview KPI cards now show all 8 summary metrics requested in the report flow instead of only 4 compact signals. The cards are `จำนวนที่ซื้อ`, `ยอดซื้อ`, `จำนวนที่ได้คอม`, `ยอดซื้อที่ได้คอม`, `จำนวนที่ไม่ได้คอม`, `ยอดซื้อที่ไม่ได้คอม`, `จำนวนซื้อทั้งปี`, and `ยอดซื้อทั้งปี`, with desktop layout capped at 4 cards per row so the second set lands on the next row. What is what: these cards are page-level report totals for the current filter scope and annual comparison. Why it stays this way: the user wants the full 8-card report summary visible while preserving the newer salesperson report-card grid and existing drilldown behavior.

Sales Commission salesperson card update on 2026-07-10: `/sales-commission` overview salesperson cards now follow the latest user-provided report-card reference. Each card shows the salesperson header/status, bill count, Supplier count, total purchase weight, commissionable weight, total purchase amount, commissionable purchase amount, and current commission in grouped white metric boxes; desktop uses up to 4 cards per row while tablet/mobile reduce columns to keep values readable. What is what: the cards are a compact per-salesperson scan surface before the Sales Summary table, and clicking the card still opens the existing full drilldown view.
