Why it stays this way: the page needs richer per-salesperson decision data without turning the overview into multiple separate KPI grids or changing the `/api/sales-commission` read model.

Sales Commission compact overview on 2026-07-10: `/sales-commission` overview was adjusted toward the user's provided compact dashboard reference. The global date/branch filters now stay in one light filter row with a reset action; the top summary was reduced from 8 narrow KPI cards to 4 primary decision cards; salesperson cards now show compact per-person purchase/commission signals with an icon and drilldown affordance; and the sales summary table now sits in a white report panel with a `ดาวน์โหลดรายงาน` action instead of a heavy dark header. What is what: the overview shows page scope, primary totals, per-salesperson decision cards, and then the category summary table; the existing salesperson drilldown keeps the detailed verification tables. Why it stays this way: the page should read like a dashboard first, not a wall of repeated KPI/table metrics, while keeping the same `/api/sales-commission` read model and drilldown behavior.

Dashboard UI demo removal on 2026-07-10: `/dashboarduidemo` was removed from the active Next app. The standalone demo route files, sidebar navigation entry, permission mapping, and page-scoped global CSS override were deleted so the demo no longer appears in the product shell or builds as a routable page. What is what: `/dashboard` remains the active dashboard overview and `/api/dashboard` remains unchanged. Why it stays this way: the user asked to remove the UI demo page entirely rather than keep a separate free-design concept route.

Dashboard UI demo redesign on 2026-07-09: `/dashboarduidemo` was previously treated as the single page allowed to break away from the current shared `docs/design.md` visual language. This checkpoint is now superseded by the 2026-07-10 removal checkpoint above; keep it as historical context only.

Duplicate design hardening on 2026-07-09: active Next UI surfaces were tightened so there is one implementation pattern for the design areas the user flagged. Data/table surface switchers now use shared `TabsList variant="line"` / `TabsTrigger variant="line"` instead of page-local pill/card/tab buttons; real filter/status/quick-range controls use the single compact slate segmented baseline; card-like headers/details/forms use `rounded-xl border ... shadow-sm`; table shells use `rounded-md border ... shadow-sm`; form controls and textareas use compact `rounded-md`. Detail routes for purchase approvals, supplier advances, and daily expenses were included, not only sidebar list pages. Intentional exceptions remain for shared line-tab internals, modal/dropdown/popover shells, floating action buttons, true colored action buttons, and the LINE phone preview mock. This is UI presentation only; component state, filters, sorting, formulas, API payloads, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed. Second-pass audit also removed remaining runtime `rounded-lg` usage from active TSX surfaces (excluding design mockups) and normalized the last clear `rounded-xl` form-control/table-shell drifts. The remaining intentional scan exceptions are modal/dropdown/popover shadows, shared line-tab internals, a LINE phone preview mock shell, real colored action buttons, and clickable row cards that visually function as cards rather than filter/action buttons.

Card radius standardization on 2026-07-09: active card-like UI surfaces now use a single `rounded-xl` corner radius. This covers KPI/Summary cards, filter cards, form/card panels, dense mobile row cards, grouped detail cards, ranked/list panels, empty/loading cards, auth cards, and generic content panels. Intentional non-card controls keep their existing radius: table shells, modals, inputs, selects, buttons, badges, dropdowns, popovers, and read-only field boxes remain on their documented compact radius. `docs/design.md` now records the distinction so future UI work does not create `rounded-md`/`rounded-lg` card variants again. This is UI radius/style only; component state, data, formulas, APIs, query params, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Stock status-convert table-header follow-up on 2026-07-09: runtime table headers now stay on one line instead of wrapping to two lines. The shared `ResizableTableHead` now uses nowrap labels, global table header CSS prevents header wrapping, and `/stock/status-convert` reserves more default width for `จำนวน (กก.)`, `ต้นทุน (บาท/กก.)`, and `วันที่สร้างรายการ`; the saved width key was bumped to `stock.operation.status-convert.v7` so old narrow localStorage widths do not keep forcing the same visual issue. `docs/design.md` now records that table headers must stay on one line, using column width and horizontal overflow instead of wrapping. Rendered sidebar verification was rerun in 6 batches covering all 106 sidebar routes on desktop and mobile; the new `table-header-multi-line-risk` audit returned 0 signals across the batch reports `sidebar-full-audit-2026-07-08T18-10-30-836Z`, `18-13-41-742Z`, `18-15-47-188Z`, `18-17-34-975Z`, `18-19-22-385Z`, and `18-21-14-042Z`. What is what: the columns still sort/resize by the same data keys and body values are unchanged; only header presentation/default widths changed. Why it stays this way: dense ERP tables should keep header labels readable without two-line headers or shortened business wording. Runtime APIs, formulas, query params, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Sidebar audit noise/exception cleanup on 2026-07-08: the remaining signals from the full sidebar design audit were handled without changing business behavior. `/analytics-dashboard` share/print actions now use the lighter `shadow-sm` action depth, `/finance/bank` chart/table shells were normalized away from heavy/gradient surfaces, and `/finance-accounting/asset-register` keeps the real CSV template download contract while changing the visible label to `ดาวน์โหลดแบบฟอร์มนำเข้า` / `แบบฟอร์มนำเข้า` so it is not mistaken for spreadsheet export drift. The sidebar audit runner now waits a little longer by default, ignores Next dev font aborts, and ignores same-origin `net::ERR_ABORTED` request cancellations caused by route changes, because those are navigation noise rather than rendered-page design failures. Final verification reran all 106 sidebar routes on desktop and mobile (212 screenshots) with 0 issue signals in `reports/playwright/sidebar-full-audit-2026-07-08T15-10-38-659Z`. What is what: template download is an import helper, export actions are real spreadsheet outputs, and aborted same-origin requests during route navigation are audit-tool noise. Why it stays this way: the design audit should flag visible UI drift, not punish valid import-template wording or normal browser cancellation while moving between pages. Runtime APIs, formulas, query params, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Full sidebar visual audit follow-up on 2026-07-08: the active sidebar inventory was opened again through the authenticated Playwright runner, covering all 106 sidebar routes on desktop and mobile (212 rendered page screenshots). The main response status was 200 for every route and there were no login redirects. Remaining automated signals were reviewed: transient dev-font aborts, chart/background gradient surfaces, the real asset-register CSV template contract, and a loading-state `/reports` export button were not treated as active filter/action design drift; a targeted `/reports` rerun with longer settle time returned 0 issues. Additional UI-only fixes landed for the visible AP/AR card layout: `/finance/ar` now separates the single AR KPI from the aging/top-customer panels, and `/finance/ap` now keeps one KPI grid instead of duplicating the total payable card above the same total. `docs/design.md` now records that single-value KPI cards must not share a grid row with taller ranked-list/breakdown panels. What is what: sidebar audit proves route coverage, KPI cards show single decision signals, ranked/breakdown panels explain the signal, and filter/export actions keep the shared placement/wording. Why it stays this way: cards should not stretch or duplicate just because a nearby panel is taller, and every design claim must be backed by rendered desktop/mobile evidence. Runtime APIs, formulas, query params, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Sidebar design consistency follow-up on 2026-07-08: active sidebar pages from the latest rendered audit received a UI-only alignment pass for the documented single-design rules. Multi-table selectors on `/reports`, `/finance/ar`, `/finance/ap`, `/trading/dashboard`, `/trading/matching`, `/po-reports/outstanding`, and `/dual-costing/cost-allocation-ledger` were moved toward the shared line-tab visual baseline; spreadsheet export wording/style was normalized on `/reports`, `/finance/ar`, and `/finance/ap`; the disabled placeholder Tax/VAT/WHT export affordance was removed; Cash/Others summary cards now use the shared `KpiCard`; the Production Dashboard gradient header was replaced with a neutral panel; selected filter shells on Daily Expense, Payment Approval, Stock Balance, and Asset Overview were tightened to the current `rounded-xl border border-slate-200/60 bg-white p-4 shadow-sm` language; `/profit-cost-analysis` and `/sales-commission` now use compact mobile filter rows with `MobileFilterSheet`, and Sales Commission summary rows render as mobile cards instead of a wide table. What is what: tabs choose data surfaces, filter cards control the active data scope, KPI cards show decision signals, and export actions only appear when there is a real action. Why it stays this way: the same sidebar audit should not find page-local tab cards, black export variants, fake disabled exports, or long mobile filter forms competing with the data surface. Runtime APIs, formulas, query params, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Sidebar card/table shell continuation on 2026-07-08: the same UI-only pass was extended to the remaining obvious sidebar/page-shell drifts found by static scan. `/stock/ledger` now uses the blue/slate line-tab active state; `/tracking/customer`, `/finance/bank`, `/tracking/supplier`, `/stock/status-convert`, `/stock/convert`, and `/stock/adjust` table shells were aligned to the rounded slate-bordered `shadow-sm` table container; admin and finance-accounting panels such as `/admin/migration-tools`, `/admin/transaction-ledger`, `/admin/company-profile`, `/admin/users`, `/finance-accounting/accounting-periods`, `/finance-accounting/posting-rules`, `/finance-accounting/cash-flow-analysis`, `/finance-accounting/equity-maint`, AR/AP ranked panels, Petty Advance, Money Movement, Daily Expense ranked/form panels, Production Orders, Profile, and Change Password now use the same neutral rounded-xl card shell instead of gradient headers, heavy shadows, or borderless white cards. Remaining old shell matches in the static scan are detail/document routes such as `/purchase/.../[id]`, `/daily/expense/[id]`, and `/sitemap`, not sidebar list/report surfaces. Runtime APIs, formulas, query params, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Customer Tracking table/filter wording follow-up on 2026-07-08: `/tracking/customer` now keeps the module title as Customer Tracking 360° but normalizes the active UI labels in the table/filter/detail surfaces to Thai. The desktop table headers use `รหัส` and `ลูกค้า` instead of `Code` and `Customer`; the year-compare table also uses `ลูกค้า`; search/product filter labels and placeholders now use `ค้นหาลูกค้า`, `สินค้า`, and `เลือกสินค้า`; empty/error/detail fallback text uses Thai customer wording. This is UI wording only; query params, filter state, sorting keys, export contract, table data, formulas, APIs, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Table header no-ellipsis global follow-up on 2026-07-08: shared runtime table headers now enforce the user rule that column headers must not collapse into `...`. `ResizableTableHead` no longer truncates labels, and the 2026-07-09 follow-up keeps header labels on one line with sufficient column width/horizontal overflow instead of wrapping. `docs/design.md` now records that important business labels such as `เลขที่ PO ซื้อ`, `วันที่สร้างเอกสาร`, and `วันที่กำหนดส่ง` must stay explicit and visible rather than being shortened only to fit. Body cells may still truncate long row data when a tooltip/detail path exists. This is UI table-header presentation only; table data, filters, sorting keys, resizing state, row actions, APIs, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

KPI/Summary card single-design sweep on 2026-07-08: active Next KPI/Summary card wrappers were folded into the shared `apps/next/src/components/ui/KpiCard.tsx` implementation. Local wrappers named `Metric`, `StatCard`, `SummaryCard`, `KpiCard`, `MetricCard`, `Tile`, or `Kpi` now only map page data into the shared white card with circular left icon, consistent `text-xs` label, `font-mono text-xl font-bold` value, and optional note. The scan now leaves no page-local KPI card markup outside the shared component itself. This is UI presentation only; page data, filters, formulas, API contracts, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

KPI/Summary card broader follow-up on 2026-07-08: the KPI scan was expanded beyond the original wrapper names to include `DashboardKpi`, `LegacyKpi`, `AnalyticsKpiCard`, `DarkKpi`, `LmeStat`, `ExpenseMetric`, `ImpactCard`, `CostCard`, `MegaCard`, finance dashboard `Card`, reconciliation `Stat`, direct Bank Statement/FCD/Payment Approval/Cash Position/Daily Expense summary blocks, AP/AR hero summaries, loan VAT/cash-flow single-value summaries, and dual-costing `DiffCard`. Those single-value KPI/Summary cards now route through the shared `KpiCard`/`DualCostingStatCard` path. Multi-value breakdown cards, chart panels, ranked-list panels, detail/list row metrics, auth/logo cards, and design mockups are intentionally outside the single-value KPI rule. This is UI presentation only; page data, formulas, APIs, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

PO Buy table header width follow-up on 2026-07-08: `/purchase/po-buy` keeps the explicit document/date header wording (`เลขที่ PO ซื้อ`, `วันที่สร้างเอกสาร`, `วันที่กำหนดส่ง`) instead of shortening business labels, but the resizable column defaults/minimums now reserve enough space so those headers do not collapse into `...` at the default desktop layout. The final action column also follows the canonical auto-stretch colgroup pattern so widescreen extra space is absorbed by the rightmost column. This is UI table layout only; PO Buy filters, sorting keys, table data, row actions, print/export behavior, APIs, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Cost Allocator auto-preview confirm visibility checkpoint on 2026-07-08: `/dual-costing/cost-allocator` now shows the preview section and `ยืนยันการจับคู่` action for non-Manual allocation modes (`FIFO`, `LIFO`, `Cheap`, `Expensive`) as soon as the user selects a target row. The root cause was that preview visibility was previously gated by `showPreview`, which only became `true` through the Manual target-cost calculate flow, so automatic modes could compute candidates but still hide the confirm surface. The page now treats non-Manual modes as auto-preview when a target is selected, while `Manual` still requires explicit target-cost calculation before preview/confirm appears. This is Cost Allocator flow/UI-state behavior only; allocation formulas, confirm payload shape, `POST /api/dual-costing/cost-allocator`, Waiting Allocations full-match policy, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed. Validation passed: `git diff --check`. Full type-check/build could not be rerun in this shell because `node`, `pnpm`, and `corepack` were unavailable.

Analytics Dashboard bar-label readability follow-up on 2026-07-08: `/analytics-dashboard` daily purchase vs sales bar chart now separates the colored fill bar from the numeric label. Values render centered on the full progress/donate-style track instead of being clipped inside the variable-width fill, and the separate white pill around the number was removed after visual review. What is what: the fill bar communicates relative size, while the centered text on the track/fill communicates the exact money amount. Why it stays this way: small or zero values must remain readable and the two chart columns should not look cramped or uneven just because the fill width is narrow. The chart should look like a progress bar, not a label chip placed inside a chart. Follow-up mobile QA also found the fixed share/print action buttons covering chart rows on mobile. They now float only on large screens and render in normal content flow on smaller screens so actions do not cover charts, tables, cards, or bottom navigation. Runtime API behavior, analytics formulas, date filters, exports/share actions, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.

Analytics Dashboard table-density follow-up on 2026-07-08: `/analytics-dashboard` was visually reviewed after the bar-label change and had too many table surfaces stacked vertically. The top flow now keeps filters, KPI cards, the daily purchase/sales progress chart, and group-value breakdown visible first, then moves the row-level detail tables into shared line tabs. What is what: `คู่ค้า` shows Top suppliers and Top customers, `สินค้า` shows Top inbound and outbound products, and `Sale` shows purchase totals by salesperson. These are detail contexts, not separate always-visible dashboard panels. Why it stays this way: the page should read as an analytics dashboard, not a long report made of many tables. Users can choose the detail context they need while still keeping the primary overview and key breakdown available. Runtime API behavior, analytics formulas, date filters, exports/share actions, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.

Profit Cost Analysis design alignment on 2026-07-08: `/profit-cost-analysis` was tightened to the shared report/list design language. Custom table-surface buttons were replaced with shared line tabs; the filter card now separates the top control grid (date range, branch, purchase/sales channel, supplier, customer) from the lower segmented product-category row and right-side clear action; supplier/customer wording and related table headers now use Thai (`ผู้ขาย`, `ลูกค้า`); purple product-category chips were replaced with the neutral segmented filter treatment; duplicate Revenue/GP/average summary panels were removed in favor of drill-down Top 10 product revenue, GP, and stock-value panels. This is UI layout/wording only; `/api/profit-cost-analysis`, query behavior, formulas, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Dashboard Overview filter layout correction on 2026-07-08: `/dashboard` desktop global filter now follows the accepted two-row filter meaning more closely. Date range, branch, product group, supplier, customer, and product filters sit together as the top control grid with wider paired date inputs; quick period buttons move to the lower-left segmented row; the purchase/sales count chip and clear-filter action move to the lower-right summary/action area. Supplier/Customer wording in the dashboard filter sheet was normalized to Thai (`ผู้ขาย`, `ลูกค้า`). This is UI layout/wording only; `GET /api/dashboard`, filter params, KPI formulas, widgets, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Table/filter/action single-design sweep on 2026-07-08: active list/report table scan still leaves only intentional print/PDF/HTML-string document tables without `ns-table`, so runtime table surfaces remain on the single shared `ns-table` / rounded slate shell baseline. Filter/action follow-up removed the remaining visible CSV/XLSX/export variants from active action rows: duplicate Transaction Ledger CSV export was removed in favor of the real Excel export, disabled placeholder CSV/import actions were removed from Bank Reconciliation and Profit Cost Analysis, dual-costing export wording now says `ส่งออก Excel`, and remaining export actions were normalized to the emerald `ส่งออก Excel` treatment. Finance-accounting local `FilterPanel` helpers and the Trading Matching/Sales Control filter shells were moved to the same `rounded-xl border border-slate-200/60 bg-white p-4 shadow-sm` baseline. Real CSV template/import wording remains only where it describes an actual file contract, such as asset-register templates. This is UI/design consistency only; table data, filters, formulas, API contracts, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Tracking 360 date-filter correction on 2026-07-08: `/tracking/product`, `/tracking/customer`, and `/tracking/supplier` now follow the accepted filter reference exactly for calendar scope: the desktop top row shows `วันที่:` with paired `DatePickerInput` controls (`จาก -> ถึง`), master-data selects/comboboxes, and a clear button; `ส่งออก Excel` stays as the green action on the lower-right action row. The temporary custom `TrackingPeriodFilter` / `ช่วงเวลา` control was removed because it created a page-local design variant. The tracking APIs now accept `dateFrom` / `dateTo` while keeping legacy `year` / `month` compatibility for old links. This is read/report filtering and UI-only; formulas, exports, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Duplicate KPI follow-up sweep on 2026-07-08: after the Product Tracking cleanup, obvious remaining count-only cards that duplicated nearby table result counts were removed from `/tracking/supplier`, `/dual-costing/match-log`, `/daily/bill-swap-history`, and `/finance-accounting/loan-contracts`. The remaining top cards on those pages now keep distinct decision signals such as weight, money totals, status/type breakdown, active matches, or outstanding balances. Table pagination/toolbars still show row counts via `พบทั้งหมด ... รายการ`. This is UI-only; filters, table rows, formulas, APIs, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Product Tracking duplicate KPI cleanup on 2026-07-08: `/tracking/product` no longer shows the `รายการ` summary card because it duplicated the table toolbar count such as `พบทั้งหมด 28 รายการ`. `docs/design.md` now explicitly forbids KPI/summary cards that repeat the same information already shown in the table toolbar, pagination summary, tab badge, or nearby table total. This is UI-only; Product Tracking filters, table rows, pagination, export URL, API query behavior, formulas, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Product Tracking filter/action alignment on 2026-07-07: `/tracking/product` filter controls were tightened after visual review so the real `ส่งออก Excel` action stays in the desktop filter row instead of dropping into a lonely second row. Product/Supplier/Customer placeholders and labels now use Thai wording (`สินค้า`, `ผู้ขายฝั่งซื้อ`, `ลูกค้าฝั่งขาย`) to match the shared action/wording consistency rule. This is UI-only; tracking filters, export URL, API query behavior, formulas, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Design consistency rule checkpoint on 2026-07-07: user confirmed that button placement, button colors, and button/action wording must stay consistent across the active Next app. `docs/design.md` now makes this a hard rule: use the documented baseline and closest accepted reference page, do not invent page-local action colors/labels/placement, and do not show placeholder actions such as disabled `CSV` unless the page has a real active export contract and an explicit documented override.

Stock Count Adjust action cleanup on 2026-07-07: `/stock/adjust` now removes the disabled CSV placeholder from the filter action row because the page does not have an active export contract and placeholder actions are not part of the accepted list/filter design. The create action now uses the blue primary list-action treatment, and the mobile FAB is blue as documented in `docs/design.md`. This is UI-only; create link target, form behavior, stock adjustment filters, table data, APIs, permissions, formulas, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Stock Count Adjust filter row hardening on 2026-07-07: after the latest visual review, `/stock/adjust` now forces the lower filter/action strip into a full-width grid so the `ประเภท` segmented filter stays on the left and the create action stays in the lower-right action area instead of visually merging back into the top search/select/date filter row. This is layout-only; stock adjustment filters, create link target, table data, APIs, permissions, formulas, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Stock Count Adjust filter alignment on 2026-07-07: `/stock/adjust` desktop filter card was corrected after visual feedback that the page action floated alone in a tall empty filter area. The top row now keeps only search/select/date/clear controls, while the lower row keeps the `ประเภท` segmented filter on the left and the `+ ปรับสต๊อกใหม่` action on the right inside the same card. This is layout-only; stock adjustment filters, create link, table data, APIs, formulas, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Customer Tracking duplicate-data cleanup on 2026-07-07: `/tracking/customer` no longer repeats the same value as KPI/table amount columns. The top `customers` KPI card was removed because it duplicated the table pagination result count (`พบทั้งหมด X รายการ`). The first tab label was simplified from `รายการ + สถิติ` to `รายการ`. The top KPI set now focuses on different decision signals: weight, revenue, outstanding receivables, and overdue customer count. In the table, `ลูกหนี้` remains the money amount while the former overdue-money column is now `อายุหนี้` with status/age text such as overdue days, so it does not duplicate the receivable amount. No API response shape, source formula, filters, detail drilldown facts, permissions, Supabase schema, or DB state were changed.

Filter layout continuation on 2026-07-07: the latest screenshot baseline supersedes the older `rounded-md bg-white p-3 shadow` filter toolbar notes in this file. Additional list/report filter surfaces were swept so top rows keep search/date/select/clear controls together, page actions such as export/create/refresh move to the lower-right row, and static scans report zero forced full-width search rows and zero top-row action suspects. This remains UI-only; filter state, API/query behavior, formulas, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Filter layout hardening on 2026-07-07: filter unification now follows the latest user screenshot exactly, not the earlier full-width-search-only interpretation. Desktop/tablet filter cards use the `rounded-xl border border-slate-200/60 bg-white p-4 shadow-sm` shell, a top row with search on the left and date/select/clear controls on the right, then a lower row with segmented/status filters on the left and page actions on the right. Tracking 360 filters, `/daily/weight-ticket-list`, `/purchase/bills`, and `/trading/dashboard` were corrected to this structure, and the temporary `md:basis-full md:w-full md:flex-none md:col-span-full` search forcing classes were removed from standard filter search inputs. Tracking export actions use the same green download-icon `ส่งออก Excel` treatment. `docs/design.md` was updated so future pages cannot interpret the baseline as a separate full-width search row.

Table design canonicalization checkpoint on 2026-07-07: the user selected the `/daily/weight-ticket-list` WTI/WTO table look as the single table design to use across active Next pages. The shared table foundation now moves toward that baseline: `Table` uses the rounded-md slate-bordered white shell, `TableHeader` uses the slate header baseline, default `TableRow` no longer adds visible row separators, `TableCell`/`TableNumberCell` use `px-3 py-3`, and `ResizableTableHead` keeps resize handles as hover/drag affordances only without always-visible vertical header separators. `docs/design.md` now marks old `Table / Plain` and `Table / Lined` wording as historical context only. This is UI-only; APIs, formulas, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed. Remaining work is to sweep raw `<table>` implementations and page-local shells in reviewable batches.

Table header visual correction on 2026-07-07: user feedback rejected the always-visible vertical header separator because it made active table headers look too large and busy. Shared `TableHead`, `ResizableTableHead`, and global `.ns-table` CSS now remove default vertical header borders; resizable headers also opt out of the global header padding so the inner sort button is not double-padded, and the resize guide line stays hidden until hover/focus/drag. This is UI-only; table data loading, sorting, resizing state, API behavior, formulas, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Table design sweep completion on 2026-07-07: the raw JSX table sweep was completed for active app screens. Added global `.ns-table` / `.ns-table-shell` CSS in `apps/next/src/app/globals.css`, made shared `Table` emit `ns-table`, and mechanically tagged 182 raw JSX `<table className="...">` instances across `apps/next/src` with `ns-table` while preserving existing layout classes such as visibility, min-width, and fixed table layout. Follow-up scan found zero raw JSX table className instances missing `ns-table`; the only untagged `<table>` occurrences are intentional print/PDF/HTML-string document tables with their own stylesheet. This remains UI-only; APIs, formulas, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

App shell loading performance fix on 2026-07-07: `/daily/weight-ticket-list` appeared to sit on `กำลังโหลดข้อมูล` while the top menu also showed `กำลังโหลดเมนู...`. Dev logs showed the slow path was dominated by repeated auth/proxy work and dev HMR/cache churn, not the table design CSS. The app shell previously triggered `/api/auth/me` from multiple layout components (`AppShell`, sidebar navigation, mobile bottom nav, and user status). `AppShell` now loads auth context once, reuses it for menu search, sidebar navigation, mobile bottom navigation, password-change redirect, and passes user/role profile data into `AuthStatus`. A static scan now keeps layout-level `/api/auth/me` at one call. This is shell/auth-loading performance only; route permissions, API handlers, formulas, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Filter design final follow-up on 2026-07-07: missed filter-adjacent surfaces were folded into the same baseline. Admin audit/user toolbars, Financial Dashboard, Tax/VAT/WHT mobile filter sheet, Production dashboard date range controls, Dashboard analytics/daily date range controls, Reports directory search/tabs, and mobile `ตัวกรอง` buttons now use the same `rounded-md`, `p-3`, `h-9`, white/neutral slate filter treatment instead of page-local `p-4`/`p-5`, `rounded-xl`/`rounded-lg`, gradient, purple, or grey toggle variants. This remains UI-only; filter behavior and API/query contracts were not changed.

Filter design unification checkpoint on 2026-07-07: list/report filter surfaces were swept against the accepted `/purchase/bills` baseline. The visible filter toolbar shell is now standardized on `rounded-md bg-white p-3 shadow`, with `h-9` controls, `rounded-md` filter buttons/inputs, no page-local `rounded-xl`/`p-4` filter shells, and no search emoji prefix on `ตัวกรอง` buttons. The shared date picker popover remains widened through `DatePickerInput` / `DatePicker` and `Calendar`. This is UI-only: filter state, query params, APIs, formulas, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed. Browser UAT was not run in this checkpoint per the current instruction to avoid browser testing unless explicitly requested.

Line tab consistency hardening on 2026-07-07: tab selectors for data/report surfaces were normalized to the single shared Line Tabs pattern. Controls that switch between multiple tables or report surfaces use shared `TabsList variant="line"` / `TabsTrigger variant="line"` outside the filter card; filter-card segmented controls are reserved for real filter conditions such as status, type, or quick range. Updated `docs/design.md` to make this a hard design rule: no custom pill/segmented/color-button/card tab containers for table/data-surface tabs without explicit approved override. This is presentation-only; APIs, formulas, permissions, Supabase schema, and business behavior were not changed.

Tracking 360 design alignment on 2026-07-07: `/tracking/customer`, `/tracking/supplier`, and `/tracking/product` were aligned to the documented Tracking 360 layout baseline. Customer/Supplier now expose the product selector that their API/filter state already supported, use the standard `rounded-xl border border-slate-200/60 bg-white p-4 shadow-sm` filter shell, mobile compact search + `MobileFilterSheet`, shared line tabs for data-surface selection, and a shared pagination row capped to `10 / หน้า` and `25 / หน้า`. Supplier now separates `สรุปตามสินค้า` into its own line tab instead of stacking Product breakdown below the main Supplier list. Product Tracking now exposes Product/Supplier/Customer filters, removes `Stock` and `WAC` from the primary table/mobile cards/XLSX export, keeps stock access only in the detail `Stock Support` link, and uses the same pagination/mobile sheet pattern. Top 10 panels across the three pages show 5 rows first and expand to 10 on demand. This is read/report presentation plus Product Tracking export cleanup only: KPI formulas, detail drilldown facts, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed. Validation passed: targeted ESLint for the three Tracking clients plus Product Tracking API, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, scoped `git diff --check` for touched files, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and authenticated Playwright smoke for the three Tracking routes on desktop/mobile with screenshots under `reports/playwright/tracking-360-postfix`.

Sidebar navigation rollback on 2026-07-07: the user clarified the desired sidebar is the legacy/raw-icon look from the latest screenshot, not the interim lucide icon-box design. `AppNavigation` was restored to render `navigationItems.icon` directly, keep plain dark list rows, avoid card-style active row backgrounds, and use compact text triangle glyphs (`▾` / `▸`) for section/menu expansion. The temporary `NavigationIcon` component was removed. Topbar/mobile search dropdown styling remains the existing app-shell raw-icon style. This is UI-shell/navigation presentation only; routes, labels, permissions, auth/session loading, search matching, page behavior, APIs, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Dashboard filter design alignment on 2026-07-07: `/dashboard` global filter now follows the shared filter baseline instead of the earlier oversized custom surface. Desktop uses the standard `rounded-md bg-white p-3 shadow` shell, `h-9` controls, slate segmented quick-range buttons, visible date range, scope count chip, and a reset button only when filters are active. The branch/category/Supplier/Customer/product fields stay in the same neutral filter card but use a tighter responsive grid so search fields do not stretch into ambiguous blank space. Mobile keeps the compact strip plus `MobileFilterSheet` pattern, with `h-9` filter controls and neutral slate apply/focus treatment so responsive filters do not drift back to blue custom styling. This is UI-only: `GET /api/dashboard`, `/api/owner-daily`, report formulas, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

FX Rate table-card follow-up on 2026-07-02: `/finance/foreign/fx-rate` now keeps the desktop rate-history summary/reset-width controls inside the same lined `rounded-md border border-slate-200 bg-white shadow-sm` card as the resizable desktop table. Mobile keeps the summary controls above the card list. This is UI-only; FX rate filters, create/edit validation, latest-rate cards, sorting/resizing state, APIs, permissions, and DB state were not changed.

Dashboard runtime stability checkpoint on 2026-07-07: `/api/dashboard` failed with `500` on `ns-dev.devkub.com` after login for `date=2026-07-07&from=2026-01-01&to=2026-07-07`. Local server-side repro showed `buildMainDashboards()` timed out with `DATABASE_POOL_MAX=1` because the main dashboard read model fired a large `Promise.all` batch against the single-connection Prisma/pg pool. Raising the pool to 8 made the same payload pass, confirming the failure was connection-pool contention rather than report formula or schema mismatch. The fix keeps dashboard formulas and source tables unchanged but runs the main read batch sequentially through `runReadBatch()` so the route respects the existing low-pool Supabase runtime contract. Repro validation passed with `DATABASE_POOL_MAX=1` using the same date range.

Dashboard Overview duplicate insight cleanup on 2026-07-07: `/dashboard` now removes the remaining duplicate insight widgets `Expense Breakdown`, `Channel Performance`, and `Quick Insights`. The top chart area is now only `Revenue vs Expense (Monthly)` plus `Cash Flow Overview`, and `Receivables & Payables Aging` now spans the full next row before the line-tab detail surfaces. This keeps the read order as global filters -> runtime notice -> KPI cards -> primary trend/cash charts -> Aging -> Top Ranking / Stock / Metrics tabs, and prevents values already shown in KPI cards, Aging, or Metrics from appearing again as separate cards. This is UI-only/read-only: `GET /api/dashboard`, formulas, filters, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed. Validation passed: targeted ESLint for `MainDashboardsPageClient.tsx`, scoped `git diff --check` (only existing LF/CRLF warning for the TSX file), `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, and `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next` with 302 routes generated successfully.

Stock Finance filter-toolbar cleanup on 2026-07-07: `/finance-accounting/stock-finance` no longer shows a separate top filter card for `ณ วันที่` and branch. The same API scope controls now live inside each active table filter row/sheet (`Stock ทั้งหมด` and `สินค้าหมุนช้า / ควรเร่งระบาย`) alongside search/category/age filters, so users have one filter surface after choosing the table context. The `Stock ทั้งหมด` toolbar also no longer shows duplicate `Qty` and `มูลค่า` chips; it keeps count, pagination, and reset-width only because quantity/value are already available in the summary cards and table columns. This is UI-only/read-only: `GET /api/finance-accounting/stock-finance`, formulas, cutoff behavior, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Dashboard Overview detail-tab cleanup on 2026-07-07: `/dashboard` now renders the `อันดับคู่ค้า` / `Stock` / `ตัวชี้วัดย่อย` selector with the shared line-tab pattern (`TabsList variant="line"` and `TabsTrigger variant="line"`) instead of large card-like buttons. These tabs are data-surface selectors, not KPI cards, so they should stay compact and aligned with the WTI/WTO and production report tab baseline. This is UI-only/read-only: tab content, default tab, `GET /api/dashboard`, formulas, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Dashboard Overview hero cleanup on 2026-07-07: `/dashboard` no longer renders the large gradient `Dashboard Overview` hero/banner because the app shell and filter surface already identify the page/scope, while the first useful dashboard work surface should be the filter, runtime notice, KPI cards, and aging/ranking/stock widgets. This removes duplicate page-title decoration and keeps the management overview denser without changing KPI cards, `Receivables & Payables Aging`, filters, formulas, `GET /api/dashboard`, permissions, Supabase schema, or DB state.

Dashboard Overview duplicate AR/AP alert cleanup on 2026-07-07: `/dashboard` no longer renders the amber AR/AP overdue alert strip below the overview/risk panels because the same receivable/payable information is already shown with more useful aging buckets in `Receivables & Payables Aging`. The KPI cards and Aging table remain, so users still see the high-level AR/AP totals and the detailed Current/1-30/31-60/61-90/>90/Total breakdown without a duplicate banner competing for attention. This is UI-only/read-only dashboard presentation work: `GET /api/dashboard`, formulas, filters, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.

Weight Ticket Dashboard WTI/WTO split checkpoint on 2026-07-07: `/daily/weight-ticket-dashboard` now separates row-level WTI and WTO work into dedicated dashboard views instead of mixing both directions in one follow-up-documents table. The visible views are `Overview`, `WTI inbound`, `WTO outbound`, and `Product summary`; the old document-type segmented filter was removed from the UI because WTI/WTO context now belongs to the view selector. `GET /api/daily/weight-ticket-dashboard` now returns `wtiRows` and `wtoRows` from the same fetched `weight_tickets` data, with `followUpWeight` mapped to WTI remaining/PB wait and WTO pending_out respectively. Each WTI/WTO table has its own toolbar summary, page size capped to `10/page` and `25/page`, previous/next pagination, resizable columns with restore-table-defaults, and dense mobile cards. Rows needing follow-up are sorted first, followed by latest documents. This is read-only/dashboard presentation work: WTI/WTO save/edit/cancel, print/share, KPI formulas, permissions, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.


Stock Finance Analysis table checkpoint on 2026-07-06: `/finance-accounting/stock-finance` now shows both requested table surfaces behind line tabs instead of stacking two long tables vertically. The default tab is the full read-only `Stock ทั้งหมด` table modeled after the requested stock list, while the second tab restores the read-only `สินค้าหมุนช้า / ควรเร่งระบาย` Top 15 table from `slowMoving`; each tab shows a small count badge. The slow-moving table has sortable/resizable columns for code, name, category, Qty, WAC, value, last sale, standard price, and margin opportunity, with dense mobile cards. The full stock table keeps search by code/name/group, group filter, age bucket filter, Qty/WAC/value, and `อายุ Stock`; it does not render a status badge column or status filter because status is already summarized in the production status donut. Desktop keeps the full filter row attached to the table, while mobile uses the standard search + `ตัวกรอง` button with `MobileFilterSheet`. The existing status donut/card remains the only status summary; no separate status table was added. The `สินค้า 10 อันดับมูลค่าสูงสุด` card still uses the API Top 10 list, but now shows only the first 5 rows by default and exposes a expand/collapse button to reveal all 10 rows only when needed. The stock finance API returns `products` sorted by value for the full table while preserving the existing summary, aging, top-products, slow-moving, formula, cutoff, permission, and no-write behavior. Validation: targeted ESLint for the stock finance component/helper passed with only existing unrelated warnings; `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false` passed.

Stock Finance pagination follow-up on 2026-07-06: both `/finance-accounting/stock-finance` table tabs now render a pagination/toolbar row before the data area. Page size options are capped to `10 / หน้า` and `25 / หน้า`, so no table renders more than 25 rows per page. The toolbar also carries the reset-width control when custom column widths exist, keeping filters -> pagination/toolbar -> table order aligned with `docs/design.md`.

Stock Finance hierarchy cleanup on 2026-07-06: removed the three lower `พร้อมขาย` / `วัตถุดิบรอผลิต` / `สต็อกจมเงิน 90+ วัน` insight cards from `/finance-accounting/stock-finance` because they repeated the same decisions already visible in the status donut and aging panel. The page flow is now global filters -> stock value overview + production status -> aging and Top 10 products -> table tabs.

Supplier ADV/PB VAT settlement correction checkpoint on 2026-07-10: Purchase Bill settlement now treats Supplier ADV as a pre-VAT base credit, not as a post-VAT payment. When PB references ADV, the allocation uses only the ADV base/subtotal amount; ADV VAT is not deducted from PB directly. PB `vat_amount` and `total_amount` are refreshed from the remaining PB base after ADV deduction, so no-VAT PB subtracts ADV base from the PB base directly, and VAT PB subtracts ADV base first then calculates PB VAT on the remaining base. `paid_amount` now represents real PMT payment only, while `supplier_advance_allocations.allocated_amount` / `allocated_subtotal_amount` represent the ADV base used by PB. The purchase bill form preview was aligned to the same rule and the ADV dropdown now labels remaining usable balance as base balance. Browser UAT was not run per current instruction to avoid browser testing unless explicitly requested.

Supplier ADV base-balance read-model follow-up on 2026-07-10: `/api/purchase/bills` ADV options now calculate usable ADV balance from `supplier_advance_payments.subtotal_amount - active supplier_advance_allocations.allocated_subtotal_amount` instead of trusting `supplier_advance_payments.remaining_amount` alone. This fixes mixed old/new data where an existing VAT ADV still had `remaining_amount` stored as gross remaining, causing the PB form preview to subtract too much base before recalculating VAT. The purchase bill dropdown copy now says `ใช้หักได้` and the helper copy says `เงินมัดจำเหลือหลังหัก` so reviewers do not read the value as gross ADV balance.

Stock Finance slow-moving filter follow-up on 2026-07-06: the `สินค้าหมุนช้า / ควรเร่งระบาย` tab now has its own table filter row and mobile filter sheet. It supports search by code/name/category and category filtering, while intentionally omitting status and age filters because the tab is already a Top 15 slow-moving subset from the server.

Advance payments tab surface checkpoint on 2026-07-06: `/purchase/advance-payments` now renders a two-tab surface. The `จ่ายเงินล่วงหน้า` tab keeps the existing Supplier ADV runtime, while `รับเงินล่วงหน้า` reuses the canonical customer receipt runtime (`MoneyMovementPageClient mode="receipt"`) instead of reviving the retired `/finance/customer-advance` page. This keeps one active customer money-in flow through `/sales/receipts` while still giving users a tab entry point from the money advance page. Updated sitemap/navigation/page-flow docs to show `/purchase/advance-payments` as the 2-tab surface backed by Supplier ADV APIs and Sales Receipt APIs. Validation passed: targeted ESLint for `src/app/purchase/advance-payments/page.tsx`, `src/components/purchase-flow/AdvancePaymentsTabbedPageClient.tsx`, and `src/app/api/finance/ar/route.ts`; `git diff --check`.

Customer advance duplicate page removal checkpoint on 2026-07-06: `/finance/customer-advance` was removed from the active app because the user confirmed it duplicates `/sales/receipts`. Deleted the route page and unused `CustomerAdvancePageClient`, and changed AR customer-advance drilldown links away from the removed page to the canonical customer receipt surface. `/sales/receipts` remains the page for receiving customer money; the CADV read API is intentionally left in place for now because sales bills/AR still need customer-advance source facts until the receipt/advance model is fully normalized. Updated active sitemap/inventory/page-flow docs to mark the old Customer Advance page as retired rather than an active sidebar/page target. Validation passed: targeted ESLint for `src/app/api/finance/ar/route.ts` and `git diff --check`.

Supplier ADV downstream presentation checkpoint on 2026-07-06: AP drilldown, PB detail/direct detail, PB print, and `/daily/payment-approval` now read the VAT-aware ADV allocation/source metadata created by the Supplier ADV VAT/type batch. Downstream screens show ADV type, invoice no, VAT mode, and allocated base/VAT/total where relevant so reviewers do not see only the gross deducted amount. Tax VAT/WHT remains purchase-bill sourced by design: ADV VAT is allocation support for PB settlement and must not be counted as an additional standalone input-VAT transaction until a dedicated tax ledger/filing model exists. Validation: targeted ESLint for touched AP/PB/payment-approval files passed; `git diff --check` passed; full type-check is blocked by the pre-existing unrelated `DualCostingWorkflowStrip` missing symbol in `DualCostingManagementPageClient.tsx`, with no ADV/PB/AP type errors remaining.

Supplier ADV VAT/type runtime checkpoint on 2026-07-06: the first implementation slice is in code and the SQL migration has been applied to dev-target. Added migration `supabase/migrations/20260706093000_add_supplier_advance_type_vat_breakdown.sql` and Prisma fields for `supplier_advance_payments.advance_type`, `invoice_no`, `vat_type`, `vat_rate_percent`, `subtotal_amount`, `vat_amount`, `total_amount`, plus `supplier_advance_allocations.allocated_subtotal_amount`, `allocated_vat_amount`, and `allocated_total_amount`. The Advance Payments form now switches between current waiting-sort ADV and invoice-advance ADV; invoice-advance requires invoice no. Current VAT rule: when `มี VAT`, the amount input is the pre-VAT base amount; the system calculates VAT and total ADV amount from that base, and PMA/PMT/remaining use the total amount. ADV list/detail/print/export expose type/invoice/VAT fields. PB allocation now blocks VAT ADV against no-VAT PB and stores allocation base/VAT/total breakdown while preserving the existing gross `allocated_amount` for payment/AP compatibility. Supabase MCP apply migration was denied by account permission, so the migration was applied through local `psql` using dev-target `DATABASE_URL`, then recorded in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations` as version `20260706093000`. Validation passed so far: `npm run prisma:generate --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, targeted ESLint for touched files, and dev-target column verification. Remaining: run service checks/browser UAT only when requested, and follow up AP/Tax report presentation if needed.

Supplier ADV VAT/type design checkpoint on 2026-07-06: the target flow for `/purchase/advance-payments` now separates ADV into `มัดจำส่งของรอคัดแยก` and `มัดจำล่วงหน้ายังไม่ส่งของ`. The current form remains the default for `มัดจำส่งของรอคัดแยก` with no invoice required and VAT defaulting to no VAT. The special `มัดจำล่วงหน้ายังไม่ส่งของ` form must collect only branch, supplier, invoice no, amount, VAT dropdown, and remark; invoice no is required. PB allocation rules are now documented: no-VAT ADV may continue reducing `PB.total_amount` as a total amount, but VAT ADV must allocate base amount and VAT separately instead of subtracting the gross amount from the PB total. Updated docs: `docs/notes/Supplier Advance Payment Flow.md`, `docs/notes/page-flows/daily-transactions-purchase-advance-payments.md`, `docs/notes/Purchase Bills Page Flow.md`, and `docs/migration/09-implementation-tasklist.md`. Runtime code/schema were not changed in this checkpoint.

Git sync safety checkpoint on 2026-07-05: before the next fetch/merge/pull/push/deploy check, protect the dirty local workspace first. The local UI work did not actually vanish; it was hidden in `stash@{0}` after sync work returned the checkout to clean `dev`, and one required untracked file (`apps/next/src/components/ui/MobileFilterSheet.tsx`) had to be restored separately from the stash's untracked parent. For future work, do not sync shared branches directly from a dirty main workspace. First run `git status --short --branch` and `git remote -v`, classify local modified/untracked files, use a temporary worktree for merge/conflict checks when possible, and only push after proving the intended files are present in the commit. A Git push must also be followed by a separate Vercel/server deploy check because an ignored/canceled build can leave `ns-dev` unchanged.

Mobile bottom navigation responsive checkpoint on 2026-07-05: the bottom nav disappeared in Chrome responsive mode at width 957px because `MobileBottomNavigation` used `md:hidden`, so Tailwind hid it from 768px upward even though the app shell and page layouts still behaved as mobile/tablet below `lg`. The bottom nav now uses `lg:hidden` in both loading and loaded states, and `AppShell` keeps main bottom spacing as `pb-20 lg:pb-6` so content is not covered by the fixed nav. This is shell/layout-only; navigation targets, permissions, auth, page data, APIs, Supabase data, schema, and DB state were not changed. Validation passed: targeted ESLint for `AppShell.tsx` and `MobileBottomNavigation.tsx`, static responsive assertion for `lg:hidden`/`pb-20 lg:pb-6`, scoped `git diff --check` (only LF/CRLF warnings), and local server smoke after restarting a corrupted Next dev cache (`/login` and `/api/health` both returned 200).

Mobile detail modal CSS checkpoint on 2026-07-05: the WTI/WTO detail modal on iPhone-size responsive width (430px) showed only the dark header area and hid the white body because the mobile app-dialog override in `globals.css` matched any direct `.bg-slate-900` child of `[data-ns-dialog-content="app"]`. `DialogHeader` is also `.bg-slate-900`, so the CSS treated the header as the flex content shell and expanded it to fill the modal. The selector now excludes `[data-ns-dialog-header]`, and direct dialog body children such as `.bg-slate-50` / `.overflow-y-auto` receive `flex: 1`, `min-height: 0`, and `overflow-y: auto`. This is CSS/layout-only; modal data loading, WTI/WTO APIs, print/share actions, permissions, Supabase data, schema, and DB state were not changed. Validation passed: scoped `git diff --check` for `globals.css`, static selector assertion, `/login` returned 200, and a browser layout probe at 430px confirmed header `flex-grow = 0`, header height about 77px, body height about 856px, and body `overflow-y = auto`.

Mobile detail modal follow-up audit on 2026-07-05: a static scan found 20 source files / 31 app-shell dialog blocks with direct `DialogHeader` or the same dark-header modal pattern. The shared selector fix is intentionally global for `[data-ns-dialog-content="app"]`, so these candidates are covered without page-by-page patches. The two true mobile full-screen dialog candidates were `WeightTicketDetailModal.tsx` and `ReceiptVouchersPageClient.tsx`; both rely on the same header guard and direct-body flex rule.

Runtime API route recovery on 2026-07-05: local `next dev` on `http://localhost:3000` was serving pages but protected and public App Router route handlers returned HTML `404` after auth, so dashboard data looked like it was not loading. The cause was the root catch-all page `apps/next/src/app/[...slug]/page.tsx` intercepting `/api/*` requests after proxy/auth allowed the request through. The catch-all was removed because the current local version only returned `notFound()` and default Next 404 behavior already covers unknown pages. After removal, `/api/health` returns `200 application/json`, logged-in `/api/auth/me` returns `200`, `POST /api/activity` returns `200`, and logged-in `/api/owner-daily?date=2026-07-05` returns `200` with the dashboard payload keys. This is routing/runtime-only; API contracts, auth logic, permissions, Supabase data, schema, and dashboard formulas were not changed. Full lint/type-check were attempted but exceeded 120s and were stopped; scoped `git diff --check -- apps/next/src/app/[...slug]/page.tsx` passed.

Mobile bottom navigation checkpoint on 2026-07-04: after the mobile sidebar was converted into a full-screen menu with its own search field, the duplicate bottom-nav `Search` action was replaced with a primary `Daily` destination. The mobile bottom nav now shows `Menu`, `Daily`, `Dashboard`, and `Account` where permissions allow it; `Daily` first routes to `/daily/weight-ticket-list`, then falls back to `/daily-report` or `/daily/expense` based on access. The active state is derived from the existing navigation section metadata so the tab represents the daily workflow rather than a second menu search shortcut. This is mobile shell navigation UI only; routes, permissions, page behavior, APIs, Supabase data, schema, and DB state were not changed. Targeted ESLint for `MobileBottomNavigation.tsx`/`AppShell.tsx` and Next type-check passed.

Mobile menu shell polish on 2026-07-04: after the user compared the mobile sidebar to a real mobile-app pattern, the bottom-nav `Menu` action was changed from a narrow desktop-style drawer into a full-screen mobile navigation panel. On mobile, the menu now covers the viewport, keeps the existing dark header/search/navigation/account surfaces, adds a clear header close button, and hides the bottom navigation while the menu is open so the old page is no longer visible on the right side. Desktop sidebar expand/collapse behavior, navigation permissions, menu data, search behavior, account/auth/logout behavior, routes, APIs, Supabase data, schema, and DB state were not changed. Targeted ESLint for `AppShell.tsx` passed.

Sidebar account menu polish on 2026-07-04: after the user pointed out the sidebar user dropdown, the account menu in `AuthStatus` was tightened visually without changing auth/session behavior. The profile/account action now has clearer dark-menu contrast, the menu surface uses a stronger floating popup shadow and spacing, and logout is a softer red action state instead of a heavy filled red row. This is UI-only; `/profile`, logout, Supabase auth, session loading, roles, permissions, route guards, and database state were not changed. Targeted ESLint for `AuthStatus.tsx` passed.

Full sidebar visual QA checkpoint on 2026-07-04: after the user explicitly asked for a detailed browser/design recheck, the active sidebar routes from `apps/next/src/lib/navigation.ts` were audited against `docs/design.md` using the existing Playwright sidebar design script. The first batch checked 95 routes and found one remaining table-mechanics warning on `/dashboard`; the remaining 10 admin/master-data routes passed. `/dashboard` was then aligned with the shared resizable table-header baseline for the Receivables & Payables Aging table without changing dashboard data, API calls, filters, formulas, permissions, Supabase data, schema, or DB state. Targeted rerun passed `/dashboard`, and the merged final evidence shows 105/105 active sidebar routes passed with 0 warnings and 0 failures in `reports/playwright/full-design-audit-20260704-final/report.md`. Supporting batch evidence is in `reports/playwright/full-design-audit-20260704-initial/`, `reports/playwright/full-design-audit-20260704-remaining/`, and `reports/playwright/full-design-audit-20260704-dashboard-rerun/`.

Design drift cleanup follow-up on 2026-07-03: after the active Next UI/theme audit, the remaining active-page drift was normalized without changing business/API behavior. The hidden `/daily/design-mockup` mockup route now returns `notFound()` so it is no longer an active app surface; the finance-accounting and daily mobile filter sheets that still used hand-written bottom-sheet shells now use the shared `MobileFilterSheet`; and dense mobile card alternatives were added for purchase bill detail product/allocation tables, sales bill detail item/source-usage tables, WTI/WTO detail usage/downstream tables, and the WTO stock-return dialog while keeping the existing desktop tables on `lg+`. This is UX/UI/layout-only; filters, form state, save/return actions, links, permissions, APIs, Supabase data, schema, calculations, print/PDF output, and DB state were not changed. Browser/Playwright UAT was not run because the user asked to fix from project code and current project rules avoid browser testing unless explicitly requested. WTI/WTO PDF blank-page follow-up on 2026-07-03: `/daily/weight-ticket-list` React PDF output was checked against the real `WTI012607-0006` share PDF after the user reported an empty page 2. The uploaded PDF had 3 A4 pages with page 2 fully blank between the print form and the photo album. The local reproduction rendered the same real ticket from dev DB and confirmed `pageCount=3`; the fix reduces the last-page vertical spacing (page padding and signature spacing) instead of disabling PDF pagination, so the compact ticket form stays within a true A4 page and the photo album becomes page 2 directly. Verification now renders `WTI012607-0006` as 2 A4 pages and the fixture harness still asserts 1 print page + 1 album page. This is PDF layout-only; WTI/WTO data, LINE payload text, storage upload paths, images, APIs, permissions, stock, and DB state were not changed.

WTI/WTO detail modal action-header follow-up on 2026-07-03: `/daily/weight-ticket-list` detail modal now moves the `พิมพ์`, `แชร์`, and `ปิด` actions from the bottom footer into the dark header action group, hides the default top-right `X`, and removes the duplicate bottom action footer. The `ปิด` action remains visible during loading/error states, while print/share remain available after the ticket loads. On mobile the same detail modal now uses a full-screen `100dvh` app-style shell with safe-area header/body padding, a compact top-right action group beside the document title, icon-only mobile action buttons, tighter card spacing, and a narrower timeline time column; desktop keeps the centered modal layout and icon+text actions. Header action colors now make `พิมพ์` green and `ปิด` red while leaving `แชร์` neutral. This is UI-only; WTI/WTO data loading, edit/cancel/stock-return actions, print/share behavior, APIs, permissions, and DB state were not changed.

Receipt Voucher form modal design follow-up on 2026-07-03: `/purchase/receipt-vouchers` create/edit modal now uses the same dark-header `Dialog` shell baseline as `/daily/weight-ticket-list`: full-screen `100dvh` on mobile, centered `90vh` desktop shell, no top-right default `X`, scrollable slate body, white grouped cards, and header action buttons instead of a bottom modal footer. The save action is green and the cancel action is red to match the approved action-header treatment. This is UI-only; Supplier/PB selection, read-only item snapshot, cash-only RV behavior, print behavior, APIs, permissions, and DB state were not changed.

Receipt Voucher detail modal follow-up on 2026-07-03: `/purchase/receipt-vouchers` detail modal now matches the approved WTI/WTO detail modal shell more closely. It uses shared `DialogContent`, full-screen `100dvh` mobile layout, centered desktop shell, dark header, header action buttons with green `พิมพ์` and red `ปิด`, no duplicate bottom footer, and white grouped detail/timeline cards inside a slate body. This is UI-only; RV detail data, print behavior, cancel flow, APIs, permissions, and DB state were not changed.

WTI/WTO mobile filter sheet follow-up on 2026-07-03: `/daily/weight-ticket-list` mobile filter sheet now uses a stronger app-style bottom sheet with a dark `bg-slate-900` header, white scroll body, fixed white action footer with safe-area bottom padding, and higher overlay stacking so it visually owns the bottom of the screen instead of floating above the mobile bottom navigation. Tapping the dimmed backdrop closes the sheet, while clicks inside the sheet stay contained. This is UI-only; filter query state, list loading, export, pagination, APIs, permissions, and DB state were not changed.

App-wide modal consistency checkpoint on 2026-07-03: active `apps/next` modals now use the approved WTI/WTO-style shell as the baseline: slate overlay, dark `bg-slate-900` header/shell, `rounded-md`, `border-0`, `!p-0`, hidden default top-right `X` for `DialogContent`, slate/white body-footer separation, and footer/header close actions instead of duplicate close controls. The pass covered shared `DialogContent` consumers plus custom `fixed inset-0` modal overlays in daily, finance, finance-accounting, stock, master-data, purchase/sales, production, trading/tracking, main, and admin flows; mobile filter bottom sheets remain intentionally separate because they are app-style filter drawers rather than record/detail modals. This is UI-only; modal open/close state, form payloads, print/share behavior, calculations, APIs, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed. Validation passed: `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next` (0 errors, 2 existing warnings), `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, and `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`. Browser/Playwright UAT was not run per the current instruction to avoid browser testing unless explicitly requested.

App-wide modal consistency follow-up scan on 2026-07-03: every active `apps/next` `DialogContent`, custom `fixed inset-0` modal overlay, and remaining `DialogFooter` consumer was rechecked after the user asked to compare all modal pages. The only main modal stragglers found were `/profit-cost-analysis` product detail and `/cash-flow-calendar` day detail, where the shell had already moved to the dark modal baseline but the `ปิด` action still rendered as a gray text link; both now use the same red header close button as the approved WTI/WTO and Receipt Voucher modals, and the cash calendar detail header no longer uses a bright divider on the dark header. Remaining bottom `DialogFooter` actions are intentional for confirm/cancel/share/picker dialogs and mobile filter bottom sheets, not record/detail/form modals. This is UI-only; route behavior, form payloads, calculations, APIs, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed. Validation passed: `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next` (0 errors, 1 existing warning in `apps/next/scripts/qa-thai-font.tsx`), `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `git diff --check`, and `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`. Browser/Playwright UAT was not run per the current instruction to avoid browser testing unless explicitly requested.

Transaction Bills total-summary visual follow-up on 2026-07-03: `/daily/transaction-bills` purchase and sales bill form modals now render the VAT/total summary card with a white background, slate border, and subtle shadow instead of the previous blue/green filled panel. This is UI-only; purchase/sales bill calculations, VAT/discount/advance logic, save payloads, APIs, permissions, and DB state were not changed.

Assigned Plane backlog Excel export checkpoint on 2026-07-03: only our assigned backlog issues `NSERP-140` through `NSERP-144` were implemented. `/purchase/receipt-vouchers`, `/daily/weight-tickets`, `/production/orders`, `/production/report`, and `/production/yield-loss-report` now expose filtered Excel `.xlsx` exports through their server routes and preserve the active list/report filters, sorting where already supported, and existing read-model behavior. `/purchase/receipt-vouchers`, `/daily/weight-ticket-list`, `/production/orders`, and `/production/report` now show the Thai `ส่งออก Excel` export action in the page UI, while `/sales/po-sell` keeps its existing export behavior but changes the visible action text from `Export Excel` to `ส่งออก Excel`. No DB schema, data migration, permissions, document lifecycle, stock, AP/AR, costing, or production business-state behavior changed; this batch is export/UI-label only. Validation passed: targeted ESLint for the touched export files, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next` (0 errors, 2 pre-existing warnings), `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, and `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next` after clearing stale `.next` and using `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192`. Local Playwright proof was captured in `reports/playwright/nserp-140-144-proof/`; the same session confirmed each Excel action returns HTTP 200 with `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet`. Plane closeout was done through the API only: `NSERP-140` through `NSERP-144` were moved to Wait for test, Thai closeout comments were posted, and the proof screenshots were attached to their matching issues.

WTO return stock-ledger checkpoint on 2026-07-02: flow `รับของคืน` ของ `WTO` ยังมี owner เป็น `WTO` เสมอ แม้ action จะถูก trigger ต่อจากฝั่ง `Sales Bill` ก็ตาม. ดังนั้น row `stock_ledger.ref_type = WTO-RETURN-LOSS` ต้องอ้างอิง `ref_id/ref_no` เป็นเลขที่ `WTO` ต้นทาง ไม่ใช่เลข `SB` ที่กดทำรายการ loss. `apps/next/src/lib/server/stock-holds.ts` ถูกแก้ใน `closeActiveWtoPendingOutForSalesBillReturn()` ให้เขียน `ref_id/ref_no = hold.source_doc_no`, และ `GET /api/stock/ledger` ถูกแก้ให้ resolve คู่ค้า `WTO-RETURN-LOSS` จาก `weight_tickets.party_name` ของ WTO ที่อ้างถึงโดยตรง เพื่อให้คอลัมน์ `ผู้ขาย/ผู้ซื้อ` และลิงก์เอกสารถูกต้องตามเอกสารจริง. มีการซ่อมข้อมูล dev DB ที่ผิดอยู่แล้ว 2 แถวใน `stock_ledger` (เดิมอ้าง `SB2607-0008`) ให้กลับไปอ้าง `WTO012607-0010` เรียบร้อยแล้ว. Validation ผ่าน `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false` และ `git diff --check`.

Sales Bill durable line-number checkpoint on 2026-07-02: `/api/sales/bills` no longer falls back to array index or legacy payload fields when assigning `sales_bill_lines.line_no` during create/edit. Every submitted Sales Bill row must resolve to one durable `line_no`; edit payloads that reference the same existing `salesBillLineNo` more than once are rejected immediately, and new split rows now allocate the next `line_no` from the bill's full historical line set, not just currently active rows. This prevents `sales_bill_lines(sales_bill_id, line_no)` unique collisions when an older split line was reversed in a previous edit and preserves append-only audit history instead of silently reusing line identity.

Sales Bill stock weight hydration checkpoint on 2026-07-02: `STOCK` Sales Bill lines now persist and read back the weight fields with one contract only: `gross_weight` / `net_weight` represent `à¸ˆà¸³à¸™à¸§à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸‚à¸²à¸¢à¹„à¸”à¹‰` à¸‚à¸­à¸‡ line à¸ˆà¸²à¸ WTO, while `qty` remains `à¸™à¹‰à¸³à¸«à¸™à¸±à¸à¸‚à¸²à¸¢à¸ªà¸¸à¸—à¸˜à¸´` à¸«à¸¥à¸±à¸‡à¸«à¸±à¸à¸ªà¸´à¹ˆà¸‡à¹€à¸ˆà¸·à¸­à¸›à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¸„à¸³à¸™à¸§à¸“à¸¢à¸­à¸”à¸‚à¸²à¸¢à¸ˆà¸£à¸´à¸‡. This fixes the edit-form bug where a line saved as `à¸ˆà¸³à¸™à¸§à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸‚à¸²à¸¢à¹„à¸”à¹‰ 50`, `à¸«à¸±à¸à¸ªà¸´à¹ˆà¸‡à¹€à¸ˆà¸·à¸­à¸›à¸™ 1`, `à¸™à¹‰à¸³à¸«à¸™à¸±à¸à¸‚à¸²à¸¢à¸ªà¸¸à¸—à¸˜à¸´ 49` reopened as `49` because the UI was hydrating the form from `line.net_weight/qty` that were written with the wrong semantic mapping.

Sales Bill PO helper checkpoint on 2026-07-02: `/sales/bills` edit/create form now renders the PO Sell helper under the `à¸­à¹‰à¸²à¸‡à¸­à¸´à¸‡` field from the current Sales Bill row allocation (`item.qty`) instead of reusing the static option label hydrated from the locked PO Sell option. This keeps `à¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¹ƒà¸™à¸šà¸´à¸¥à¸™à¸µà¹‰` and `à¸„à¸‡à¹€à¸«à¸¥à¸·à¸­` in sync when the user changes `à¸ˆà¸³à¸™à¸§à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸‚à¸²à¸¢à¹„à¸”à¹‰`, `à¸«à¸±à¸à¸ªà¸´à¹ˆà¸‡à¹€à¸ˆà¸·à¸­à¸›à¸™`, or split rows before save. This is a UI display correction only; the server save path already allocates PO Sell from Sales Bill line quantity. Validation passed: targeted ESLint for `TransactionBillsPageClient.tsx`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, and `git diff --check`.

Sales Bill PO split rule clarification on 2026-07-02: the target UX for `/sales/bills` is now fixed more narrowly. If the user fills Sales Bill quantity first and then selects `PO Sell`, the form may auto split that WTO-backed line into `PO Sell` + `Spot Sale` immediately when the requested amount is greater than PO remaining. But if the user already selected `PO Sell` and later edits `à¸ˆà¸³à¸™à¸§à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸‚à¸²à¸¢à¹„à¸”à¹‰` or `à¸«à¸±à¸à¸ªà¸´à¹ˆà¸‡à¹€à¸ˆà¸·à¸­à¸›à¸™` until the line exceeds PO remaining, the UI must not split silently; that path requires explicit user acknowledgement (confirm) or a blocking validation error. The backend PO-over-remaining guard stays in place as the final integrity check and must not be removed.

Sales Bill PO split implementation checkpoint on 2026-07-02: `TransactionBillsPageClient` now follows the clarified rule above. Auto split still runs when the user chooses `PO Sell` for a WTO-backed line that already has a higher sales quantity than the PO can absorb. But editing `à¸ˆà¸³à¸™à¸§à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸‚à¸²à¸¢à¹„à¸”à¹‰` or `à¸«à¸±à¸à¸ªà¸´à¹ˆà¸‡à¹€à¸ˆà¸·à¸­à¸›à¸™` after a line is already tied to `PO Sell` no longer auto-splits silently; the form keeps the edited value, shows a field-level PO overflow error on that row, and blocks save before the request reaches the server guard.

Document lock/no-fallback checkpoint on 2026-07-01: tightened the visible edit policy for downstream-used documents. `WTO` already reads active Sales Bill usage from `sales_bill_source_allocations` and server-side `PUT /api/daily/weight-tickets/[id]` rejects edits once the delivery has been used in an active Sales Bill; the flow docs now record this as the intended audit rule. Sales Bill list responses now expose `canEdit` and `editLockedReason` from the same active Customer Receipt allocation count used by cancel policy, and `/sales/bills` hides the edit action when a bill has active `customer_receipt_allocations`. The receipt lock no longer counts legacy `receipts` as a runtime fallback; older data must be migrated/repaired into the Customer Receipt contract. The Sales Bill WTO write path also no longer falls back from missing WTO product summary/source data to the Sales Bill line SKU or header WTO number; missing source summary data now errors instead of saving a guessed allocation.

Sales Bill WTO source allocation checkpoint on 2026-07-01: tightened the `WTO -> SB` data contract for cases where the customer reclassifies part of a delivered item into another SKU at billing time. `sales_bill_lines` remains the source of truth for the SKU sold on the bill, while `sales_bill_source_allocations` now has durable FK `weight_ticket_product_summary_id` back to the original WTO product summary that supplied pending_out stock and COGS. New writes no longer store the Sales Bill line number in `source_line_no`; that field now represents only the original source document line when line-specific, otherwise `null`. The detail read model uses the durable summary FK for source/cost display instead of deriving source summary from the outward `deliverySummaryId` string. Migration `20260701133000_link_sales_bill_sources_to_wto_summaries.sql` backfills existing WTO source allocations and corrects historical `source_line_no` values. The Sales Bill edit modal now labels PO Sell options as `à¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¹ƒà¸™à¸šà¸´à¸¥à¸™à¸µà¹‰` plus separate current `à¸„à¸‡à¹€à¸«à¸¥à¸·à¸­`, and no longer warns `à¸‚à¸²à¸”` merely because the PO still has remaining quantity available for new split rows.

NSERP-125 Receipt Voucher print follow-up on 2026-07-01: account selection now persists the selected supplier bank account instead of forcing `payment_method = à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸ªà¸”` on save. The print template uses that persisted selected account only for the `à¹€à¸¥à¸‚à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µ / Bank Account` box, and falls back to the supplier's first active bank account for older RV records where the selected account was previously lost.
