WTO durable read-model optimization checkpoint on 2026-06-30: removed remaining sales-side WTO read paths that parsed `sales_bills.items` JSON for usage. `apps/next/src/lib/server/weight-tickets.ts` now reads active sales usage and downstream bill references from `sales_bill_source_allocations` (with bill-status guard) while timeline history continues to read `weight_ticket_usage_logs`. `/api/daily/weight-tickets` list no longer does one usage query per row; it batch-loads purchase/sales usage counts by `weight_ticket_id`, eliminating the N+1 pattern on the list page. `/api/sales/bills` WTO availability summary also now reads `sales_bill_source_allocations.meta.deliverySummaryId` instead of scanning bill item JSON. This batch changes read/query source only; WTO/SB write flow and business states are unchanged. Validation: `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`.
Sales Bill detail no-fallback checkpoint on 2026-06-30: removed the remaining Sales Bill detail fallback that built bill items from `sales_bills.items` JSON snapshot when durable `sales_bill_lines` facts were missing. `getSalesBillDetail()` now requires durable line facts and source/allocation facts as the only read model. If an older or malformed SB has no durable facts, `/api/sales/bills/[id]` returns an explicit contract error instead of silently reconstructing detail from snapshot JSON. `readModelWarning` is no longer used for this fallback path. Validation: `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`.

Pagination Implementation (NSERP-133) checkpoint on 2026-06-30: Implemented Pagination and navigation controls for all 6 target pages/tables. Added pagination buttons at the bottom of the Desktop table list in Weight Ticket List. Implemented client-side pagination (20 items per page) for PO, Bill, and Production tabs in Waiting Allocations and Allocation Ledger (50 items per page) in Dual Costing, resetting the page when filters are changed. Added bottom pagination controls matching backend pagination in AR/AP Detail tabs, and client-side pagination (50 items per page) in AR/AP Summary tabs with bottom controls on Desktop/Mobile. In Stock Operation, added client-side pagination (20 items per page) and top controls in Grade Adjustment (convert mode), and bottom controls for all modes (status-convert, adjust, convert) beneath the main table. Local type-checking, lint checks, and production build succeeded.
Product Tracking Annual Data (NSERP-132) checkpoint on 2026-06-30: Fixed a bug where the annual month-by-month compare table in Product Tracking (under the 'à¸£à¸²à¸¢à¸›à¸µ' tab) displayed empty data dashes ('-') for all products. The root cause was a key mismatch in the `/api/tracking/product` API endpoint: the accumulated monthly data map `productMonthlyMap` was populated using the numeric `product.id` (as a string) as the key, but the subsequent rows map retrieved it using `row.id` (which corresponds to `product.code`). Changed the map key during accumulation to `product.code` to resolve the mismatch. Local type-checking and Next.js production build verified successfully.

Sales Commission Dashboard (NSERP-112) checkpoint on 2026-06-30: Checked NSERP-112 and implemented the requirements from `main-dashboard-reports-sales-commission.md`. Removed the legacy "à¸œà¸¹à¸ Supplier à¸à¸±à¸šà¸žà¸™à¸±à¸à¸‡à¸²à¸™à¸‚à¸²à¸¢" section from the main page. Re-implemented the `/api/sales-commission` endpoint to accept query parameters `dateFrom`, `dateTo`, and `branchId`. Re-implemented `buildSalesCommission` in `main-sales-control.ts` to compute current period and annual totals, verify commission eligibility (salesperson `commission_eligible` is true, item price <= face price `sales_price`), and calculate commission using strict step tier rules (base >= 1M gives 1000, and +500 for each additional 500k strictly above). Re-implemented `SalesCommissionPageClient` component in `MainSalesControlClients.tsx` to feature: 1. Period and Branch filters; 2. 8 Summary Metrics Cards; 3. Salesperson Cards Grid; 4. "à¸ªà¸£à¸¸à¸›à¸¢à¸­à¸”à¸‹à¸·à¹‰à¸­à¸£à¸²à¸¢ Sales" pivot table by product category; and 5. Individual detail drilldown view featuring 4 verification tables with search, sort, and pagination. Validation: `npm run type-check` and `npm run build` both successfully passed.

Receipt Voucher, WTI/WTO, Mobile Nav & 360Â° Tracking Filters checkpoint on 2026-06-30: Fixed Receipt Voucher print template (`receipt-voucher-print.ts`) to selectively render only the chosen supplier bank account based on the selected `paymentMethod` when using bank transfer (also changing the total summary label from "à¸¢à¸­à¸”à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸ªà¸”" to "à¸¢à¸­à¸”à¸ªà¸¸à¸—à¸˜à¸´"), and falling back to "à¸ˆà¸³à¸™à¸§à¸™à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™ (à¸•à¸±à¸§à¸­à¸±à¸à¸©à¸£)" when paying in cash. Verified that Receipt Voucher note field correctly pre-populates from the selected purchase bill's note in the UI. Solved duplicate `draft` key in `statusLabels` map and cleaned up status validation type mismatch checks in `weight-tickets.ts`. Customized mobile bottom navigation (`MobileBottomNavigation.tsx`) to render exactly 6 menus (à¹€à¸¡à¸™à¸¹, à¸£à¸±à¸š-à¸ªà¹ˆà¸‡à¸‚à¸­à¸‡, à¹à¸”à¸Šà¸šà¸­à¸£à¹Œà¸”, à¸ªà¸±à¹ˆà¸‡à¸œà¸¥à¸´à¸•, à¸£à¸²à¸¢à¸‡à¸²à¸™, à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µ) using fluid flex-1 styling and smaller text size for clean presentation on smaller screens. Removed Product, Supplier, and Customer filters from 360Â° Tracking pages (`CustomerTrackingPageClient.tsx`, `SupplierTrackingPageClient.tsx`, and `ProductTrackingPageClient.tsx`) leaving only Year, Month, Category, and Export filters to declutter report views. Verification: `npm run type-check` and `npm run build` both successfully passed.

Sales Bill edit checkpoint on 2026-06-25: fixed `/sales/bills` edit modal so it no longer opens as read-only for all edits. The first supported correction slice mirrors Purchase Bill's allocation-edit pattern for changes that do not affect stock: line count, product, WTO source, and gross/deduct/net/qty must remain unchanged; users can change `Spot Sale <-> PO Sell`, price, discount, note, VAT/header totals, and export order. `PATCH /api/sales/bills` now rejects active RCP/customer-advance edits, marks old active `sales_bill_po_sell_allocations.status = reversed` (Sales Bill table allows `active/cancelled/reversed`, not PB-style `released`), creates new allocation rows, appends `po_sell_allocation_logs` release/allocate events, recalculates `po_sells.remaining_qty/remaining_amount/status`, and updates Sales Bill AR totals/balance without touching stock ledger or WTO pending_out. UI wording no longer says "à¸¥à¹‡à¸­à¸à¹ƒà¸šà¸ªà¹ˆà¸‡à¸‚à¸­à¸‡à¹€à¸”à¸´à¸¡"; edit mode now says it keeps the original business identity for audit continuity. After create or edit save, the list must reload with the user's current search/filter/page context and must not auto-fill the search box with the saved SB doc no. Validation: `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false` passed.
Historical reporting snapshot checkpoint on 2026-06-24: user asked to cover History/Snapshot for old Bill, old PO, finance, stock, and other dashboard data that must roll up from daily to monthly/yearly. Added `docs/notes/Reporting History Snapshot Policy.md` as the cross-domain contract: separate transaction facts, document snapshots, and reporting snapshots; group business metrics by `business_date/document_date`; use `as_of_date` for historical views; derive monthly/yearly from checked daily snapshots; and never fallback from missing historical facts to current-state balances. User then clarified stock must support real-time and historical views for both quantity and WAC/à¸•à¹‰à¸™à¸—à¸¸à¸™à¹€à¸‰à¸¥à¸µà¹ˆà¸¢; stock docs now require quantity, value, WAC, pending_out, and available to be calculated with the same cutoff, with historical WAC derived from ledger value/qty as-of instead of current WAC. Updated Dashboard/Finance/Stock docs and tasklist with remaining work: define daily snapshot schema/view plan for Bill/PO/Finance/Stock, source coverage matrix per card, asOf API contracts, Stock real-time/as-of API contract, backfill/rebuild scripts, reconciliation checks, and stale/incomplete snapshot warnings.
Sales Bill WTO return/loss checkpoint on 2026-06-25: the explicit `à¸£à¸±à¸šà¸‚à¸­à¸‡à¸„à¸·à¸™` action belongs on WTO detail, not Sales Bill detail. `/api/daily/weight-tickets/[id]/stock-returns` exposes active return candidates only when a WTO has already been allocated to an active SB and still has remaining `pending_out` after partial billing. The submit action still posts through `POST /api/sales/bills/{docNo}/stock-return` so the audit is linked to SB/WTO/product/warehouse. User must enter actual returned weight. Exact return releases the pending_out and decrements WTO remaining weight without writing stock ledger because that unsold qty was never stock-out in ledger. Short return requires a reason, splits/closes pending_out into returned + `lost`, appends `returned_from_sales_bill` and `loss_from_sales_bill` usage logs, and writes `stock_ledger.ref_type = WTO-RETURN-LOSS` for the loss qty/value using the current WAC of that stock bucket. Sales Bill detail remains a read-only bill detail surface for this action. Docs updated in Sales Bill flow, Stock Ledger DB/API design, Stock Ledger/Balance, and tasklist. Validation passed: `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next` (existing tracking-page warnings only), and `git diff --check`.
Stock Sale pending_out hardening checkpoint on 2026-06-25: confirmed the durable Sales Bill allocation tables already exist and are written on new SB create/cancel (`sales_bill_lines`, `sales_bill_source_allocations`, `sales_bill_po_sell_allocations`, `sales_bill_customer_advance_allocations`). This batch did not add fallback paths. Runtime/service/API business names now use `pending_out`; `stock_holds` remains only the technical DB table name. Removed the old service compatibility alias names so type-check fails if a caller still uses the old concept. Stock Balance detail payload now exposes `pendingOutKey` and UI wording says pending_out. SB cancel after WTO return/loss now checks real return/loss usage facts and will not reopen pending_out or increment WTO remaining summary again; it only posts `SB-CANCEL` stock ledger for the sold qty.
Sales Bill export order checkpoint on 2026-06-25: added target field `sales_bills.export_order_no` for foreign sales bills via migration `20260625113000_add_sales_bill_export_order_no.sql`, applied to dev-target and marked in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`. The `/sales/bills` create modal now shows `à¹€à¸¥à¸‚à¸—à¸µà¹ˆ order à¸ªà¹ˆà¸‡à¸­à¸­à¸` immediately after the read-only `à¸Šà¹ˆà¸­à¸‡à¸—à¸²à¸‡à¸‚à¸²à¸¢` only when the selected Customer has `marketScope = à¸•à¹ˆà¸²à¸‡à¸›à¸£à¸°à¹€à¸—à¸¨`. Client/API validation requires the field for foreign Customers and rejects it for domestic Customers; this is a new explicit column and does not reuse or fallback to `ref_no`. Detail and print surfaces show the export order number when present.
Sales Stock partial-billing checkpoint on 2026-06-24: updated `/sales/bills` Stock SB create flow so commercial sales quantity and stock consume quantity are separate. In the modal, `à¸™à¹‰à¸³à¸«à¸™à¸±à¸à¸ªà¸¸à¸—à¸˜à¸´à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸ªà¹ˆà¸‡` is the read-only WTO net after container deduction, `à¸ˆà¸³à¸™à¸§à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸‚à¸²à¸¢à¹„à¸”à¹‰` is the Customer-side accepted/weighed quantity, `à¸«à¸±à¸à¸ªà¸´à¹ˆà¸‡à¹€à¸ˆà¸·à¸­à¸›à¸™` is the Customer quality deduction, and `à¸™à¹‰à¸³à¸«à¸™à¸±à¸à¸‚à¸²à¸¢à¸ªà¸¸à¸—à¸˜à¸´ = à¸ˆà¸³à¸™à¸§à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸‚à¸²à¸¢à¹„à¸”à¹‰ - à¸«à¸±à¸à¸ªà¸´à¹ˆà¸‡à¹€à¸ˆà¸·à¸­à¸›à¸™`. API validation now allows Customer accepted weight to be less than or greater than the WTO sent weight; stock consume is capped by the active WTO `pending_out` quantity and saved as `stockIssueQty` in Sales Bill snapshots/allocation metadata. `consumeActiveWtoPendingOut` now supports partial consume by reducing the active pending_out and creating a consumed pending_out slice for the SB, so remaining `pending_out` stays active for the required `à¸£à¸±à¸šà¸‚à¸­à¸‡à¸„à¸·à¸™` action on WTO detail. SB cancel can reopen consumed pending_out slices only before return/loss facts exist; after return/loss, cancel returns stock directly through `SB-CANCEL` and must not recreate pending_out.
Finance/accounting menu summary checkpoint on 2026-06-24: user asked whether there is a document explaining every page under `à¸à¸²à¸£à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™ & à¸«à¸™à¸µà¹‰` and `Finance / Accounting`. Existing docs were `Finance Debt Flow` and `Finance Accounting Flow`, but there was no single per-menu summary and `Finance Accounting Flow` still missed the active `Accounting Periods` and `Posting Rules` policy pages. Added `docs/notes/Finance And Accounting Menu Summary.md` to summarize each page, source/write boundary, finance/stock/asset impact, and month/year close freeze direction. Updated `Finance Accounting Flow` to 21 active pages and recorded that `Accounting Periods`/`Posting Rules` are policy/readiness surfaces until runtime period-lock enforcement and snapshot schemas are implemented.
AR/AP docs-first checkpoint on 2026-06-24: user asked to update documents before implementation. Updated the finance contracts so `/finance/ar` no longer mentions Pending Sale / stock issue summary, AR/AP rows must read visible balances from `sales_bills.receivable_balance` / `received_amount` and `purchase_bills.payable_balance` / `paid_amount`, and allocation/payment/receipt facts are drilldown-only. `/finance/ap` now explicitly has no channel filter until purchase channel exists as a real document source, and this implementation batch must not add credit-term/due-date schema; AP aging remains based on `purchase_bills.date`. Tasklist now tracks the remaining code work separately: AR API source-of-truth, AP API source-of-truth, AR/AP drilldown, AP channel filter removal, and AR/AP UI alignment with `docs/design.md` / Peach.
AR/AP balance-source checkpoint on 2026-06-24: user asked to record what affects AR/AP and how each page must calculate it. Target rule is now documented as one contract: AR is created only when `Sales Bill (SB)` is saved, reduced by active `RCP` and Customer Advance allocation, and read from `sales_bills.receivable_balance` / `sales_bills.received_amount`; `PO Sell` and `WTO` do not create AR. AP is created only when `Purchase Bill (PB)` is saved, reduced by active `PMT` and Supplier Advance allocation, and read from `purchase_bills.payable_balance` / `purchase_bills.paid_amount`; `POB`, `WTI`, and `PMA` do not create or reduce AP. Receipt/payment/allocation tables are drilldown/audit facts, not the primary balance source. Docs updated in Finance AR/AP page flows plus Sales/Purchase flows, and tasklist now tracks API follow-up for `/api/finance/ar` and `/api/finance/ap`.
AR/RCP source-of-truth checkpoint on 2026-06-24: user asked when AR is created and whether current AR is correct. Confirmed target rule: AR is created when `Sales Bill (SB)` is saved, not when `RCP` is saved; `RCP` only reduces AR. `WTO` does not create AR. For read/report surfaces, `sales_bills.receivable_balance` and `sales_bills.received_amount` must be the primary AR balance fields, while `customer_receipt_allocations`, `customer_receipts`, and legacy `receipts` are drilldown/audit facts. Current `/api/finance/ar` still aggregates legacy `receipts` into a received map before falling back to `sales_bills.received_amount`, which can overstate AR when Customer Advance was allocated at SB creation. Docs and tasklist updated; implementation follow-up is to change `/api/finance/ar` to read `sales_bills` balance snapshots first and add RCP/Customer Advance allocation drilldown without deriving balance from legacy receipt rows.
AP/PMT source-of-truth checkpoint on 2026-06-24: user asked whether AP has the same issue as AR. Confirmed target rule: AP is created when `Purchase Bill (PB)` is saved, not when `PMT` is saved; `PMT` only reduces AP. `WTI` does not create AP and `PMA` is approval/queue, not payment. For read/report surfaces, `purchase_bills.payable_balance` and `purchase_bills.paid_amount` must be the primary AP balance fields, while `payments`, `payment_allocations`, `payment_approvals`, and `supplier_advance_allocations` are drilldown/audit facts. Current `/api/finance/ap` still aggregates non-cancelled `payments` into a paid map before falling back to `purchase_bills.paid_amount`, which can overstate AP when Supplier Advance was allocated to PB. Docs/tasklist updated; implementation follow-up is to change `/api/finance/ap` to read `purchase_bills` balance snapshots first and add PMT/ADV allocation drilldown without deriving balance from payment rows.
WTO/SB return-cancel costing checkpoint on 2026-06-24: user confirmed the target rule for `WTO -> SB -> à¸£à¸±à¸šà¸‚à¸­à¸‡à¸„à¸·à¸™ -> SB cancel`. If an SB is cancelled before any return-from-WTO/SB exists, cancellation may reopen the consumed `WTO pending_out` back to active. If return-from-WTO/SB already happened, cancellation must not reopen/recreate `pending_out`; the existing return/diff audit remains as posted, and `SB-CANCEL` returns stock directly for the SB-sold quantity using the original SB unit cost/value snapshot. Example: WTO sends 100, SB sells 50, actual return is 48 with diff/loss 2; later SB cancel returns 50 through `SB-CANCEL`, final stock restoration is 98 and diff/loss 2 stays as audit. Docs updated in Sales Bill flow, Sales flow, Stock Ledger design, Stock Balance design, WTI/WTO flow, and Sales Bill page-flow notes. Implementation follow-up: build the return-from-WTO/SB write path and make SB cancel branch on real return facts, with no fallback to PSALE or silent status inference.
Customer/Supplier branch eligibility planning checkpoint on 2026-06-24: user confirmed branch ownership must be visible for both Customer and Supplier so each branch can know which counterparties belong to it. Target contract is strict mapping tables, not a single branch field on master rows: `customer_branches` and `supplier_branches` own active branch eligibility, support primary branch, and must be the source of truth for transaction selectors/API validation. No fallback is allowed: unmapped Customer/Supplier must not appear in new branch-bound documents, and API writes must reject rather than treating unmapped rows as all-branch. Docs updated before implementation in target requirements, master Customer/Supplier page flows, WTI/WTO, Purchase/Sales canonical flows, PO Sell, Purchase Bill, Sales Bill, and the implementation tasklist. Next implementation batch should start with additive DB/Prisma migration and data audit/backfill report before tightening runtime selectors.
Customer/Supplier branch eligibility DB checkpoint on 2026-06-24: added migration `20260624203000_add_customer_supplier_branch_mappings.sql`, Prisma models `customer_branches` and `supplier_branches`, and `npm run audit:party-branch-mappings --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`. Migration was applied to dev-target and marked in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`. Audit passed structure checks (`tables = 2`, `indexes = 6`) but reported `active_customers_without_mapping = 51` and `active_suppliers_without_mapping = 1871`. Supplier backfill from legacy `suppliers.branch_id` inserted 0 rows because current dev data has no supplier branch id values to migrate. Strict runtime validation is now active after the master UI/import/export mapping editor was added, so those unmapped rows must be fixed through master UI/import/backfill before users can select them in new branch-bound documents.
Customer/Supplier branch eligibility API checkpoint on 2026-06-24: Customer/Supplier domain schemas and master data read APIs now expose `branchIds`, `branchNames`, `primaryBranchId`, and `primaryBranchName` from the mapping tables. Customer write API syncs mappings only when branch mapping payload fields are explicitly submitted, so existing Customer forms cannot accidentally clear mappings before the UI editor exists. Supplier write API syncs mappings from the existing `branchId` field plus the new multi-branch payload fields. Remaining work is the visible multi-branch editor/import/export/detail/table support and then strict transaction selector/API enforcement after mapping data is populated.
Customer/Supplier branch eligibility master UI checkpoint on 2026-06-24: Customer and Supplier master list/mobile cards now show allowed branches and mark unmapped rows as `à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸à¸³à¸«à¸™à¸”`. Create/edit forms now require at least one allowed branch and support choosing a primary branch from the selected branches; Supplier wording explicitly separates document branch eligibility from bank account branch code. Customer/Supplier export now includes `à¸£à¸«à¸±à¸ªà¸ªà¸²à¸‚à¸²à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¹„à¸”à¹‰`, `à¸ªà¸²à¸‚à¸²à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¹„à¸”à¹‰`, `à¸£à¸«à¸±à¸ªà¸ªà¸²à¸‚à¸²à¸«à¸¥à¸±à¸`, and `à¸ªà¸²à¸‚à¸²à¸«à¸¥à¸±à¸`. Customer/Supplier import now accepts those columns and rejects active rows with no branch mapping or invalid/inactive branch codes; it does not infer branch ownership from name/address/legacy branch fields. Next batch should populate/fix the 51 Customer and 1871 Supplier unmapped rows through master UI/import/backfill before transaction selectors/API are tightened.
Customer/Supplier branch eligibility runtime checkpoint on 2026-06-24: added shared server helpers for `customer_branches` / `supplier_branches` eligibility and tightened branch-bound runtime without fallback. WTI/WTO options are filtered by selected branch and POST/PUT reject unmapped Supplier/Customer. PO Buy, Purchase Bill, Supplier Advance, PO Sell, and Sales Bill selectors include mapping `branchIds`, clear invalid selections on branch change, and reject writes when the selected party is not active for the document branch. AP/AR report filters also use mapping `branchIds` when a branch filter is selected. Audit service checks confirm unmapped Customer/Supplier are rejected; mapped-accepted checks are `null` only because dev-target currently has no mapped active party rows.
Customer/Supplier branch eligibility data checkpoint on 2026-06-24: per user instruction, dev-target active Customer/Supplier rows that still had no active branch mapping were initially assigned to branch `01` / `à¸ªà¸¡à¸¸à¸—à¸£à¸ªà¸²à¸„à¸£` so the strict selector/API flow can be used immediately. Inserted/activated mappings: `51` Customers and `1871` Suppliers, all marked active and primary for à¸ªà¸¡à¸¸à¸—à¸£à¸ªà¸²à¸„à¸£ with actor `codex:default-samut-sakhon-branch-mapping`. Branch ownership can now be corrected per party from Customer/Supplier master data.
WTI/WTO impurity option checkpoint on 2026-06-24: user confirmed `à¸ªà¸´à¸™à¸„à¹‰à¸²à¸­à¸·à¹ˆà¸™` must not come from master impurity data and must not fallback to legacy master rows. Runtime now treats `à¸ªà¸´à¸™à¸„à¹‰à¸²à¸­à¸·à¹ˆà¸™` as a WTI-only system option with id `__OTHER_PRODUCT__`; the WTI modal injects it from code, filters master rows named `à¸ªà¸´à¸™à¸„à¹‰à¸²à¸­à¸·à¹ˆà¸™`/`à¸­à¸·à¹ˆà¸™à¹†`/`à¸­à¸¢à¹ˆà¸²à¸‡à¸­à¸·à¹ˆà¸™` out of impurity options, and WTO never shows it. Create/update APIs reject WTO payloads that use the system option and reject payloads that try to use master impurity rows for `à¸ªà¸´à¸™à¸„à¹‰à¸²à¸­à¸·à¹ˆà¸™`; new writes store the special option as system-owned impurity name with no master `impurity_id`. Read runtime also does not translate historical/master `à¸ªà¸´à¸™à¸„à¹‰à¸²à¸­à¸·à¹ˆà¸™` rows back into the system option; old bad rows require data repair/master deactivation instead of runtime fallback.
WTO pending-out cleanup checkpoint on 2026-06-24: continued removal of the old Pending Sale / PSALE runtime flow after the target decision that stock sale must be `WTO pending_out -> Sales Bill -> SB ledger`. Sales Bill validation no longer exposes `pendingStockIssueId`, `fromPsaleNo`, or `fromPsaleId`; the Sales Bill API no longer has a PSALE payload guard or PSALE cancel/data-repair reject path and now follows the target Sales Bill schema/WTO usage path only. Active dashboard/finance links that previously opened `/sales/stock-issue` now point to `/sales/bills` and use `WTO pending out` wording, Trading Matching flow copy now reads `PO Sell -> WTO -> Sales Bill Trading`, and Stock Ledger no longer links legacy `PSALE` rows to the removed route. The active route file `apps/next/src/app/sales/stock-issue/page.tsx` and API file `apps/next/src/app/api/sales/stock-issue/route.ts` were deleted, so `/sales/stock-issue` and `/api/sales/stock-issue` are no longer active app routes. The shared transaction list component `TransactionBillsPageClient` now accepts only `purchase | sales`; the unreachable `mode="stock-issue"` type/state/API/form/modal/table/action branches were removed from that component.

Pending Sales report removal checkpoint on 2026-06-24: user confirmed no fallback should remain. Removed the active `/pending-sales` page, `GET /api/pending-sales`, menu/permission/report-index links, the `PendingSalesPageClient`, and the page-flow doc. Removed runtime reads of `stock_issues`/PSALE/pending issue fallback from dashboard, AR, Cash & Others, Finance Accounting dashboard, sales bill detail, and sales-line COGS facts. `Sales Plan` now keeps only an internal sales-planning snapshot sourced from stock/SB/WTO/PO data and no public Pending Sales API/report surface remains.
Pending Sales metadata cleanup checkpoint on 2026-06-24: removed the stale active `/pending-sales`, `/api/pending-sales`, `/sales/stock-issue`, and `/api/sales/stock-issue` entries from OpenAPI, navigation CSV, page inventory, and system sitemap metadata. Updated AR/Cash & Others report flow docs so they no longer describe a pending sale / pending stock issue banner or source table. QA/browser work remains intentionally skipped per user instruction.
Sales WTO/SB timeline checkpoint on 2026-06-24: cleaned `docs/migration/09-implementation-tasklist.md` so PSALE and `/sales/stock-issue` are no longer listed as target-complete runtime work; target source is now `WTO -> SB` only. Added `po_sell_allocation_logs` as an append-only audit table/model plus runtime logging for `allocated_to_sales_bill` on SB create and `released_from_sales_bill` on SB cancel. Sales Bill detail now reads `weight_ticket_usage_logs`, `po_sell_allocation_logs`, and `sales_bill_status_logs` directly for source/timeline facts instead of relying on status strings alone. Sales docs now record the user-confirmed COGS rule: Stock SB uses weighted average cost at the time of sale, captured in the posted `SB` stock ledger cost.
WTI/WTO LINE notification checkpoint on 2026-06-23: started the `/daily/weight-ticket-list` LINE Messaging API implementation. The list `à¹à¸Šà¸£à¹Œ` action now opens a choice dialog for sending a generated PDF Flex Message to the configured default LINE target or falling back to manual LINE share. Added server PDF generation with Noto Sans Thai, Supabase Storage upload, LINE push API route, LINE webhook signature verification route, `weight_ticket_notification_logs` migration, `weight-ticket-pdfs` bucket migration, and env placeholders for LINE/Supabase/public app URL setup. Auto-send after create remains off until LINE target config is verified.
WTI LINE image UAT repair checkpoint on 2026-07-01: investigated customer UAT reports that sharing a WTI to LINE sent without the expected images. Root cause was environment/storage contract drift: earlier UAT notification logs stored PDF URLs on the dev Supabase host because the UAT deployment still had dev Storage env at send time, and the customer UAT Supabase project did not yet have a usable `weight-ticket-pdfs` bucket. The bucket contract also only allowed `application/pdf` while the current LINE flow uploads PDF, generated album JPEGs, and WTI/WTO source images as PNG/JPEG. Added migration `20260701070000_allow_weight_ticket_line_image_storage.sql`, updated the admin LINE setup helper, applied the bucket repair directly to UAT, and verified public reads for PNG and PDF on `oolzfvqhovmjhiocfqdw.supabase.co`. The business rule remains: WTI/WTO LINE share stores generated PDF and image snapshots in public Supabase Storage so LINE can fetch them without user authentication.
Sales/WTO detail cost snapshot checkpoint on 2026-07-01: fixed detail read models after user found `SB2607-0005` Source usage facts and `WTO012607-0005` detail showing blank cost values. Root cause was read-model-only: Sales Bill detail mapped WTO usage log amount to `0` even though the posted `SB` stock ledger already held COGS, and WTO detail calculated product cost only from `active` stock holds even after a billed WTO consumes the hold. The target flow is unchanged: WTO confirmation locks `stock_holds.unit_cost_snapshot/value_snapshot`; Sales Bill posting writes `SB` stock ledger `value_out`; detail pages must read those durable facts even after pending_out has been consumed. The read model now derives WTO usage COGS from the existing sales line stock COGS/ledger facts and keeps WTO average cost visible from active or consumed hold snapshots without inventing fallback cost.
Last pushed checkpoint: Merge `peach` UI updates into `dev` and prepare dev sync (local merge in progress on 2026-06-13)
Stock transfer modal UX follow-up on 2026-06-13: per user clarification, `/stock/transfer` create/edit modal no longer shows document number or date fields; document number remains server-generated and date is kept automatic in the form payload. Source/destination warehouse selects are disabled until the matching branch is selected, and changing branch clears the selected warehouse. Product line selection now uses a searchable combobox over active products, searchable by product code or name, while still submitting the validated product id/code to the API. Docs updated in Stock Transfer page-flow notes. Validation passed: targeted ESLint for `StockTransferPageClient.tsx`, `type-check`, `git diff --check`, and authenticated Playwright UI QA confirmed the modal hides doc/date, both warehouse selects are disabled before branch selection, and searching `SKU` in the product combobox returns options.
Sales bill detail click follow-up on 2026-06-13: fixed `/sales/bills` row/card click behavior in the shared transaction bill client. Root cause was the shared `openRow()` handler returning for every mode except `purchase`, while `/sales/bills` still wired its rows to that handler. The sales path now loads `GET /api/sales/bills/{docNo}` into a dedicated sales detail state and opens a Sales Bill detail modal with document, customer, source WTO/POS, item, VAT, receipt, and print actions. Existing purchase bill detail behavior remains on the purchase-only modal path. Validation passed: `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next` with only the existing unrelated Products `<img>` warning, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`.
Stock balance WTO/SB bucket repair on 2026-06-13: investigated user-reported `/stock/balance` mismatch for `POS6906-0001` / `SB2606-0001`, where Stock Balance showed `SKU001` as `RM +110` plus a separate `- -5` row instead of `RM 105`. Root cause was not warehouse loss: `WTO012606-0001` stores warehouse at line/hold level (`RM-SK`) and `SB2606-0001` consumes that hold, but the generated `SB` ledger row did not carry `output_category`, so the stock-out aggregated into the `-` bucket. Added migration [supabase/migrations/20260613084801_add_stock_hold_bucket_fields.sql](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/supabase/migrations/20260613084801_add_stock_hold_bucket_fields.sql) to add bucket fields to `stock_holds`, backfill existing holds from warehouse type, repair resolvable `SB`/`PSALE` ledger rows consumed from holds, and rebuild the hold lookup index with `output_category`. Runtime now records hold bucket fields when creating WTO holds, validates/allocates from saleable bucket matching the selected warehouse type, and writes `SB`/`PSALE` ledger rows with hold `output_category`, `lot_no`, and `not_available_for_sale`. Dev-target apply verified `SB2606-0001` now has `output_category = RM` and `SKU001 / RM-SK / RM` aggregates to `105` kg. Validation passed: Prisma generate/validate, `type-check`, `verify:stock-ledger` with `.env.local`, `lint` with only the existing Products `<img>` warning, `build`, and `git diff --check`.
Stock convert allocation detail/export checkpoint on 2026-06-13: `/api/stock/convert` now supports `GET ?detail=<docNo>` for Grade Adjustment cost allocation drilldown and `GET ?detail=<docNo>&format=csv` for per-document allocation export from the same server contract. `/stock/convert` list now has a Detail action that opens source/target Cost Pool line detail with qty, unit cost, total cost, allocation status, and CSV export. Authenticated local QA passed on `GA-000002`: modal opened, detail API returned 200 with 1 allocation line, CSV returned `text/csv` and included the doc no, with no browser console errors. Validation passed: targeted ESLint, full type-check, full build, full lint with only the existing unrelated Products `<img>` warning, and `git diff --check`.
Stock convert cost policy checkpoint on 2026-06-13: added migration [supabase/migrations/20260613193000_add_stock_convert_cost_policy.sql](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/supabase/migrations/20260613193000_add_stock_convert_cost_policy.sql), applied it to dev-target, and marked migration history. `grade_adjustments` now stores `target_cost_policy`, `source_unit_cost`, `target_unit_cost`, `cost_variance`, and `cost_override_reason` with DB checks. Runtime policy is no-fallback: new POST rejects insufficient Cost Pool instead of creating pending-cost documents; `pending_cost` remains reserved for legacy/import/read-model rows. `partial` now means a fully costed GA where at least one selected source Cost Pool lot remains `Partially Used` after allocation. Target custom cost is admin/owner only, requires unit cost > 0 and reason, writes target ledger/pool at the custom unit cost, and preserves source allocation costs plus variance audit. Authenticated API e2e passed with `GA-QA-CUSTOM-1781342922809`: POST custom cost 200, detail showed `CUSTOM_UNIT_COST`, source unit cost `45.90909090909091`, target unit cost `46.90909090909091`, variance `0.5`, reason persisted, then reverse 200 and allocation status changed to `reversed`.
Stock transfer API/DB optimization closeout on 2026-06-13: finished the hardening pass for `/stock/transfer` after the initial source-document implementation. Posted transfer now allocates stock from source buckets on the server and writes one or more paired `ST` ledger rows preserving source `lot_no`, stock status/output category, source unit cost, and value; UI still keeps Lot manual input removed per latest requirement. API doc-no filtering was narrowed to prefix search and migration [supabase/migrations/20260613152000_add_stock_transfer_source_documents.sql](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/supabase/migrations/20260613152000_add_stock_transfer_source_documents.sql) now includes `idx_stock_transfers_doc_no_pattern` in addition to source document and ST ledger lookup indexes. Dev-target verification passed: `verify:stock-transfer` returned `{"checked":{"indexes":7,"tables":2},"ok":true}`, `verify:stock-ledger` returned `ok: true` with stock reconciliation totals all `0`, and EXPLAIN with `enable_seqscan=off` showed doc prefix reads using `idx_stock_transfers_doc_no_pattern`, date list reads using `idx_stock_transfers_date_doc`, and ST source lookup using `idx_stock_ledger_st_source_lookup`. Full validation passed: Prisma generate/validate, `type-check`, `lint` with only the unrelated Products `<img>` warning, `build`, and `git diff --check`. Local dev server was restored in tmux session `ns-scrap-next`; unauthenticated smoke confirmed `/stock/transfer -> /login?redirect=%2Fstock%2Ftransfer` and `/api/stock/transfer?pageSize=10 -> 401`. Remaining follow-up: none for posted cancel; posted ST remains immutable and corrections must be entered as a new opposite-direction transfer.
Stock transfer logged-in browser QA closeout on 2026-06-13: authenticated QA was completed locally against `http://localhost:3000/stock/transfer` using a temporary Supabase Auth admin user and dev-target data only. The run created draft `ST2606-0013`, edited it, cancelled it without ledger rows, then posted `ST2606-0014`; posted ledger verification returned 4 paired `ST` rows from two source buckets with `qty_in = qty_out = 60`, `value_in = value_out = 7000`, two preserved lots, and positive unit cost. Negative API checks confirmed posted edit and posted cancel return `400`. The QA exposed a real route bug in `nextStockTransferDocNo`: `select pg_advisory_xact_lock(...)` used Prisma `$queryRaw`, causing `P2010 Failed to deserialize column of type 'void'`; `/api/stock/transfer` now uses `$executeRaw` for that advisory lock. QA stock setup and posted test balances were offset with append-only `QA-STOCK-CLEANUP` ledger rows, and the post-check found no remaining `QAST%`/`ST2606-%` stock-balance residue. Re-validation after the fix passed: `type-check`, `lint` with only the existing unrelated Products `<img>` warning, `build`, `verify:stock-transfer`, and `verify:stock-ledger`. Local dev server is running again in tmux session `ns-scrap-next`. Posted cancel/reversal is intentionally not planned; posted ST remains immutable and corrections must be entered as a new opposite-direction transfer.
Stock convert runtime checkpoint on 2026-06-13: implemented the latest annotated `/stock/convert` / Grade Adjustment reference as a Cost Pool-backed runtime slice. New migration [supabase/migrations/20260613170000_add_stock_convert_cost_pool_allocation.sql](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/supabase/migrations/20260613170000_add_stock_convert_cost_pool_allocation.sql) extends `grade_adjustments`, adds `stock_cost_pool_entries` and `stock_cost_pool_allocations`, adds available/allocation indexes, and backfills current stock balance into the initial Grade Adjustment cost pool. Migration was applied to dev-target, marked in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`, and inserted 4 initial pool rows. `/api/stock/convert` now lists `reference.costPoolEntries`, rejects when Cost Pool/ready stock is insufficient, supports FIFO/LIFO/Highest Cost/Lowest Cost/Manual allocation without fallback/hard-code cost, writes paired `GA` stock ledger rows, consumes source pool rows, creates target `Regrade` pool rows, writes allocation rows, and supports `PATCH action=reverse` with append-only `GA-REV` stock ledger rows while blocking reverse if target pool/stock was used. `/stock/convert` UI now has allocation method, Cost Pool lot preview/manual selection, source type filters for auto/manual/legacy, and reverse action. Reverse path was hardened to calculate target ready qty inside the same transaction instead of calling the global Prisma client from inside `$transaction`, after authenticated QA exposed a transaction-timeout `DATABASE_ERROR`. Authenticated browser smoke with Supabase SSR cookie passed on local dev server: `/stock/convert` rendered, `GET /api/stock/convert` returned `200` with `costPoolCount = 4`, modal exposed Cost Pool Lots, FIFO, Manual, and Post button, and console errors were empty. API e2e create/reverse passed with `GA-000002`: post set source pool id `1` to `Partially Used`, reverse marked GA/allocation `reversed`, restored source pool to `1|20|0|0|Available`, released target pool, and wrote 2 `GA` plus 2 `GA-REV` ledger rows. Docs updated in [docs/notes/Stock Convert Page Flow.md](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/docs/notes/Stock%20Convert%20Page%20Flow.md) and [docs/notes/page-flows/stock-stock-convert.md](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/docs/notes/page-flows/stock-stock-convert.md). Runtime PSALE/production stock-ledger service QA checkpoint on 2026-06-12: `apps/next/scripts/verify-stock-ledger-contract.ts` now gates both stock and production reconciliation. In addition to the stock reconciliation groups, it fails on production reconciliation issues, reversed `PI`/`PO2` rows whose `PI-REV`/`PO2-REV` ledger rows are missing or do not net back to zero, and duplicate production reversal document numbers reused across different source docs. New `npm run qa:stock-ledger-write-paths --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next` adds repeatable dev-target write-path QA without hardcoded branch/product/warehouse master codes: it resolves a valid production scenario from current stock/master data, creates and cancels a PSALE from an active WTO hold, creates PI/PO2 production movement, reverses PO2 then PI, and verifies reconciliation remains clean. The first repeated service QA exposed a production doc-number bug: `PI-REV`/`PO2-REV` generation scanned `doc_no` even though reversal numbers are stored in `reversal_doc_no`, so repeated reversals could reuse `...0001`. Runtime generation now scans `reversal_doc_no` for `*-REV` prefixes, and migration [supabase/migrations/20260612233500_repair_duplicate_production_reversal_doc_numbers.sql](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/supabase/migrations/20260612233500_repair_duplicate_production_reversal_doc_numbers.sql) generically renumbers duplicate dev-target reversal groups and updates matching `stock_ledger.ref_no` plus production status-log metadata. The migration was applied directly to dev-target and marked in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`. Final service QA after the fix passed: `PSALE2606-0006` from `WTO012606-0005` wrote `PSALE` + `PSALE-CANCEL` net zero and reopened the hold; production QA created `PO2606-0024`, `PI2606-0023`, `PO22606-0008`, `PO2-REV2606-0003`, and `PI-REV2606-0003` with all expected `PI/PI-REV/PO2/PO2-REV` ledger ref types present. `npm run verify:stock-ledger` then returned all stock totals `0` and production `issueCount = 0`. Production build passed. Browser QA was attempted against `http://127.0.0.1:3005`, but the Playwright transport closed before navigation; unauthenticated HTTP smoke confirmed `/stock/reconciliation`, `/production/reconciliation`, and `/sales/stock-issue` redirect to login as expected. Remaining stock-ledger runtime follow-up is logged-in browser QA evidence for PSALE UI convert/cancel and any required UI rerun for the exact production write-path batch; PSALE edit remains policy-open and should stay cancel/recreate unless product explicitly requires in-place edit semantics.
PSALE edit policy update on 2026-06-12: user confirmed PSALE can be edited while it has not been pulled into another document. The implemented policy is now: edit only when `stock_issues.status = pending` and `converted_to_bill_id` is empty; keep the same PSALE doc no and WTO source; changing WTO source requires cancel/recreate. `/api/sales/stock-issue` now supports `PATCH action = edit`: it reverses only currently unreversed `PSALE` ledger rows with appended `PSALE-CANCEL`, reopens the WTO hold, consumes it again under the same PSALE doc no, updates item/date/note/estimated amount snapshots, and appends `stock_issue_status_logs.action = edited`. `/sales/stock-issue` UI now enables the edit button for pending PSALE only, opens the existing form with row snapshot values, locks the WTO selector during edit, and submits `PATCH action = edit`. Service QA now covers create -> edit -> cancel for PSALE; the latest dev-target run created `PSALE2606-0007`, edited it append-only, cancelled it, and `npm run verify:stock-ledger` returned all stock totals `0` with production `issueCount = 0`. Production build passed. Browser smoke on `127.0.0.1:3005` reached the expected unauthenticated guard (`/sales/stock-issue` redirects to login and `/api/sales/stock-issue` returns 401); logged-in browser click QA for the PSALE edit modal remains optional follow-up because service/API invariants are already covered.
Runtime Purchase Bills API/DB optimization checkpoint on 2026-06-12: reviewed `/api/purchase/bills` list/create/options paths and dev-target query plans. New migration [supabase/migrations/20260612225500_optimize_purchase_bills_queries.sql](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/supabase/migrations/20260612225500_optimize_purchase_bills_queries.sql) adds targeted indexes for PB list/sort/filter, PB doc-no prefix lookup, WTI options, ADV options, active PMT-by-bill lookup, locked PMA-by-source lookup, and active allocation lookup. The migration was applied directly to dev-target through the active Next `DATABASE_URL` and marked in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`. `/api/purchase/bills` now uses DB `skip/take`, `count`, and `sum(total_amount)` for list reads when the request does not filter/sort by derived workflow status; status-filtered reads still derive PMA/PMT workflow in memory to preserve current response semantics. WTI options are now restricted by the caller's branch scope. PB doc number generation now uses a month-scoped advisory lock plus SQL `max(running)` instead of a global doc-no lock and JS scan. Validation passed: `npm run prisma:generate --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, targeted ESLint for `src/app/api/purchase/bills/route.ts`, scoped TypeScript diagnostics showed no PB/schema-related errors, doc-no SQL smoke returned `last_number = 12`, and migration/index verification passed on dev-target.

Authenticated browser QA on local Next dev server `127.0.0.1:3004` passed: login, `GET /api/production/reconciliation = 200`, `issueCount=0`, page rendered `/production/reconciliation`, empty state showed no issue, desktop overflow check passed, and browser console errors were none. The edit policy is explicit cancel-and-reissue, not silent in-place mutation: submitting an existing RCP from the UI or `PATCH /api/sales/receipts` with `action = replace` cancels the old RCP, writes the reversing `RCP-CANCEL` bank statement and AR/SB restoration, validates replacement lines against restored SB balances, then creates a new RCP in the same transaction with status logs linking the replacement to the old doc no. Runtime RV manual checkpoint on 2026-06-12: user confirmed RV should follow manual behavior, not PMT auto-generation, and the modal should use Supplier selection as the primary receiver pre-fill source while PB remains an optional item/amount pre-fill source. `/api/purchase/receipt-vouchers` now supports `GET` list + Company Profile + active Supplier options + active PB options with item snapshots, `POST` manual create with generated `RVYYMM-NNNN`, and `PATCH` edit of printable snapshot fields. `ReceiptVouchersPageClient` now opens create/edit modal, can choose Supplier to pre-fill receiver name/tax/address/phone/signer, can choose PB filtered by Supplier to pre-fill items/amount/date/vehicle/note, preserves item unit in form/print, and keeps RV as cash-only Supplier printable evidence. `POST /api/purchase/payments` still validates active payment method but no longer creates/updates/deletes `receipt_vouchers`; PMT remains owner of payment/BST/AP settlement. The RV print preview now uses a compact A4 portrait template close to legacy print density rather than the earlier card/block layout: Company Profile header, centered title, date/doc no/reference text, receiver/company payer text blocks, item table, unit-separated quantity summary, amount-in-words line, signer lines, and cash-only evidence note. The API now returns `companyProfile.logoUrl` for this print surface. RV print remains cash-only Supplier evidence and still reads document/PB snapshots only.
Docs RV API-spec checkpoint on 2026-06-12: expanded [docs/notes/Receipt Voucher Page Flow.md](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/docs/notes/Receipt%20Voucher%20Page%20Flow.md) with the concrete current API contract for `GET/POST/PATCH /api/purchase/receipt-vouchers`. Runtime RV server-snapshot hardening checkpoint on 2026-06-12: `POST/PATCH /api/purchase/receipt-vouchers` now re-read selected PB inside the write transaction when `purchaseBillDocNo` is present and copy `supplier_*_snapshot`, `license_plate`, and `purchase_bill_items` server-side. PB-backed RV saves ignore client-submitted `seller*`, `salesPerson`, `licensePlate`, and `items` values, so direct API calls cannot forge or accidentally blank data that differs from the PB snapshot. This remains a no-fallback rule: if PB snapshot fields are blank because the PB/master data was old or incomplete, the RV saves blank fields instead of reading live Supplier master or alternate PB fields. Updated [docs/notes/Receipt Voucher Page Flow.md](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/docs/notes/Receipt%20Voucher%20Page%20Flow.md) with the server-side enforcement behavior.

Docs checkpoint on 2026-06-12: user clarified `/sales/stock-issue` as `Pending Sale Release / à¹€à¸šà¸´à¸à¸­à¸­à¸à¸£à¸­à¸šà¸´à¸¥` with stricter business rules: this menu is used to issue stock to a customer before the real Sales Bill, must come after outbound weighing/WTO, creates `PSALE` stock-out immediately when items are linked, starts in `pending`, changes to converted/open-billed after linked to Sales Bill, and must block issue quantities above available stock. Updated [docs/notes/Pending Sale Page Flow.md](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/docs/notes/Pending%20Sale%20Page%20Flow.md), [docs/notes/page-flows/daily-transactions-sales-stock-issue.md](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/docs/notes/page-flows/daily-transactions-sales-stock-issue.md), [docs/notes/Stock Ledger DB API Design.md](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/docs/notes/Stock%20Ledger%20DB%20API%20Design.md), and [REQUIREMENTS_TARGET_SYSTEM.md](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/REQUIREMENTS_TARGET_SYSTEM.md). Target contract now keeps reservation/hold ownership at `WTO`; `PSALE` itself is a movement fact that writes `stock_ledger.ref_type = PSALE` immediately and must consume/release the related WTO hold so stock is not counted as both reserved and issued. Convert-to-SB links the SB to PSALE and must not create duplicate stock-out rows. Runtime implementation remains pending.

Runtime weight-ticket options contract checkpoint on 2026-06-11: `/daily/weight-tickets` must not load master product option data through `/api/master-data/products?all=1` because that route returns full product image payloads for the product master page. The target contract is now page-scoped: `/api/daily/weight-tickets/options` returns lightweight header options (branch/supplier/customer/impurity) under the WTI/WTO permission flow, then `/api/daily/weight-tickets/products` preloads product choices in the background immediately after options succeed, without waiting for the user to choose header fields. That same `/products` route now returns the product thumbnail inline with each product row, so the client no longer makes a separate image request. The product picker can load and show product images before branch and supplier/customer are chosen; branch + party are still required by validation before save so the weighing document remains tied to the correct business context. This keeps the user-required product pictures without calling the heavy master product list on page bootstrap. Follow-up remains to move product images from DB/base64 into Storage with generated thumbnail URLs.
Runtime product-image storage checkpoint on 2026-06-11: `/master-data/products` and the shared WTI/WTO product picker now cut over to `products.image_storage_key` with no runtime fallback to old `products.image_names`. Migration `20260611050202_add_product_storage_image_key.sql` was applied directly to `dev-target`; it adds `products.image_storage_key`, creates public bucket `product-images`, and adds authenticated insert/update/delete policies on `storage.objects`. The product modal now saves master-data fields first, uploads the selected image to Storage through the browser-authenticated Supabase client, then persists the storage key back to the product row and best-effort removes the replaced file. `/api/master-data/products` and `/api/daily/weight-tickets/products` now emit `thumbnailUrl` derived from the storage key, so both WTI and WTO product pickers read the same image contract without a separate image API. Validation passed with `npm run prisma:generate --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`. Remaining follow-up is cleanup/removal of legacy `products.image_names` data.
Runtime product-image original+thumb checkpoint on 2026-06-11: the product-image flow was hardened from `original only + runtime transform` to explicit `original + thumbnail` objects in Storage. Migration `20260611052713_add_product_image_thumbnail_storage_key.sql` was applied directly to `dev-target`; it adds `products.image_thumbnail_storage_key` beside `image_storage_key`. The product modal now resizes/compresses the selected image in the browser into two `.webp` files before upload: optimized original (`max 1600px`) and thumbnail (`320x320`). Both files are uploaded to `product-images`, then both storage keys are persisted on the product row; if upload/save fails after partial upload, the newly uploaded files are removed best-effort to avoid new orphans. `/api/master-data/products` and `/api/daily/weight-tickets/products` now serve `thumbnailUrl` from the stored thumb object directly instead of runtime transform, so WTI and WTO product pickers read the physical thumbnail file.
Runtime legacy product-image backfill checkpoint on 2026-06-11: legacy `products.image_names` rows were backfilled into the new Storage contract using one-off script [apps/next/scripts/backfill-product-images.mjs](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/apps/next/scripts/backfill-product-images.mjs). The script decoded the first legacy image payload per product, generated optimized original + thumbnail files locally with `sips`, uploaded both to bucket `product-images` through `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, and updated `products.image_storage_key` / `products.image_thumbnail_storage_key` for 62 products with `success=62, failed=0`. On this macOS host, `sips` could not write `webp`, so the backfilled files were written as `jpeg`; runtime does not depend on file extension and continues to read thumb URLs from stored keys normally. Legacy `image_names` source data was intentionally retained for now; cleanup/removal stays as a separate follow-up after user verification.
Docs checkpoint on 2026-06-11: WTI/WTO page flow is now split into its own canonical note at [docs/notes/WTI-WTO Flow.md](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/docs/notes/WTI-WTO%20Flow.md). The note owns `/daily/weight-tickets` and `/daily/weight-ticket-list` behavior: create/edit/list/detail/timeline/print/share, page-scoped options/products APIs, product thumbnail preload contract, image responsibilities, WTI/WTO statuses, and handoff boundaries into Purchase Flow (`WTI -> PB`) and Sales Flow (`WTO -> SB`).
Docs checkpoint on 2026-06-11: WTI/WTO and Sales Bill flow docs were clarified further before implementation. Superseded by the later bill-driven + hold decision on the same date: `WTO` records line-level intended warehouse and creates active stock hold/reservation in `/daily/weight-tickets`, but it must not cut stock ledger on save. Stock availability must display `à¸„à¸‡à¹€à¸«à¸¥à¸·à¸­à¸ˆà¸£à¸´à¸‡ / à¸ˆà¸­à¸‡à¹„à¸§à¹‰ / à¸žà¸£à¹‰à¸­à¸¡à¸ªà¹ˆà¸‡` and validate from `à¸žà¸£à¹‰à¸­à¸¡à¸ªà¹ˆà¸‡ = on_hand - active_holds`. The `billing / usage / stock-out loop` is owned by `/sales/bills`; `SB Stock` must consume the WTO hold and write `stock_ledger.ref_type = SB` in the sales-bill transaction. This is a docs-only checkpoint; no application code changed yet in this follow-up.
Docs checkpoint on 2026-06-11: WTI/WTO doc was refined again before implementation. Target business rule is now explicit that both `WTI` and `WTO` are `all-or-nothing` billing documents in the new write path: one ticket must be fully consumed in one downstream bill (`WTI -> PB`, `WTO -> SB`) with no target partial-billed state. The same note now also separates `document status` from `mutability/lock state`, and defines `WTO` stock reverse rules for edit/cancel (`edit = reverse + rebuild`, `cancel = reverse only`, both blocked once used in SB). This remains docs-only; no application code changed in this follow-up.
Docs checkpoint on 2026-06-11: `docs/notes/Sales Bills Page Flow.md` was aligned to the finalized outbound rule set. `STOCK` sales-bill create flow now documents `WTO 1 à¹ƒà¸š -> SB 1 à¹ƒà¸š` explicitly, removes wording that implied one SB can pick multiple WTOs, and changes the source eligibility wording from `à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¸­à¸­à¸à¸šà¸´à¸¥à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸„à¸£à¸š` to `à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸–à¸¹à¸à¸­à¸­à¸à¸šà¸´à¸¥`. After the later bill-driven decision, this is the target direction for stock-in: `stock_ledger` should use `ref_type = 'PB'` for purchase stock movement, while `WTI` remains source evidence and usage control. Recent `weight_tickets` such as `WTI012606-0019` and `WTO012606-0002` correctly have no `WTI`/`WTO` stock-ledger source rows under the revised target. Remaining stock write gap is sales-side: `SB Stock` must create `ref_type = 'SB'` stock-out rows.
Docs checkpoint on 2026-06-11: added [docs/notes/Stock Ledger and Stock Balance.md](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/docs/notes/Stock%20Ledger%20and%20Stock%20Balance.md) as the canonical stock semantics note. It separates `stock_ledger` as movement fact from `stock balance` as derived aggregate, documents aggregate keys (`product/branch/warehouse/lot/status/not_available`), and now clarifies the revised bill-driven movement ownership: `PB Stock` owns stock-in, `SB Stock` owns stock-out, and `WTI/WTO` are source evidence/usage controls.
Docs/API alignment checkpoint on 2026-06-11: WTI/WTO, Purchase Flow, Sales Flow, Purchase Bills Page Flow, Sales Bills Page Flow, and Purchase Flow Status Matrix were synced back to the legacy-aligned bill-driven rule set with a hold layer: `WTI 1 à¹ƒà¸š -> PB 1 à¹ƒà¸š`, `WTO 1 à¹ƒà¸š -> SB 1 à¹ƒà¸š`, no target partial-billed state for WTI/WTO, `PB/SB` own stock movement, and `WTO` owns stock hold/reservation only. Docs checkpoint on 2026-06-11: added [docs/notes/Document Aging Policy.md](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/docs/notes/Document%20Aging%20Policy.md) for aging across `PB/SB/WTI/WTO/POB/POS`. The target separates `financial_due_aging` for AP/AR bills from `operational_pending_aging` for WTI/WTO and PO commitments, defines bucket/stop-counting rules, and records the current API gap: `/api/finance/ap` and `/api/finance/ar` exist for bill aging, but WTI/WTO/PO aging and a combined document-aging report are still follow-up work.
Docs checkpoint on 2026-06-11: aging/date semantics were refined further. `created_at` / system-created timestamp must be visible on every record/document list/detail page as `à¸§à¸±à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸ªà¸£à¹‰à¸²à¸‡à¸£à¸²à¸¢à¸à¸²à¸£` for audit, but it must stay separate from business dates such as document date, payment/receipt date, due date, and downstream used date. Backdated documents must keep both business close date and system close timestamp so business aging and process latency do not get mixed.

Docs checkpoint on 2026-06-11: remaining implementation gaps after the status decision are now tracked explicitly. Docs checkpoint on 2026-06-11: WTI/WTO target decisions were confirmed, then superseded by the bill-driven + stock hold legacy alignment later the same day. Final target for this checkpoint is: `WTI save` does not write stock ledger, `WTO save` creates active pending_out but does not write stock ledger, `PB Stock save = stock in`, `SB Stock save = consume hold + stock out`; edit/cancel stock reverse belongs to `PB/SB`, while WTO edit/cancel before billing rebuilds/releases hold; `WTI/WTO` lock after downstream usage. `WTI 1 à¹ƒà¸š -> PB 1 à¹ƒà¸š` and `WTO 1 à¹ƒà¸š -> SB 1 à¹ƒà¸š` remain target rules with no target partial-billed state. WTO UX remains product-first: user selects product in each line first, then selects an active branch-matched `RM/FG` warehouse for that line, after which stock balance is shown as `à¸„à¸‡à¹€à¸«à¸¥à¸·à¸­à¸ˆà¸£à¸´à¸‡ / à¸ˆà¸­à¸‡à¹„à¸§à¹‰ / à¸žà¸£à¹‰à¸­à¸¡à¸ªà¹ˆà¸‡`.

Runtime printable-documents checkpoint on 2026-06-09: legacy print audit found business documents that must have print/Save as PDF parity beyond PB. `docs/notes/Printable Documents.md` is now the canonical print backlog/contract: implemented `PB` and `WTI/WTO`; required follow-ups are `SB` delivery-note print, payment-history `PMT` print, `PMA` approval sheet, hardened `RV` receipt-voucher print, and `RCP` customer receipt print. Runtime PMT daily print implementation on 2026-06-09: `/purchase/payments?tab=history` now has a `à¸žà¸´à¸¡à¸žà¹Œà¸£à¸²à¸¢à¸‡à¸²à¸™à¸›à¸£à¸°à¸ˆà¸³à¸§à¸±à¸™` action in the history filter toolbar. Runtime purchase-bill print implementation checkpoint on 2026-06-09: `/purchase/bills` now has per-document PB print from the list row action, the in-page detail modal, and the direct `/purchase/bills/{docNo}` detail page. The print flow fetches `/api/purchase/bills/{docNo}` detail, opens a print window immediately to avoid popup-block timing, then writes a corporate A4 portrait document after loading `/api/admin/company-profile`. The template shows only branch-specific Company Profile data in the header; if a field or logo is missing it prints `à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸¡à¸µà¸‚à¹‰à¸­à¸¡à¸¹à¸¥` in that slot and does not use default/fallback company data. It includes Supplier, supplier tax/address, WTI refs, truck/license plate when available, prepared by, Sale when available, item rows with gross/deduct/net weight and unit, price, amount, subtotal/discount/VAT/total/payable balance, notes, signature lines, and cancelled/supplier-swap watermark. `purchase-bill-detail` read-model now exposes warehouse, supplier address/tax id, ref no, sales name, and vehicle/license data needed by the print template. Validation passed with `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`. Browser MCP smoke could not run because the Playwright MCP Chrome profile was already locked by another process.
Runtime purchase-bill multi-page print follow-up on 2026-06-10: PB print now explicitly supports bills with 30+ item rows. The item table can split across A4 portrait pages, repeats a compact PB/table header on every printed page, avoids breaking item rows across page boundaries, hides filler rows in print, and keeps weight summary, note/totals, and signatures as non-splitting blocks that move to the final page/new page when needed.
Runtime purchase-bill single-page print fit follow-up on 2026-06-10: PB print received print-only compact spacing so normal one-page bills do not push the signature block onto page 2. The print media rules reduce A4 margin, header/logo size, panel/table padding, total-row spacing, and signature vertical spacing while preserving the multi-page table behavior for 30+ item rows.
Runtime purchase-bill A4 portrait follow-up on 2026-06-10: per user instruction, PB print is now A4 portrait instead of landscape. The print helper and docs were synced to use portrait wording, and the item table widths/font spacing were compressed so gross/deduct/net weight, unit price, and amount still fit within portrait A4 while retaining multi-page table-header repeat behavior. PB print header also remains aligned with the latest user rule: no time field and no document-status field in the top document metadata.
Runtime purchase-bill print remark follow-up on 2026-06-10: PB print item-table `REMARK` now uses the source receipt/WTI lot notes from `weight_ticket_lines.note` through the allocated `weight_ticket_product_summaries` relation, instead of showing the PB line note for WTI-backed rows. Trading/no-receipt rows still use the PB line note because there is no source receipt note.
Runtime purchase-bill print totals follow-up on 2026-06-10: PB print summary now follows the user-defined order: subtotal, discount, after-discount total, VAT from after-discount total, separator, grand total, deposit/partial-payment deduction, and payable balance. The deposit/partial-payment line uses `paidAmount` so it represents both ADV/mà¸±à¸”à¸ˆà¸³ and any partial PMT settlement, not just active ADV allocation.
Runtime purchase-bill short-item print follow-up on 2026-06-10: PB print now keeps filler table rows visible in print and extends short bills to a 15-row item table so documents with few products visually stretch toward the footer/signature area. Multi-page PB prints also add filler rows on the final item-table page using a continuation-page capacity of 24 rows, so cases where the last page has only one or a few real item rows still stretch the table toward the footer instead of leaving a mostly empty final page.
Runtime purchase-bill print design checkpoint on 2026-06-09: legacy purchase bills already support per-row printing via `erp.printDocument('receipt', row.raw.id)` (`old-apps/legacy/index.html:15119`) and a popup print helper (`old-apps/legacy/index.html:6449`). Active Next `/purchase/bills` still needs a real per-document PB print action from list/detail/direct detail. Target print must use `/admin/company-profile` / `à¸‚à¹‰à¸­à¸¡à¸¹à¸¥à¸šà¸£à¸´à¸©à¸±à¸— (à¸ªà¸³à¸«à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹ƒà¸šà¸žà¸´à¸¡à¸žà¹Œ)` as the canonical header source, not hardcoded company data. The customer sample image received on 2026-06-09 is a landscape `à¹ƒà¸šà¸£à¸±à¸šà¸ªà¸´à¸™à¸„à¹‰à¸²` with delivery/time/seller/truck/preparer/Sale fields, a total-summary band, seller/date/document-number metadata row, and an item table with `à¸ªà¸´à¸™à¸„à¹‰à¸²`, `REMARK`, `à¸™à¸™.à¸à¹ˆà¸­à¸™à¸«à¸±à¸`, `à¸™à¸™.à¸«à¸±à¸`, `à¸™à¸™.à¸ªà¸¸à¸—à¸˜à¸´`, `à¸£à¸²à¸„à¸²`, `à¸£à¸§à¸¡`. User clarified after sharing the image that the active app may redesign the form as long as the data is complete and the output looks corporate, so the image is now a data completeness checklist rather than a layout to copy. Required print content and the corporate layout direction are documented in `docs/notes/Purchase Bills Page Flow.md`: PB number/date, Supplier, branch/warehouse, Stock/Trading, line-level PO/Spot source, product rows with actual units (`à¸à¸.`/`à¸¥à¸±à¸‡`), subtotal/discount/VAT/net/ADV/payable balance, payment status, remarks/reference numbers, and signature lines. This is a PB document print only; it must not create PMA/PMT/BST or any transaction side effect.
Runtime quantity-unit display decision on 2026-06-09: user clarified the target rule for product quantities with units `à¸à¸.` and `à¸¥à¸±à¸‡`. The active convention is to separate units clearly wherever possible instead of summing different units into one number. Line-item surfaces must show the actual unit from document snapshot or product master, especially PO/PB/SB details, receipt vouchers, print previews, exports, and external-facing documents. Summary/KPI/report surfaces should show grouped totals such as `à¸£à¸§à¸¡ 1,250 à¸à¸. / 32 à¸¥à¸±à¸‡` when multiple units exist; only use a single combined quantity if a specific approved conversion rule exists for that report. Internal input labels may use neutral wording like `à¸ˆà¸³à¸™à¸§à¸™ (à¸à¸./à¸¥à¸±à¸‡)` or `à¸£à¸²à¸„à¸²/à¸«à¸™à¹ˆà¸§à¸¢`, but hardcoded `à¸à¸.` is not acceptable for fields that can contain `à¸¥à¸±à¸‡`.
Runtime petty-advance modal/design follow-up on 2026-06-09: `/daily/petty-advance` create/edit modal now hides server-owned `PADV` numbers, uses `à¸§à¸±à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` as the only user-editable date, removes the old company-account section, and splits the form into compact sections. `à¸œà¸¹à¹‰à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` is now a SearchCombobox backed by active `director_employees` rows of type `à¸à¸£à¸£à¸¡à¸à¸²à¸£` or `à¸žà¸™à¸±à¸à¸‡à¸²à¸™` that have receiving bank data; the selected person's receiving account is shown read-only from master data. `petty_advances` now stores recipient person code plus receiving-account snapshots (`recipient_bank_name`, `recipient_bank_account_name`, `recipient_account_no`, `recipient_bank_branch`) so history does not drift when master data changes. `PADV` is now treated as the loan/advance outstanding document only: create/edit no longer creates or rewrites `BST` bank statement rows and no longer requires a company payment account. `BST` is created only when saving a `PRET` return-money entry. The list filter uses segmented filters with `à¸›à¸£à¸°à¹€à¸ à¸—` and `à¸ªà¸–à¸²à¸™à¸°` on separate rows, table wording uses `à¸§à¸±à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢`, row click opens detail, both row action and detail modal expose `à¸„à¸·à¸™à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` for active rows with remaining amount, notes are textarea, and the money fields use text+decimal input rather than native number. Migration `20260609073126_add_petty_advance_recipient_account_snapshot.sql` was applied to dev-target and marked applied with `supabase migration repair 20260609073126 --status applied`.
Runtime EXP direct-payment checkpoint on 2026-06-09: `/daily/expense` modal now has an explicit mode choice before save: `à¸ªà¹ˆà¸‡à¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´` keeps the existing EXP -> PMA -> PMT path, while `à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸¥à¸¢` requires Supplier receiving channel, company payment account, Discount, and Bank fee, then creates `EXP` + `PMT` in one transaction without creating `PMA`. Direct EXP PMT writes `payments.lines` source snapshots (`sourceType = expense`, `sourceDocNo = EXP...`), `bank_statement`, `payment_account_splits`, and `payment_status_logs`, so it appears in `/purchase/payments?tab=history` even without `payment_allocations`. Cancelling a direct EXP PMT cancels the PMT/payment facts and sets the source EXP to `cancelled` because there is no PMA to return to the waiting-payment queue. The modal order now asks `à¸§à¸´à¸˜à¸µà¸”à¸³à¹€à¸™à¸´à¸™à¸à¸²à¸£` first, places Supplier receiving channel after `à¸œà¸¹à¹‰à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™`, places `à¸§à¸´à¸˜à¸µà¸à¸²à¸£à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` after expense lines, filters company payment accounts by payment method from `/master-data/payment-methods`, hides Bank fee for cash methods, and uses a redesigned `à¸ªà¸£à¸¸à¸›à¸à¹ˆà¸­à¸™à¸šà¸±à¸™à¸—à¸¶à¸` that separates EXP totals from actual cash-out totals. The expense list row click now opens an in-page detail modal instead of navigating away, the modal follows the compact detail/read-only design with summary cards and expense-line table, and the list table supports sortable resizable headers using the shared `ResizableTableHead` pattern. `docs/notes/Payment Flow.md`, `docs/notes/Daily Cash Flow.md`, and `REQUIREMENTS_TARGET_SYSTEM.md` now describe the same two-mode rule.
Runtime supplier master design follow-up on 2026-06-09: `/master-data/suppliers` list UI now follows `docs/design.md` list/table baseline. The filter card uses `h-9` controls, clear-filter action only when filters are active, lucide icons for Import/Export/Add, and a segmented `à¸ªà¸–à¸²à¸™à¸°:` filter (`à¸—à¸±à¹‰à¸‡à¸«à¸¡à¸”`, `à¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¸‡à¸²à¸™`, `à¸›à¸´à¸”`). The table is now a lined/resizable table with shared `ResizableTableHead`, `useResizableColumns('master-data.suppliers')`, persisted column widths, `colgroup` + `table-layout: fixed`, compact `text-xs font-semibold` body cells, slate row dividers, neutral outline row action, visible active toggle labels, and `Set col to default` when widths are customized. Supplier export/list APIs accept the same `active=active|inactive` filter so Excel output matches the visible status filter. Validation passed with `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`.
Runtime EXP supplier-reference/payment-approval follow-up on 2026-06-09: `/daily/expense` now treats `à¸œà¸¹à¹‰à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` as a required active Supplier master selection and stores the canonical relation in `expenses.supplier_id`; `expenses.payee` remains only a supplier-name snapshot/display value. New migration `20260609061848_add_expenses_supplier_reference.sql` was applied to dev-target and migration history was recorded; it adds the FK/index and backfills old rows only when the normalized `payee` name matches exactly one active supplier, leaving ambiguous rows for data repair instead of adding runtime name resolve. `/daily/payment-approval` now builds EXP `à¸Šà¹ˆà¸­à¸‡à¸—à¸²à¸‡à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢ / à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µà¸›à¸¥à¸²à¸¢à¸—à¸²à¸‡` from `expense.suppliers.supplier_bank_accounts` directly, the same model as PB/ADV. The API no longer injects synthetic payment-method options from master data or company cash/bank accounts, and EXP no longer resolves supplier from `expenses.payee` at approval time. A selectable option must come from `supplier_bank_accounts` of that source supplier and may represent either a supplier-specific payment method (for example cash/channel row) or a supplier bank account; labels show payment method, bank name, and account number when present. Supplier export also accepts the active filter used by the page.

Phone was removed from the UI. Bank fields are `à¸˜à¸™à¸²à¸„à¸²à¸£`, `à¸Šà¸·à¹ˆà¸­à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µ`, `à¹€à¸¥à¸‚à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µ`, and `à¸ªà¸²à¸‚à¸²`; if any bank field is entered, API/client validation requires bank, account name, and digit-only account number. The shared master-data table now uses the lined table baseline with compact font, sortable/resizable headers, persisted column widths, and the active toggle remains visible in status cells. Dev-target `director_employees` was cleared and reseeded with 10 mock rows `P001`-`P010`; no old transaction data was involved. Validation passed: `npm run prisma:generate --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`. Runtime purchase-bills supplier-swap-history tab checkpoint on 2026-06-09: `/daily/bill-swap-history` is no longer a standalone sidebar page. `/purchase/bills` now owns two tabs, `à¸šà¸´à¸¥à¸£à¸±à¸šà¸‹à¸·à¹‰à¸­` and `à¸›à¸£à¸°à¸§à¸±à¸•à¸´à¹€à¸›à¸¥à¸µà¹ˆà¸¢à¸™à¸šà¸´à¸¥ Supplier`; the history tab reuses `BillSwapHistoryPageClient` and keeps the persisted resizable-column behavior under table key `purchase.bills.supplier-swap-history`. Runtime resizable table-column design checkpoint on 2026-06-09: `docs/design.md` now defines an opt-in Resizable Columns pattern for dense report/history/ledger tables. The shared `useResizableColumns(tableKey, columns)` hook persists per-table widths to `localStorage`, requires default/min widths, supports reset to defaults, and keeps resize handles from triggering sort/click actions. The supplier-swap history table was the first consumer: its wide report table uses `colgroup`, `table-layout: fixed`, persisted column widths, a slim header resize handle, horizontal overflow for narrow screens, and a `Set col to default` action when custom widths exist. Validation passed with `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`.
Runtime app-shell sidebar auto-collapse checkpoint on 2026-06-08: active Next app desktop sidebar now defaults to a narrow icon rail and auto-expands on hover or keyboard focus, then collapses again when the pointer/focus leaves the sidebar. Mobile drawer behavior remains unchanged at full-width menu labels. `AppNavigation` now supports a compact desktop rail mode with native titles for collapsed items while preserving full Thai labels in the mobile drawer.
Runtime PB detail modal/time-series checkpoint on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/bills` row click now opens PB detail in a modal instead of navigating away from the list, while `/purchase/bills/{docNo}` remains available as a direct-link fallback using the same server read-model. The new shared PB detail read-model preserves historical PO source display after supplier-swap/cancel release, exposes product summaries, allocation rows, active ADV allocation, VAT totals, and `purchase_bill_status_logs`, and the UI renders `à¸›à¸£à¸°à¸§à¸±à¸•à¸´ PB` as a latest-first Time Series matching the `/purchase/po-buy` detail modal pattern.

Runtime purchase-payments design follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/payments` waiting-payment queue now aligns its visible list shell with `docs/design.md`: KPI cards use the white summary-card baseline, the PMA search/sort toolbar uses `h-9` controls, the clear action appears only when search/sort filters are active, pagination buttons match the page-size selector height, loading/empty table cells use the shared padding baseline, and account-copy controls use lucide icons instead of inline SVG.
