Runtime supplier-payment history tab follow-up on 2026-06-09: à¸›à¸£à¸°à¸§à¸±à¸•à¸´à¸à¸²à¸£à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸–à¸¹à¸à¸¢à¹‰à¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸‚à¹‰à¸² `/purchase/payments` à¹€à¸›à¹‡à¸™à¹à¸—à¹‡à¸š `à¸›à¸£à¸°à¸§à¸±à¸•à¸´` à¸‚à¹‰à¸²à¸‡à¹à¸—à¹‡à¸š `à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™ Supplier`. à¹à¸—à¹‡à¸šà¸£à¸­à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¹‚à¸«à¸¥à¸” `/api/purchase/payments`, à¹à¸—à¹‡à¸šà¸›à¸£à¸°à¸§à¸±à¸•à¸´à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¹‚à¸«à¸¥à¸” `/api/purchase/payment-history`, route à¹€à¸à¹ˆà¸² `/purchase/payment-history` redirect à¹„à¸› `/purchase/payments?tab=history`, à¹à¸¥à¸° sidebar à¹€à¸­à¸²à¹€à¸¡à¸™à¸¹à¸›à¸£à¸°à¸§à¸±à¸•à¸´à¹à¸¢à¸à¸­à¸­à¸à¹€à¸žà¸·à¹ˆà¸­à¹ƒà¸«à¹‰ user à¹€à¸‚à¹‰à¸²à¸œà¹ˆà¸²à¸™à¸«à¸™à¹‰à¸² Supplier payment à¸ˆà¸¸à¸”à¹€à¸”à¸µà¸¢à¸§.
Runtime PO Buy detail timeline follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/po-buy` detail modal now merges the separate current-status field, `à¸›à¸£à¸°à¸§à¸±à¸•à¸´à¸ªà¸–à¸²à¸™à¸°`, and `à¸›à¸£à¸°à¸§à¸±à¸•à¸´à¸à¸²à¸£à¸ˆà¸±à¸”à¸ªà¸£à¸£` block into one `à¸›à¸£à¸°à¸§à¸±à¸•à¸´ POB` section. The section sorts status and allocation events by one timestamp stream but renders latest-first, showing actor, action, transition/source PB, allocation qty/amount/before-after remaining, note/meta, and a latest status marker so the PO lifecycle is read as one sequence instead of separate surfaces. PO Buy status display in both the table and modal now follows the `/purchase/bills` pattern: `dot + à¸ªà¸µà¸‚à¹‰à¸­à¸„à¸§à¸²à¸¡` instead of a filled badge, with clearer semantic colors for open/partial/received/short-closed/cancelled states.
Runtime PB supplier-swap historical source-display follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/bills/{docNo}` detail now distinguishes active allocation facts from historical source display. For supplier-swap/cancelled PB rows such as `PB012606-0008`, `purchase_bill_po_allocations` may be released/deleted to restore PO remaining, but the detail page must still display the original PO source from `purchase_bill_items.po_buy_id` or `source_snapshot.poBuyId` instead of falling back to `Spot Buy`. `docs/notes/Purchase Bills Page Flow.md` now records this contract explicitly.
Runtime PB supplier-swap cancel-status follow-up on 2026-06-08: when `/purchase/bills` supplier swap saves, the old PB is no longer stored as plain `cancelled`; it uses raw status `cancelled_supplier_swap` and displays `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸/à¹€à¸›à¸¥à¸µà¹ˆà¸¢à¸™ Supplier`. The status remains treated as a cancelled PB by WTI allocation usage, payment approval/AP queues, cash-position, tracking, and main sales-control read models. New migration `20260608114500_add_purchase_bill_supplier_swap_cancel_status.sql` expands `purchase_bills_status_chk` to allow the status.
Runtime PB supplier-swap ADV lookup follow-up on 2026-06-08: in `/purchase/bills` supplier swap mode, the ADV/à¸¡à¸±à¸”à¸ˆà¸³ search now uses the draft `Supplier à¹ƒà¸«à¸¡à¹ˆ` instead of the old locked PB supplier. Changing `Supplier à¹ƒà¸«à¸¡à¹ˆ` clears the selected ADV so an ADV from the previous supplier cannot be carried into the new PB payload. The WTI and original PB supplier remain locked and are not used for the ADV lookup while swap mode is active.
Runtime ADV partially-paid status checkpoint on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/advance-payments` now adds runtime status `partially_paid` (`à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§à¸šà¸²à¸‡à¸ªà¹ˆà¸§à¸™`) and relabels `paid` as `à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§`. ADV status priority is `cancelled` > allocation (`allocated` / `partially_allocated`) > payment (`paid` / `partially_paid`) > approval (`approved` / `partially_approved`) > `pending_approval`. Allocation release paths, including PB cancel and supplier swap voiding the old PB, now recalc ADV from active allocation plus actual PMT settlement so an ADV returns from `à¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¸«à¸±à¸à¸šà¸´à¸¥...` to `à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§` or `à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§à¸šà¸²à¸‡à¸ªà¹ˆà¸§à¸™` as appropriate. New migration `20260608113000_add_supplier_advance_partially_paid_status.sql` expands the DB status constraint and backfills current ADV statuses from active PMA/PMT/allocation facts; it was applied to dev-target via direct `psql` and marked applied with `supabase migration repair 20260608113000 --status applied`. DB verification now shows `paid = 1`, `partially_paid = 1`, and the live constraint includes `partially_paid`. Validation passed with `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next prisma:generate`, and `git diff --check`.
Runtime ADV net-weight input follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/advance-payments` now derives `à¸™à¹‰à¸³à¸«à¸™à¸±à¸à¸ªà¸¸à¸—à¸˜à¸´` in the form from `à¸™à¹‰à¸³à¸«à¸™à¸±à¸à¹€à¸‚à¹‰à¸² - à¸™à¹‰à¸³à¸«à¸™à¸±à¸à¸­à¸­à¸`, shows the net field as read-only, uses the derived value for the summary calculation, and normalizes `netWeight` from the same inputs before submit so the payload matches the displayed value.
Runtime purchase-bill supplier-swap draft-supplier follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/bills` supplier swap mode no longer unlocks or mutates the original `form.supplierId` while the edit modal is open. The modal now shows Supplier à¹€à¸”à¸´à¸¡ as readonly, adds a separate `Supplier à¹ƒà¸«à¸¡à¹ˆ` combobox backed by draft state, and only substitutes that draft supplier into the `supplier_swap` save payload. This removes the remaining post-select/blur reset path where Supplier combobox state could update the main PB form context and cascade into WTI/receipt-dependent UI.
Runtime purchase-bill supplier-swap supplier-blur follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/bills` supplier swap mode now ignores empty intermediate Supplier values emitted by the supplier search combobox while typing or blurring, so clicking blank space in the modal after choosing a new Supplier cannot push `supplierId = ''` through the normal form-change path. The edit modal also snapshots the original WTI receipt when opening the PB edit flow and reuses that locked receipt snapshot while supplier swap is active, keeping the original receipt visible even if live receipt options are filtered by the newly selected Supplier.
Runtime purchase-bill supplier-swap receipt-lock follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/bills` supplier swap mode now keeps the original WTI visible after choosing a new Supplier by retaining the selected locked receipt in the combobox option set even when the normal receipt list filters by the new Supplier. The `à¹€à¸›à¸¥à¸µà¹ˆà¸¢à¸™ Supplier` button is hidden after entering swap mode, and the `à¸¥à¹‰à¸²à¸‡à¹ƒà¸šà¸£à¸±à¸šà¸‚à¸­à¸‡` button is hidden in edit/supplier-swap contexts so the locked receipt cannot be cleared from this flow. `docs/notes/Purchase Bills Page Flow.md` now records that Supplier changes in swap mode must not reset, clear, or hide the original WTI.
Runtime purchase-bill supplier-swap checkpoint on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/bills` now has a page-specific flow doc at `docs/notes/Purchase Bills Page Flow.md` and an implemented supplier-swap path in the edit modal. The `à¹€à¸›à¸¥à¸µà¹ˆà¸¢à¸™ Supplier` button appears beside Supplier only while editing a locked Stock PB with WTI selected; entering the mode keeps branch, warehouse, WTI, row count, source summary, and qty locked, clears old ADV/PO selection, and forces all new lines to `Spot Buy à¹€à¸—à¹ˆà¸²à¸™à¸±à¹‰à¸™`. `PATCH /api/purchase/bills` action `supplier_swap` validates no active PMA/PMT, requires the original WTI source, rejects any PO allocation, rejects changed rows/source/qty, voids the old PB with stock/WTI/PO/ADV release side effects, creates a replacement PB with a new `PB...` number, writes new stock ledger for the replacement bill, and creates `bill_swap_history` rows linked to the old PB with the replacement PB doc no in `reason`. ADV allocation from the old PB is released and is not carried to the new PB. Validation passed with `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`. Headless mobile smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:3000/purchase/bills` redirected unauthenticated to `/login?redirect=%2Fpurchase%2Fbills`, reported `overflow = 0`, and had no HTTP responses `>= 400`; the only console error was the local dev HMR websocket handshake noise.
Runtime expense docs/flow sync on 2026-06-08: target requirements and Payment Flow now document `EXP` as the expense source document for approval/payment lifecycle. The synced rules cover user-selected `à¸§à¸±à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢`, Supplier-master payee suggestions, server-owned EXP numbering by selected date month, multi-line expense items with searchable categories and VAT/WHT, source status ownership by approval/payment flow, row-click detail navigation, and pending-only edit/cancel locks.
Runtime daily transfer modal/design checkpoint on 2026-06-08: `/daily/transfer` modal now hides system-managed fields from user entry. `à¹€à¸¥à¸‚à¸—à¸µà¹ˆ` is no longer shown in the modal, `à¸§à¸±à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆ` stays auto-filled by the client/server write path instead of being edited in the modal, and `à¸œà¸¹à¹‰à¸—à¸³à¸£à¸²à¸¢à¸à¸²à¸£` remains the authenticated creator from `created_by` rather than a form field. The page also now follows the list-page design baseline more closely with count/page-size/pagination above the table, active-account options reused across filters/modal, field-level validation focus in the modal, text/decimal money inputs, and a compact transfer total summary.
Runtime daily transfer account-option balance follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/daily/transfer` modal `à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µà¸•à¹‰à¸™à¸—à¸²à¸‡` and `à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µà¸›à¸¥à¸²à¸¢à¸—à¸²à¸‡` options now display current balance in the label, matching the supplier payment modal pattern `à¸Šà¸·à¹ˆà¸­à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µ (à¸„à¸‡à¹€à¸«à¸¥à¸·à¸­ ...)`. This is UI display only; `/api/daily/transfers`, bank-statement posting, and outer list filter labels are unchanged.
Runtime daily transfer field-pattern follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/daily/transfer` modal `à¸ˆà¸³à¸™à¸§à¸™à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` and `à¸„à¹ˆà¸²à¸˜à¸£à¸£à¸¡à¹€à¸™à¸µà¸¢à¸¡` now use the design money input pattern with editable draft text during focus and formatted `formatMoney(...)` display on blur, fixing the format-on-every-keystroke typing issue. `à¸«à¸¡à¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸«à¸•à¸¸` now renders as a textarea per the Remark / Note Field Pattern. This remains a client-side form behavior/layout change only; the transfer save payload fields are unchanged.
Runtime daily transfer save 400 follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/api/daily/transfers` POST save could fail in the local/dev runtime because new `TRF` and paired `BST` doc numbers were generated inside `prisma.$transaction` through the global Prisma client instead of the active transaction client. This matched the same constrained-pool transaction pattern already fixed in PO short-close and expenses. Transfer save now calls `nextDailyDocNo('transfers', 'TRF', date, tx)` and `nextDailyDocNos('bank_statement', 'BST', date, 2, tx)` so document numbering, transfer insert/update, old statement cleanup, and paired bank-statement posting stay on the same transaction connection.
Runtime daily transfer flow-doc expansion on 2026-06-08: `docs/notes/Daily Cash Flow.md` now documents the `/daily/transfer` business objective, list/filter behavior, create/edit modal rules, system-managed fields, save payload contract, paired `BST` ledger side effects, example posting, and error/rollback rules. The note is the current canonical page flow for transfer UX and bank-statement side effects until a broader cash/bank flow document supersedes it.
Runtime daily transfer row-detail follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/daily/transfer` table rows are now clickable and keyboard-openable to a detail modal that summarizes the selected `TRF`, transfer amount, fee, source/destination accounts, note, actor, and the paired Bank Statement impact. Row action clicks stop propagation so `à¹à¸à¹‰à¹„à¸‚` opens edit directly instead of opening detail first. The disabled destructive action label is now `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸` instead of `à¸¥à¸š`, pending a reviewed append-only transfer reversal flow.
Runtime expense list detail navigation follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/daily/expense` list now labels the row date column as `à¸§à¸±à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢`, and table rows are clickable to open `/daily/expense/{docNo}` detail pages. Row-level `à¹à¸à¹‰à¹„à¸‚` / `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸` actions stop propagation so they keep their existing modal/cancel behavior instead of navigating.
Runtime expense payment-date follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/daily/expense` create/edit modal now exposes required `à¸§à¸±à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` using the shared date picker and existing `expenseFormSchema.date` validation. `/api/daily/expenses` now uses the submitted `date` for `expenses.date`; new `EXP` running numbers still remain server-owned but their year/month sequence is based on the selected payment date.
Runtime expense category searchable follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/daily/expense` create/edit line-item `à¸«à¸¡à¸§à¸”à¸„à¹ˆà¸²à¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` now uses the shared `SearchCombobox` instead of a plain row select. Users can type-search category name, category code, or expense type text inside the modal table, while the save payload still submits the same optional `lines[].categoryId` value and keeps the existing expense write contract unchanged.
Runtime expense-dashboard legacy-function follow-up on 2026-06-09: `/daily/expense-dashboard` now ports the legacy `view-expenseDashboard` calculation function into the active Next client. The dashboard again builds active category buckets plus `_uncat`, uses header-level `row.categoryId`, sums `row.amount + row.vat` per expense/month, sorts categories by total, computes `avg/latest/prev/vsAvg/deviation`, and applies the legacy anomaly rules (`high` when latest exceeds both `1.5Ã— avg` and 5,000; `low` when latest is positive but below `30% avg`). Legacy dashboard wording/icons for period selector, KPI labels, anomaly cards, status pills, and rule note were restored while keeping the topbar-driven page title. `docs/notes/Expense Dashboard Flow.md` is now the page-specific flow doc for this read-only dashboard contract and is indexed in `docs/migration/00-doc-index.md`.
Runtime Thai business-date helper follow-up on 2026-06-09: `todayDateInput()` now returns the current `YYYY-MM-DD` using `Asia/Bangkok` instead of slicing UTC `toISOString()`. This makes `/daily/expense-dashboard` year/month rollover follow Thai business date and also affects daily/finance/stock form defaults that import the same helper, such as payment date, transfer date, due date, and default report filters. Export filenames and utilities that still call `new Date().toISOString()` directly are intentionally outside this helper change. `docs/notes/Expense Dashboard Flow.md` now records the dashboard-specific date/year rollover behavior and helper impact.
Runtime PO Sell flow-doc checkpoint on 2026-06-09: `docs/notes/PO Sell Flow.md` now captures the page-specific `/sales/po-sell` contract after the legacy comparison. New PO Sell rows start as raw `Open` and derived `Not Matched`, should appear immediately in the PO Sell table, and should feed downstream read models including `/dual-costing/cost-allocator`, `/po-reports/outstanding`, and `/sales/bills` / `/sales/stock-issue` handoff. The doc also records current gaps before coding: branch-aware POS numbering, Thai PO Sell lifecycle statuses, line-level PO Sell allocation in sales bills, and stock-issue integration.
Runtime PO Sell downstream integration checkpoint on 2026-06-09: `/sales/po-sell` now separates normalized document status (`à¹€à¸›à¸´à¸”à¸­à¸¢à¸¹à¹ˆ`, `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸`, `à¸›à¸´à¸”à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§`) from match status in both filters and table display, and the match filter includes `Cancelled`. `GET /api/sales/po-sell` normalizes raw `Cancelled/cancelled/Canceled/canceled`, `Completed/Closed`, and zero remaining quantity into stable document-status values instead of exposing raw status as the only filter. `POST /api/sales/bills` now consumes selected bill-level `poSellId`: it validates active PO Sell, customer, branch, product codes, and remaining quantity; creates the sales bill and updates `po_sells.items[].remainingQty`, `remaining_qty`, `remaining_amount`, `cut_amount`, and final `Completed` status in one transaction. `/po-reports/outstanding` and `/dual-costing/cost-allocator` now exclude closed/completed/cancelled/canceled PO Sell rows from active downstream lists. Line-level PO Sell allocation and stock-issue/PSALE integration remain follow-up scope.
Runtime expense due/payee input follow-up on 2026-06-08: per user request, `/daily/expense` create form now defaults `à¸„à¸£à¸šà¸à¸³à¸«à¸™à¸”` to the current date each time the modal opens while keeping the date input editable and preserving existing row due dates on edit. The `à¸œà¸¹à¹‰à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` search datalist is now restricted to active Supplier master rows only; Customer, Sale, and employee sources are no longer returned as payee suggestions. Runtime expense save 400 follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/daily/expense` POST save could fail after the transaction-data cleanup because the create path generated the new `EXP` doc no inside `prisma.$transaction` while calling `nextDailyDocNo()` through the global Prisma client. This repeats the single-connection transaction deadlock pattern already seen in PO short-close when the local/dev runtime pool is constrained. `/api/daily/expenses` now takes an advisory transaction lock on `expenses.doc_no` for new rows and calls `nextDailyDocNo('expenses', 'EXP', date, tx)` through the same transaction client, keeping doc-number generation and insert on one connection.
Runtime sales receipt tabs checkpoint on 2026-06-08: `/sales/receipts` now separates the customer receipt action and receipt history into explicit tabs. Runtime product image upload checkpoint on 2026-06-08: `/master-data/products` create/edit modal now includes a single-image `à¸£à¸¹à¸›à¸ªà¸´à¸™à¸„à¹‰à¸²` profile-style upload field. Runtime weight-ticket image/product picker UI checkpoint on 2026-06-08: `/daily/weight-tickets` vehicle and product-line image upload controls now use compact profile-style image tiles while still allowing multiple images. Empty/add tiles open the file picker directly, uploaded images show fixed `112px` square previews, image tiles open the preview dialog, and each tile keeps its own remove action without changing the existing image payload/storage contract. Product selection keeps the original searchable `SearchCombobox` as the primary mobile input, and adds a secondary visual selector with product-type category chips plus compact 3-column mobile image cards backed by product master `imageNames`, while still submitting the existing product code contract to the WTI/WTO API.
Runtime weight-ticket mobile overflow follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/daily/weight-tickets` now constrains the mobile page, active line panel, and product-image selector with `min-w-0` / `overflow-hidden` guards so the form cannot create page-level horizontal scrolling. Runtime supplier-payment WHT/voucher-summary follow-up on 2026-06-08: per user request, `/purchase/payments` Payment Voucher modal no longer shows WHT in `à¸£à¸²à¸¢à¸à¸²à¸£à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` or the payment KPI. New PMT saves force supplier-payment `withholding_tax = 0`; the client distributes voucher discount into payload lines only for legacy row-level persistence/reconciliation, while existing payment-history rows can still display historical WHT values. `docs/notes/Payment Flow.md` was updated to record the no-WHT, voucher-level discount/fee PMT modal/write contract.

Runtime supplier-payment line layout follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/payments` Payment Voucher modal `à¸£à¸²à¸¢à¸à¸²à¸£à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` now renders PMA/source/recipient plus approved `à¸Šà¹ˆà¸­à¸‡à¸—à¸²à¸‡à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™`, `à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µà¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™`, and `à¸„à¹‰à¸²à¸‡` in one PMA row. Runtime supplier-payment doc/flow sync on 2026-06-08: `docs/notes/Payment Flow.md` and `docs/migration/09-implementation-tasklist.md` now document the latest `/purchase/payments` PMT modal contract: no manual `à¸§à¸´à¸˜à¸µà¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` field, derive `PMT.method` from selected PMA `destination_payment_method_snapshot`, require one recipient/payment method per PMT, and show approved `à¸Šà¹ˆà¸­à¸‡à¸—à¸²à¸‡à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` / `à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µà¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` next to source document information before the numeric payment row. Runtime ADV flow-doc sync on 2026-06-08: `docs/notes/Supplier Advance Payment Flow.md` Mermaid now shows partial approval as `ADV = à¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§à¸šà¸²à¸‡à¸ªà¹ˆà¸§à¸™`, void PMA returning to source pending/recalc, cancel PMT closing the old PMA cycle and forcing re-approval, and allocation status before PB settlement. `docs/notes/Payment Flow.md` now has a dedicated ADV status-recalc Mermaid derived from cancelled/allocation/active PMA/settled PMT facts, and explicitly keeps Supplier refund as a future separate flow outside the current ADV status/filter set.
Runtime ADV partial-approval status/filter checkpoint on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/advance-payments` now derives ADV current status from active PMA/PMT/allocation facts and adds `à¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§à¸šà¸²à¸‡à¸ªà¹ˆà¸§à¸™` (`partially_approved`) when approved PMA amount covers only part of the ADV. `à¸£à¸­à¸„à¸·à¸™à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` / `à¸„à¸·à¸™à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§` are no longer valid runtime ADV statuses and no longer appear in the API-provided filter list; Supplier refund remains a separate future flow instead of a `supplier_advance_payments.status` value. Migration `20260608093000_add_supplier_advance_partially_approved_status.sql` was applied to dev-target, normalizes any old refund status rows, recalculates ADV status from active facts, and constrains status to `pending_approval`, `partially_approved`, `approved`, `paid`, `partially_allocated`, `allocated`, and `cancelled`; `supabase migration repair 20260608093000 --status applied` marked migration history after the direct SQL apply. Dev-target transaction tables are currently empty after the approved transaction-data cleanup, so schema verification found `blockedRefundRows = 0`, `migration_marked = true`, and confirmed `partially_approved` is allowed by the live check constraint. Validation passed with `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`. Local unauthenticated smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:3000/purchase/advance-payments` redirects to login and `/api/purchase/advance-payments` returns `401`; route payload builder shows filter statuses are `all`, `pending_approval`, `partially_approved`, `approved`, `paid`, `partially_allocated`, `allocated`, and `cancelled` only.
Runtime PO Buy modal and short-close follow-up on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/po-buy` create/edit modal was widened to `max-w-5xl`, top fields now use a 3-column layout, and all modal controls are normalized to the same `h-9` sizing so `à¸œà¸¹à¹‰à¸‚à¸²à¸¢`, `à¸ªà¸²à¸‚à¸²`, `à¸§à¸±à¸™à¸ªà¹ˆà¸‡à¸¡à¸­à¸š`, product, quantity, and unit price controls align. The item table now has an overflow wrapper and wider fixed numeric columns, the item header label is `à¸ªà¸´à¸™à¸„à¹‰à¸²`, and the cancel action in the form footer is an outline button. `SearchCombobox` gained a backward-compatible `inputClassName` prop for local sizing without changing default combobox behavior elsewhere. Create/edit, cancel, and short-close now reload the list without auto-filling the saved PO doc no into the search/filter box, so the user's current filter stays unchanged. Short-close notes now accept textarea-style multiline reasons, and the PO Buy PATCH fallback message is action-specific for `shortClose`. The short-close button now stays disabled for unreceived `Open` PO rows and only enables when the PO is `Partially Received` with remaining quantity; the PATCH route also rejects direct short-close requests unless the PO is partially received. PO Buy Excel export now uses Thai headers, a Thai worksheet name, a dated filename, and includes the branch code while preserving search/status/date/selected-row filters. The `POB012606-0010` short-close 500 root cause was a transaction bug, not an unreceived-PO rule: the short-close transaction reloaded the PO through the global Prisma client instead of the transaction client, which deadlocked on the local `DATABASE_POOL_MAX=1` pool until timeout. `resolvePoBuyByDocNoOrId()` now accepts a client and short-close reloads through `tx`. Validation passed with `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`. Diagnostic rollback against dev-target `POB012606-0010` confirmed the short-close transaction/reconciliation path succeeds; the row remains `Open` after rollback. Headless browser smoke reached `/purchase/po-buy` and redirected unauthenticated to login with no console errors; unauthenticated PATCH smoke returns `401` instead of `500`.
Runtime ADV form simplification on 2026-06-08: per user request, `/purchase/advance-payments` no longer shows create/edit/detail fields for `à¸§à¸´à¸˜à¸µà¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢`, `à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µà¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢`, `à¸Šà¸·à¹ˆà¸­à¸¥à¸¹à¸à¸„à¹‰à¸²`, `à¸žà¸™à¸±à¸à¸‡à¸²à¸™à¸‚à¸±à¸šà¸£à¸–`, `à¸œà¸¹à¹‰à¸Šà¸±à¹ˆà¸‡à¸™à¹‰à¸³à¸«à¸™à¸±à¸`, or `à¸œà¸¹à¹‰à¸ªà¹ˆà¸‡`. ADV source capture now keeps supplier, branch, amount, large-scale/vehicle/product/weight, photo, and remark fields visible; payment method/account selection stays owned by downstream approval/payment screens. Migration `20260608024819_make_adv_payment_fields_optional.sql` relaxes `supplier_advance_payments.payment_method` to nullable so runtime does not store fake payment-method values when the field is intentionally hidden; dev-target apply succeeded and migration history is marked applied. Validation passed with `npm run prisma:generate --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `git diff --check`, schema smoke for blank payment fields, and unauthenticated browser smoke redirecting `/purchase/advance-payments` to login with no console/network failures.
Runtime purchase-bill workflow-state hardening on 2026-06-08: `/purchase/bills` now uses the derived Payment Flow read model as the visible list/filter/export status surface: `à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´`, `à¸£à¸­à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢`, `à¸Šà¸³à¸£à¸°à¸šà¸²à¸‡à¸ªà¹ˆà¸§à¸™`, `à¹€à¸ªà¸£à¹‡à¸ˆà¸ªà¸´à¹‰à¸™`, and `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸`. `/api/purchase/bills` derives `à¸£à¸­à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` from active `payment_approvals.status in ('approved','paid')`, derives `à¸Šà¸³à¸£à¸°à¸šà¸²à¸‡à¸ªà¹ˆà¸§à¸™` only from real non-cancelled PMT/payment activity while `payable_balance` remains, keeps partial ADV allocation as `à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´` until the remaining payable enters Payment Flow, and includes related PMA/PMT doc numbers in the row/export payload. Purchase-bill edit/cancel actions are disabled in the list and rejected by PATCH when a PMA is approved/paid or any non-cancelled PMT row exists. Validation passed with `git diff --check`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`. Browser smoke on existing local dev server reached `/purchase/bills`, redirected unauthenticated to `/login?redirect=%2Fpurchase%2Fbills`, and had no new console warnings/errors on the final run.
Runtime payment-voucher modal validation follow-up on 2026-06-08: investigated `PMA012606-0011/2` save failure. Dev-target data shows the PMA is still `approved`, has no active PMT, points to `ADV012606-0001`, uses active supplier `SU0001`, branch `01`, and can settle the remaining 2,000.00 with cash amount 1,940.00 plus WHT 60.00 at the default 3% rate. `/purchase/payments` modal now disables browser-native form validation so React/Zod can show validation errors inside the modal, especially missing `à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µà¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` or split-total mismatch, instead of making the save button appear unresponsive. This change does not alter PMA/PMT workflow state or server settlement rules.
Runtime PMT bank-statement numbering follow-up on 2026-06-08: investigated `PMA012606-0012/2` duplicate-save failure. Dev-target data shows the PMA is still `approved` and has no committed payment, while a cancelled PMT left reversed `payment_account_splits` rows with `PMTSPLIT-BST2606-0002` and `PMTSPLIT-BST2606-0003` after the actual `bank_statement` rows were deleted. The next PMT save reused `BST2606-0002`, then collided with the historical split key. `/api/purchase/payments` now generates PMT bank statement numbers from both current `bank_statement.doc_no` and historical `payment_account_splits.bank_statement_doc_no`, locks `bank_statement.doc_no` while generating PMT statement numbers, and writes new split keys as `PMTSPLIT-{PMT doc no}-{BST doc no}`. `/purchase/payments` also guards the modal submit handler against duplicate POSTs while a save is already in flight.
Runtime PMA split numbering follow-up on 2026-06-08: future split approvals no longer append visible suffixes such as `/1` or `/2` to one PMA cycle number. `/api/daily/payment-approval` now requests a consecutive set of PMA numbers for the submitted split count and creates each approval item as its own business document number, for example `PMA012606-0012`, `PMA012606-0013`. The PMA doc-number generator still reads legacy suffix rows by their base running number so existing historical `PMA.../n` rows do not make the next running number go backwards, and approval creation now takes an advisory lock on `payment_approvals.doc_no` during numbering.
Runtime dev-target transaction cleanup checkpoint on 2026-06-08: user requested deleting transaction data after the PMA/PMT numbering cleanup. Scope was dev-target Supabase project `fhglqymcdmrgbsbadnwr` only and transaction/document/ledger/history tables only; master data, configuration, auth/session, permissions, roles, profile, branch, warehouse, product, customer, supplier, account, tax, and migration-history tables were intentionally excluded. The cleanup intentionally removed old test transaction rows such as historical `PMA.../1` / `PMA.../2` records instead of data-repairing them, because the old dev transaction data is no longer needed. The `TRUNCATE ... RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE` whitelist covered 51 transaction tables, including purchase, PO, WTI, PMA/PMT, bank statement, payment split/allocation/status logs, ADV, EXP, sales, stock, production, trading, loan/finance transaction records, and related status/usage/allocation logs. Pre-check confirmed no FK cascade target outside the whitelist. Post-check: `transaction_tables_checked = 51`, `transaction_tables_nonzero = 0`; master sanity counts remain `products = 232`, `suppliers = 1888`, `customers = 51`, `accounts = 11`, `branches = 2`, and `warehouses = 4`.

Run tag `QA-PUR-1780884426224` created Spot `WTI012606-0011` / `PB012606-0008`, PO partial/full `POB012606-0007` with `PB012606-0009` and `PB012606-0010`, split PO+Spot `POB012606-0008` / `WTI012606-0014` / `PB012606-0011`, cancel-reversal `WTI012606-0015` / `PB012606-0012`, and PMT `PMT012606-0002`. Runtime purchase-bill warehouse doc-sync on 2026-06-08: code re-check confirmed `/purchase/bills` no longer lets users choose a warehouse manually for Stock PB. The client auto-selects `warehouseId` from the active `warehouses.type = RM` row for the selected branch, renders the `à¸„à¸¥à¸±à¸‡` field as read-only, recalculates it when branch changes, blocks manual `warehouseId` edits for Stock, and shows a save-blocking message when no active RM warehouse exists for that branch. `/api/purchase/bills` rejects Stock payloads with missing, inactive, cross-branch, or non-RM warehouses before writing `purchase_bills.warehouse_id` and Stock `stock_ledger.warehouse_id`. `docs/notes/Purchase Flow.md` was synced to this single rule; any older "user chooses warehouse/dropdown" note is superseded.

Runtime sidebar detail-parent checkpoint on 2026-06-07: sidebar active-state resolution now supports explicit parent mappings for detail routes whose URL is not a slash child of the visible menu route. `/purchase/payment-approvals/[id]` resolves to the main `/daily/payment-approval` menu, while normal detail paths such as `/purchase/bills/[id]`, `/purchase/payments/[id]`, `/purchase/advance-payments/[id]`, `/daily/expense/[id]`, and `/daily/weight-ticket-list/[id]` continue to resolve through slash-bounded prefix matching.
Runtime WHT master-rate checkpoint on 2026-06-07: migration `20260607143532_seed_wht_master_rates.sql` seeds dev-target `wht_settings` with active rates `WHT 1% (à¸‚à¸™à¸ªà¹ˆà¸‡/à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸«à¸¡à¸²)`, `WHT 2% (à¹‚à¸†à¸©à¸“à¸²)`, `WHT 3% (à¸šà¸£à¸´à¸à¸²à¸£)`, `WHT 5% (à¸„à¹ˆà¸²à¹€à¸Šà¹ˆà¸²)`, `WHT 10% (à¸•à¹ˆà¸²à¸‡à¸Šà¸²à¸•à¸´)`, and `WHT 15% (à¸”à¸­à¸à¹€à¸šà¸µà¹‰à¸¢/à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸›à¸±à¸™à¸œà¸¥)`. The migration normalizes the old default row named `WHT 3%` with `rate_percent = 0` back to `WHT 3% (à¸šà¸£à¸´à¸à¸²à¸£)` / `3.00`, keeps exactly one active default at 3%, and is marked applied in dev-target migration history. Runtime tax setting lookup and master-data list ordering now put `is_default` before `updated_at`, so adding active WHT choices does not silently change supplier-payment WHT calculation or make `/admin/system-settings` show a different current rate.
Runtime WHT settings UI checkpoint on 2026-06-07: `/admin/system-settings` now shows all `wht_settings` rows in a compact table instead of only the primary WHT record. Each WHT row has its own editable percent input and save action, while the active default row is labeled as the current calculation rate. The master-data payload now includes `isDefault` for VAT/WHT settings, and the UI keeps the VAT card as a single primary-rate editor.
Git safety checkpoint: `origin` / `https://github.com/sirimasth/ns-scrap-erp.git` is legacy read-only reference material only. Never push, force-push, branch, tag, PR, delete, or otherwise mutate that repo; use it only for read operations.
Runtime environment checkpoint on 2026-06-04: dev-target DB password for project `fhglqymcdmrgbsbadnwr` was rotated. Local `apps/next/.env.local` `DATABASE_URL` was updated for the current machine, local `next dev` on `http://localhost:3000` reconnected successfully, Vercel production `DATABASE_URL` for `new-ns-scrap-erp` was updated, and a new production deployment was completed and verified via `/api/health` = `200` and `/api/auth/me` unauthenticated = `401`. Do not record or commit the password itself; if connection errors recur, re-check local `.env.local` and Vercel env after any future password reset.
Runtime DB connection checkpoint on 2026-06-07: `/api/auth/me`, `/api/activity`, and `/api/master-data/impurities` reported shared `500` failures while Supabase session pooler returned `EMAXCONNSESSION max clients reached in session mode`. This was not an impurity-table schema issue. `apps/next/src/lib/server/prisma.ts` now resolves runtime DB URLs from `DATABASE_RUNTIME_URL` / `DATABASE_PRISMA_URL` / `DATABASE_URL` and normalizes Supabase pooler URLs from session port `5432` to transaction-pooler port `6543` before creating the shared `pg` pool. It also recreates the pool/client if the runtime URL changes, keeping local/Vercel from holding stale session-pooler connections after env or HMR changes. Validation passed with transaction-pooler `psql` smoke, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`.
Runtime PO Buy allocation-key checkpoint on 2026-06-07: `POB012605-0005` exposed a stale-reconciliation bug in the purchase-bill PO allocation flow. The DB still showed `po_buys.status = Open` and `remaining_qty = 120`, while active `purchase_bill_po_allocations` already consumed `240` kg through `PB012605-0420`, `PB012605-0421`, and `PB012606-0010`. Root cause was not a UI selector issue: old `po_buys.items` rows stored product keys as business codes such as `SKU001`, while `purchase_bill_po_allocations.purchase_bill_items.product_id` stores canonical internal bigint ids such as `3`; `reconcilePoBuys()` compared these mismatched keys and therefore did not reduce remaining qty/status. Migration `20260607111513_normalize_po_buy_item_product_keys.sql` now backfills resolvable `po_buys.items.productIdInternal` from current `products.code` without guessing legacy `P###` values, and dev-target migration history was repaired as applied. `reconcilePoBuys()` now requires canonical internal product ids for JSON item rows instead of falling back to `productId`/code, persists `productIdInternal` back into item snapshots, and `/api/purchase/bills` validates PO remaining qty by `PO + product` rather than header-level remaining qty. PO options now use the first remaining canonical item for display/filtering; legacy non-canonical PO rows are not offered as active options instead of breaking the page or falling back. Dev-target reconciliation refreshed 2 canonical allocated PO rows. Post-check for `POB012605-0005`: status is `Partially Received`, total qty `120`, `SKU001/productIdInternal=3` remaining `0`, `SKU003/productIdInternal=5` remaining `20`, cut amount `1000`, and remaining amount `600`. Existing over-allocation evidence remains visible in `purchase_bill_po_allocations` (`SKU001` active allocations total `240`) and was not deleted automatically. Validation passed with `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`.
Runtime dev-target transaction cleanup checkpoint on 2026-06-07: dev-target test transaction/document data was cleared after the PO allocation-key fix so runtime does not need fallback or hard-coded support for bad test rows. The cleanup used one explicit `TRUNCATE ... RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE` transaction against public transaction/document/ledger/history tables only, including purchase, PO, WTI, payment/PMA/PMT, sales, stock, production, trading, finance transaction logs, and related status/usage/allocation logs. Master/config/auth tables were intentionally excluded. Post-check: `po_buys`, `purchase_bills`, `purchase_bill_items`, `purchase_bill_po_allocations`, `weight_tickets`, `payments`, `payment_approvals`, `bank_statement`, `supplier_advance_payments`, and `stock_ledger` all have `0` rows; master sanity counts remain `products = 232`, `suppliers = 1888`, `customers = 51`, and `accounts = 11`. This was a dev data cleanup, not a schema migration or production cleanup.

Runtime purchase-bill warehouse RM-lock follow-up on 2026-06-07: user requirement changed `/purchase/bills` Stock warehouse behavior from user-selectable branch-filtered dropdown to locked auto-selection. The form now displays `à¸„à¸¥à¸±à¸‡` as read-only text, computes `warehouseId` from the active `RM` warehouse for the selected branch, clears/fails when no active branch RM warehouse exists, and prevents manual `warehouseId` updates in the Stock form state. `/api/purchase/bills` POST/PATCH also reject Stock payloads whose warehouse is not type `RM` or is outside the bill branch, so edited client payloads cannot bypass the lock. This supersedes the earlier "office chooses warehouse dropdown" requirement for Stock purchase bills. Validation passed with `git diff --check`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`. Browser QA on `http://localhost:3000/purchase/bills` redirected to `/login?redirect=%2Fpurchase%2Fbills`, so authenticated modal-selector verification still needs a logged-in browser session.
Runtime product master item-status removal on 2026-06-07: `/master-data/products` no longer exposes `à¸›à¸£à¸°à¹€à¸ à¸—à¸„à¸¥à¸±à¸‡à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸ˆà¸°à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‚à¹‰à¸²` in the filter row, table, form, export, or import contract. Migration `20260607141612_drop_products_item_status.sql` removes `products.item_status` and `idx_products_status`; runtime reads that previously selected the product column now rely on transaction/ledger `output_category` or default purchase-bill Stock lines to `RM` at write time. This keeps receiving warehouse routing on branch RM warehouse selection instead of product master metadata. Dev-target DB was applied and repaired in migration history on 2026-06-07; verification returned `0` remaining `public.products.item_status` columns and `1` migration-history row for `20260607141612`. Validation passed: `npm run prisma:generate --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`. Local unauthenticated HTTP smoke for `/master-data/products` returned the expected `307` login redirect.
Runtime stock product-relation hotfix on 2026-06-07: after dropping `products.item_status`, stock pages that still used Prisma `include: { products: true }` could fail by selecting the removed product column. `/api/stock/ledger`, `stockBalanceSnapshot()`, `/api/stock/convert`, `/api/stock/status-convert`, and `/api/stock/transfer` now select only the product display fields they actually use (`code`, `name`, and `metal_group` where needed). Stock Ledger category display now relies on `stock_ledger.output_category` instead of product master metadata. Validation passed with `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`; unauthenticated HTTP smoke for `/api/stock/ledger` returned the expected `401`.
Runtime purchase-bill WTI allocation UX follow-up on 2026-06-07: in `/purchase/bills` Stock receipt allocation, pressing `+ à¹€à¸žà¸´à¹ˆà¸¡à¹à¸–à¸§` from a PO-selected row now creates the next row as `Spot Buy` and pre-fills `qty` with the remaining unallocated WTI product-summary weight in the draft. This keeps the common "PO does not cover all receipt weight" path explicit without forcing the user to recalculate residual weight manually.
Runtime purchase/sales bill discount-field design follow-up on 2026-06-07: `/purchase/bills` and `/sales/bills` now treat `à¸ªà¹ˆà¸§à¸™à¸¥à¸”à¸—à¹‰à¸²à¸¢à¸šà¸´à¸¥` as a money input per `docs/design.md` instead of a raw `type=number` field. The shared transaction helper uses `type="text"` with `inputMode="decimal"`, sanitizes to numbers with at most 2 decimal places, shows editable draft text on focus, formats with comma + 2 decimals on blur, and keeps the field right-aligned.
Runtime purchase-bill scope checkpoint on 2026-06-06: the immediate blocker "à¸šà¸±à¸™à¸—à¸¶à¸à¸šà¸´à¸¥à¸£à¸±à¸šà¸‹à¸·à¹‰à¸­à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¹„à¸”à¹‰" is fixed in `/api/purchase/bills`. The stock PB write contract now treats `item.productId` as outward `products.code` only, resolves it once to internal `products.id`, and validates WTI receipt summaries by that resolved internal id without reparsing or falling back to legacy/internal ids. A follow-up UI save bug was also fixed: `/purchase/bills` options expose `receiptTicketId = weight_tickets.doc_no`, summary ids as `${doc_no}:${productCode}:${lineCount}`, and line ids as `${doc_no}:${line_no}`, so the API now resolves those outward keys back to internal bigint ids before writing `purchase_bill_receipt_allocations`; it no longer parses client receipt keys as bigint. Dev-target data cleanup followed the same rule instead of adding code fallback: 4 old test `purchase_bill_items.source_snapshot` rows (`PB012606-0001` through `PB012606-0004`) were backfilled from internal WTI/summary/line ids to outward WTI doc/composite keys, and the post-check count for numeric `source_snapshot.receiptTicketId` is now `0`. Live smoke evidence created `POB012606-0010`, `WTI012606-0007`, `PB012606-0005`, and `PB012606-0006`; PO status moved `Partially Received -> Received`, WTI status became `billed`, and both PB rows reached the approval queue. The same smoke then failed later at `/api/purchase/payments` with a PMT save/database error; that is Payment Flow scope and is intentionally not mixed into the purchase-bill fix. Current report is [testpurchaseflow_date.md](/docs/tasks/testpurchaseflow_date.md).
Runtime supplier-payment PMA split checkpoint on 2026-06-06: `/purchase/payments` now treats approved PMA rows as the UI/API selection identity, while the source PB/ADV document remains an explicit reference. Root cause for `PMA012605-0011/2` being unable to save was twofold: the payment queue exposed split PMAs from the same PB with the same row id/source doc key, and the shared daily doc-number schema rejected `/` even though PMA split documents are valid business doc numbers (`.../1`, `.../2`). `/api/purchase/payments` now returns PMA doc no as `id`/`approvalId`, requires `payment_approvals.source_doc_no_snapshot` as the PB/ADV reference instead of falling back to source table doc no, and resolves supplier party ids from either internal bigint id or valid supplier code without falling back to junk display values. The payment modal converts the selected PMA back to `sourceDocNo` only for the POST `billId`, keeps `lines[].approvalId` as the PMA doc no, and initializes single-account split amount in state instead of silently filling it during submit normalization. Browser QA on `localhost:3000/purchase/payments` for `PMA012605-0011/2` verified GET `/api/purchase/payments` = `200`, row supplier/à¸¢à¸­à¸” `1,000.00` correct, modal split total `1,000.00` matches net cash out, and an intercepted POST payload contains `billId = PB012605-0387`, `supplierId = SU0506`, `accountId = AC-MOVBEKA2NLYD`, `lines[0].approvalId = PMA012605-0011/2`, `lines[0].billId = PB012605-0387`, and `splits[0].amount = 1000`. The POST was intentionally aborted by Playwright to avoid creating a real PMT in dev data. Validation passed with `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`.
Runtime supplier-payment PMT checkpoint on 2026-06-07: the same `PMA012605-0011/2` was pushed through the real PMT write path in dev-target. The remaining POST `500 DATABASE_ERROR` root cause was transaction timeout `P2028`: `/api/purchase/payments` called `nextDailyDocNos('bank_statement', ...)` inside an interactive transaction, but the helper used the global Prisma client instead of the transaction client; with the local pool at `max=1`, generating the `BST` doc no waited on the active transaction until timeout. `nextDailyDocNo()` / `nextDailyDocNos()` now accept an optional Prisma client/transaction and `/api/purchase/payments` passes `tx` for the bank-statement doc numbers. Real browser QA then created `PMT012606-0001` from `PMA012605-0011/2` using account `AC-MOVBEKA2NLYD`; POST returned `200` with `paymentId = 311`. DB verification: `payment_approvals.PMA012605-0011/2` is `paid` with `payment_id = 311`; `payments.311` is active amount/net `1000`; `bank_statement.BST2606-0001` references voucher `PMT-599bcbbe-5965-417c-8ff6-6b104aaa94ff` and `PMT012606-0001` with `amount_out = 1000`; `purchase_bills.PB012605-0387` refreshed to `status = partial`, `paid_amount = 1000`, `payable_balance = 1712.6`; and `purchase_bill_status_logs` recorded `payment_recorded`. `/api/purchase/payment-history` returned `200` and includes payment `311`, `PMA012605-0011/2`, and `PB012605-0387`. Validation passed with `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`.
Runtime payment-approval source-derived pending checkpoint on 2026-06-07: `/daily/payment-approval` now treats `à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´` as source-derived rows from `PB / ADV / EXP`, not `PMA pending`. Migration `20260607014615_remove_pending_payment_approvals.sql` voided the 150 legacy `payment_approvals.status = pending` placeholder rows, added PMA status logs with action `legacy_pending_placeholder_voided`, changed `payment_approvals.status` default to `approved`, and hardened the DB constraint to allow only `approved`, `paid`, and `voided`. Source write paths for PB, ADV, and EXP no longer create or delete pending PMA placeholder rows; `payment-approval-pending.ts` now only owns PMA doc-number generation and source lock checks for `approved/paid` PMA rows. `/api/daily/payment-approval` GET computes pending balance from source current state: PB uses `purchase_bills.payable_balance - approved PMA amount` to avoid double-hitting paid PMAs, while ADV/EXP use source amount minus approved/paid PMA amount because those source tables do not carry the same payable-balance contract. POST now accepts source doc no as `approvalId`, looks up the source by `sourceType + doc_no`, and creates new `PMA approved` rows for the submitted split amounts instead of updating a pending PMA row. UI wording changed from `PMA pending` to `source à¸£à¸­à¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´`. DB verification after migration shows statuses `approved = 14`, `paid = 3`, `voided = 155`, column default `approved`, and constraint `payment_approvals_status_chk` allows only `approved/paid/voided`. Authenticated Playwright QA on `localhost:3000/daily/payment-approval` returned `/api/daily/payment-approval = 200`, no console errors, `source à¸£à¸­à¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´` visible, and `PMA pending` absent. Partial approval smoke used dev data `PB012605-0411`: before approval pending balance was `10.80`; POST approved `5.00` to `PMA012605-0162`; subsequent GET still shows source pending `PB012605-0411` with balance `5.80` and approved tab/source rows include `PMA012605-0162`. Validation passed with `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`.
Runtime expense approval split checkpoint on 2026-06-07: `EXP` now follows the same split-approval UI contract as `PB/ADV` in `/daily/payment-approval`. Migration `20260607025327_normalize_expense_status_contract.sql` normalized legacy `expenses.status` values from `pending`/blank to `pending_approval`, set `expenses.status` default to `pending_approval`, made it `not null`, and added `expenses_status_chk` for `pending_approval`, `approved`, `paid`, and `cancelled`; dev-target verification shows both current expense rows are `pending_approval`. Prisma now matches that status contract, and expense API mutation guards no longer treat `pending` or blank as valid pending states. `/api/daily/payment-approval` now returns active account destination options for pending expense rows, and `PaymentApprovalPageClient` uses the same split section for `PB/ADV/EXP`: select destination account, edit approval amount, add/remove split rows, and submit `splits[]` instead of auto-approving the full expense amount. Browser QA on `localhost:3000/daily/payment-approval` confirmed the expense tab has 2 rows, `EXP2605-0002-B` opens a modal with `à¹à¸šà¹ˆà¸‡à¸£à¸²à¸¢à¸à¸²à¸£à¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢`, destination account select, amount input, and adding a second split row works without console errors. Validation passed with `npm run prisma:generate --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`.
Runtime sidebar detail-route checkpoint on 2026-06-07: `AppNavigation` now treats a route as active when the current path equals the menu href or is a slash-bounded child of that href. This fixes purchase bill detail URLs such as `/purchase/bills/PB012606-0010` so the sidebar highlights the main `à¸šà¸´à¸¥à¸£à¸±à¸šà¸‹à¸·à¹‰à¸­` menu instead of losing the active state, and it also auto-expands the section containing the active route.
Runtime impurity master schema/API checkpoint on 2026-06-07: `/master-data/impurities` root cause was a schema/API drift after the bigint identifier cutover. Dev-target DB and Prisma use `public.impurities.id bigint generated by identity` as the internal FK key and `code` as the business key, but the active API still generated legacy string ids such as `IMP-###` into `id` and omitted `code` from read responses. Migration `20260607102230_normalize_impurity_code_contract.sql` normalizes existing codes, sets `impurities.code` to `not null`, and adds `impurities_code_not_blank`; dev-target verification shows `id bigint identity not null`, `code text not null`, `impurities_code_key` unique index, and `1/1` impurity rows with valid codes. API contract now returns `id` as a serialized internal bigint string for WTI/WTO FK payloads and returns required `code` separately; create inserts `(code, name, active)` and lets DB generate `id`, while edit/toggle parses route/form id strictly as bigint. No fallback to business code or display text was added for FK writes. Validation passed with `npm run prisma:generate --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`.
Runtime Cost Pool docs checkpoint on 2026-06-07: `docs/notes/Cost Pool.md` is now the canonical Cost Pool/Dual Costing rule note. It records copper/brass-only eligibility via `products.metal_group`, and narrows source eligibility to only `PO Buy` plus `Stock Spot Buy / No PO purchase bill`; PB lines that reference PO do not create an additional Cost Pool source. The note also records short-close/reversal rules, backend API contract, and the current implementation gap where `/api/dual-costing/cost-pool` still lacks `metal_group` filtering and still treats purchase bill items too broadly. `docs/notes/Purchase Flow.md` now links to the dedicated Cost Pool note instead of carrying the full rule inline, and `docs/notes/_Index.md` plus `docs/migration/00-doc-index.md` include the new canonical entry.
Runtime Cost Pool display checkpoint on 2026-06-07: `/api/dual-costing/cost-pool` now resolves PO Buy item display names from `products.name` by product code before falling back to item snapshots. This keeps the `/dual-costing/cost-pool` table column `à¸ªà¸´à¸™à¸„à¹‰à¸²` showing the product name instead of a product code/snapshot value when master data is available.
Runtime PMA/PMT timeline checkpoint on 2026-06-07: PMA/PMT now have dedicated business timeline tables and fact tables. Migration `20260607005615_add_payment_approval_and_payment_timeline_logs.sql` adds `payment_approval_status_logs`, `payment_status_logs`, `payment_allocations`, and `payment_account_splits`, with backfill from existing `payment_approvals`, `payments`, and `bank_statement` rows. PMA approve, PMA void-before-payment, PMT post, PMT bank-post, and PMT cancel now append timeline events; PMT save also writes allocation/split facts, and PMT cancel reverses those facts. Policy correction: cancelling a PMT no longer puts the old PMA back to `approved` for reuse; it voids the affected PMA unless another active payment still fully settles it, forcing source amounts back through approval as a new PMA. `/purchase/payment-history` now links PMT rows to `/purchase/payments/[id]` and shows PMA links to `/purchase/payment-approvals/[...id]`; both detail pages aggregate their dedicated logs/facts into timelines. Dev-target apply used direct `psql` against `apps/next/.env.local` `DATABASE_URL` because `supabase db push --dry-run` found many older local migrations missing from remote history; do not run `--include-all` casually for this repo. Apply output backfilled `29` PMA status logs, `293` PMT status logs, `4` payment allocation facts, and `307` account split facts, then `supabase migration repair 20260607005615 --status applied --db-url "$DATABASE_URL"` marked the remote migration history. Local validation passed with `npm run prisma:generate --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`.
Runtime WTI cancel/reuse checkpoint on 2026-06-06: `WTI012605-0004` was verified after cancelling related PB rows. The root issue was stale dev-target allocation facts left behind by cancelled purchase bills, not a need for runtime fallback. Migration [20260606080820_cleanup_cancelled_purchase_bill_allocations.sql](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/supabase/migrations/20260606080820_cleanup_cancelled_purchase_bill_allocations.sql) deletes `purchase_bill_receipt_allocations` and `purchase_bill_po_allocations` tied to `purchase_bills.status = cancelled`, then refreshes WTI billed/remaining/status from active allocations. Dev-target apply removed 5 cancelled receipt allocations and 5 cancelled PO allocations. Post-check: cancelled allocation counts are `0`, `WTI012605-0004` has `status = received`, `billed_weight = 0`, `remaining_weight = 70`, and authenticated `/api/purchase/bills` receipt options include `WTI012605-0004` with `remainingWeight = 70`. UI wording on `/purchase/bills` now says `à¸ˆà¸±à¸”à¸ªà¸£à¸£à¹ƒà¸™à¸šà¸´à¸¥à¸™à¸µà¹‰à¸„à¸£à¸šà¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§` so users do not confuse draft allocation with database usage status.
Runtime WTI full-allocation checkpoint on 2026-06-06: the Stock purchase-bill save path now enforces the superseded rule that every selected WTI product summary must be fully allocated in the PB before save. `apps/next/src/components/daily/TransactionBillsPageClient.tsx` blocks client submit when the draft summary total is under/over the selected `remainingWeight`, highlights/focuses the affected qty field, and shows a field-level Thai error. `apps/next/src/app/api/purchase/bills/route.ts` now rejects API POST/PATCH when requested summary qty is lower than the current available WTI summary qty, while still rejecting over-allocation and PO over-use. Validation passed with `git diff --check`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`. Browser smoke on `http://127.0.0.1:3000/purchase/bills` using an isolated unauthenticated profile reached the expected login redirect; authenticated form UAT for `UC-PUR-07/08` still needs a browser session with test login if deeper click-path evidence is required.
Runtime WTI usage-history checkpoint on 2026-06-06: `weight_ticket_usage_logs` is now the append-only usage history layer for `WTI -> PB`. Migration [20260606093408_add_weight_ticket_usage_logs.sql](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/supabase/migrations/20260606093408_add_weight_ticket_usage_logs.sql) creates typed allocation/release event rows with `event_key`, WTI doc no, target document, product snapshots, allocated gross/deduct/net weights, before/after remaining weight, actor, note, and FK links. Runtime WTI document-timeline checkpoint on 2026-06-06: `weight_ticket_status_logs` is now the append-only status/lifecycle history layer for `WTI/WTO`, and WTI detail timeline no longer uses `app_audit_logs` as document timeline. Migration [20260606121841_add_weight_ticket_status_logs.sql](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/supabase/migrations/20260606121841_add_weight_ticket_status_logs.sql) creates status event rows with `event_key`, `from_status`, `to_status`, WTI/WTO doc snapshot, weight snapshots, actor, note, and metadata. Dev-target apply via direct `psql` succeeded with `17` created baseline rows and `7` current-status sync rows, then `supabase migration repair 20260606121841 --status applied --db-url "$DATABASE_URL"` marked migration history. `/api/daily/weight-tickets` appends created logs, `/api/daily/weight-tickets/[id]` appends edit/cancel logs, and `/api/purchase/bills` appends `usage_status_changed` when PB create/edit/cancel recalculates WTI status from active allocations. `/api/daily/weight-tickets/[id]` now aggregates `weight_ticket_status_logs` + `weight_ticket_usage_logs` into `timeline`, while keeping `usageTimeline` for the WTI usage table. Verification for `WTI012605-0002`: DB status is `billed`, product summaries all have `remaining_weight = 0`, status timeline has `created received` and `status_synced received -> billed`, and combined timeline has 7 events including `PB012606-0009` plus previous `PB012605-0417` allocations. Direct unauthenticated API curl returned expected `401`, so authenticated browser/API payload verification remains pending.
Runtime PB receipt-item weight checkpoint on 2026-06-06: `PB012606-0009` exposed a display/data mismatch where active `purchase_bill_receipt_allocations` correctly allocated `WTI012605-0002` for `1,950` kg, but the related `purchase_bill_items` row still kept full WTI summary gross weight `2,060` kg in `gross_weight` and `source_snapshot.grossWeight`. `/api/purchase/bills` now builds Stock-from-WTI item gross/deduct weights proportionally from the selected WTI product summary and the line `qty`, so split WTI summary rows mirror their allocated receipt weights in the line display table while `purchase_bill_receipt_allocations` remains the allocation source of truth. Migration [20260606123631_normalize_purchase_bill_receipt_item_weights.sql](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/supabase/migrations/20260606123631_normalize_purchase_bill_receipt_item_weights.sql) normalized the 5 dev-target mismatched rows and repaired migration history; post-check mismatch count between PB item weights and receipt allocation weights is `0`. Current sample: `PB012606-0009` item and allocation both show gross `1,950`, deduct `0`, qty `1,950`, while linked `WTI012605-0002` remains `billed` with all summary remaining weights `0`.
Runtime partial-status wording question on 2026-06-06: user observed that wording/state around `à¸£à¸±à¸šà¸‚à¸­à¸‡à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§à¸šà¸²à¸‡à¸ªà¹ˆà¸§à¸™` can still appear, so docs now mark it as an unresolved business-rule question instead of confirmed target behavior. Current working assumption is that `à¸£à¸±à¸šà¸šà¸²à¸‡à¸ªà¹ˆà¸§à¸™` belongs to `POB` receiving progress, while `WTI` should represent a saved receipt document and use partial wording only as `à¸­à¸­à¸à¸šà¸´à¸¥à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§à¸šà¸²à¸‡à¸ªà¹ˆà¸§à¸™` based on active PB usage. Because new PB writes now require selected WTI/summary remaining weight to be fully allocated before save, the open decision is whether to remove/hide WTI partial states from new UI/API paths entirely, leaving `WTI.partially_billed` only for legacy active partial usage or edit/cancel recalculation that still has true partial usage.
Runtime ADV timeline checkpoint on 2026-06-06: `supplier_advance_status_logs` and `supplier_advance_allocation_logs` are now the append-only business timeline for `ADV`. Migration [20260606093705_add_supplier_advance_timeline_logs.sql](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/supabase/migrations/20260606093705_add_supplier_advance_timeline_logs.sql) creates typed status/allocation event rows with stable `event_key`, ADV/PB doc snapshots, before/after remaining amount, status transition fields, actor, note, and metadata. `appendSupplierAdvanceStatusLog()` records ADV create/edit/cancel, PMA approve/void, PMT paid/reversed, and allocation-status sync events; `appendSupplierAdvanceAllocationLogs()` records ADV allocation/release from purchase bills before active allocation facts are refreshed. `/api/purchase/advance-payments/[id]` now reads the detail timeline from these dedicated tables instead of fabricating history from `app_audit_logs` or current allocation rows. Dev-target apply succeeded via direct `psql` over `apps/next/.env.local` `DATABASE_URL`, backfilling `3` status rows and `0` allocation rows for the current ADV data set; `supabase migration repair 20260606093705 --status applied` then marked the remote migration history as applied. Sample rows include `ADVLOG-ADV012605-0001-0001|created|ADV012605-0001` and `ADVLOG-ADV012605-0001-0002|approved|ADV012605-0001`. Local validation passed with `npm run prisma:generate --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and scoped `git diff --check`.
Runtime document timeline policy checkpoint on 2026-06-06: the target architecture now treats every user-facing business document with a document number (`doc_no`, `document_no`, `voucher_no`, or `ref_no`) as requiring timeline/history. [Document Timeline Policy](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/docs/notes/Document%20Timeline%20Policy.md) records the rule: current document tables hold latest state, append-only event logs hold history, detail pages must expose timeline/history, event rows need stable `event_key`, and runtime code must not fabricate history from internal ids or fallback around bad data.
Runtime document history design checkpoint on 2026-06-06: the table design is now explicit that NS Scrap ERP should not use one generic `document_events` table as the source of truth for business timeline. [Document History Table Design](/Users/watcharathatsrithanesiganon/Documents/GitHub/ns-scrap-erp/docs/notes/Document%20History%20Table%20Design.md) sets the target: keep status/usage/allocation history separated by document or business flow (`weight_ticket_status_logs`, `weight_ticket_usage_logs`, `po_buy_allocation_logs`, `supplier_advance_status_logs`, `payment_approval_status_logs`, `payment_status_logs`, etc.), keep typed fact tables for enforceable details, and let UI/read services aggregate per-document timeline views. This is docs/design only; no schema migration was applied in this checkpoint.
Runtime document timeline coverage audit on 2026-06-06: current implementation is not complete against the new policy. Confirmed covered at first level: `po_buys` via `po_buy_status_logs`, `purchase_bills` via `purchase_bill_status_logs`, `weight_tickets` via `weight_ticket_status_logs` plus `weight_ticket_usage_logs` for `WTI -> PB`, `supplier_advance_payments` via `supplier_advance_status_logs` + `supplier_advance_allocation_logs`, `payment_approvals`/`PMA` via `payment_approval_status_logs` + `payment_allocations`, and `payments`/`PMT` via `payment_status_logs` + `payment_allocations` + `payment_account_splits`. Remaining partial gap for `weight_tickets`: `WTO -> SB` usage history is still future work. `stock_ledger` has `ledger_key` but still needs source-event trace. Missing dedicated document timelines: `po_sells`, `stock_issues`, `sales_bills`, `receipts`, `expenses`, `transfers`, `bank_statement`, `petty_advances`, `petty_advance_returns`, `receipt_vouchers`, `stock_adjustments`, `grade_adjustments`, `production_orders`, and `loan_payments`.
Runtime UI checkpoint on 2026-06-04: `/stock/status-convert` removed the extra in-page usage banner and now follows the shared list-page baseline more closely. The list adds header-click sorting, the standard count/page-size/pagination row above the table, and keeps the search/refresh/create actions in the shared filter shell. Current implementation is frontend pagination/sort over the loaded dataset only; no API-side paging or mutation behavior was changed.
Runtime schema checkpoint on 2026-06-05: Group A simple master migration is now complete for `bank_names`, `payment_methods`, `product_units`, and `product_types`. Each table keeps business-facing `code` as the UI/API identifier while the database primary key is now `id bigint generated by identity`. `apps/next/src/lib/server/simple-master-tables.ts` now supports code-keyed simple masters so the shared master-data pages still edit/toggle by `code`, and `public.payment_methods` consumers such as `/api/purchase/advance-payments` no longer leak internal bigint ids back to the client. Local validation passed with `npm run prisma:generate --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run lint --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and `git diff --check`.
Runtime Wave 2 fallout checkpoint on 2026-06-05: the focused ledger / PO reconciliation / purchase settlement fallout from the all-bigint DB cutover is now adapted in code. `apps/next/src/app/api/admin/transaction-ledger/route.ts` now resolves referenced records by internal bigint ids while still exporting outward strings/doc numbers, `apps/next/src/lib/server/purchase-bill-settlement.ts` now accepts bigint bill/advance ids, `apps/next/src/lib/server/po-buy-reconciliation.ts` now reconciles PO buys and status logs with bigint owner ids / bigint relation keys, and `apps/next/tmp-po-reconcile-test.ts` now calls the reconcile helper with bigint ids. Verification for this scoped batch: the active `type-check` output no longer reports these four files when filtered for `transaction-ledger`, `purchase-bill-settlement`, `po-buy-reconciliation`, and `tmp-po-reconcile-test`; broader daily/finance/report fallout remains pending outside this batch.
Runtime Wave 2 fallout checkpoint on 2026-06-05: the focused `dual-costing + trading` API batch is also adapted to bigint internals. `apps/next/src/app/api/dual-costing/cost-allocator/route.ts`, `cost-pool/route.ts`, `deal-margin/route.ts`, `match-log/route.ts`, `apps/next/src/app/api/trading/dashboard/route.ts`, and `trading/matching/route.ts` now keep bigint ids inside Prisma queries/maps, stringify only outward ids, and stop using internal ids as display fallbacks for product/party labels. Verification for this scoped batch: filtering `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false` output for those six route paths returns no matches.
Runtime Wave 2 weight-ticket fallout checkpoint on 2026-06-05: `apps/next/src/app/api/daily/weight-tickets/route.ts`, `apps/next/src/app/api/daily/weight-tickets/[id]/route.ts`, and `apps/next/src/lib/server/weight-tickets.ts` now conform to the DB-first bigint schema for WTI/WTO. The routes no longer seed manual string ids such as `WT-*` / `WTL-*` / `WTS-*`; `weight_tickets`, `weight_ticket_lines`, `weight_ticket_product_summaries`, and `weight_ticket_product_summary_lines` now use DB-generated bigint ids internally, while outward JSON stringifies ids only at the API boundary. Runtime Wave 2 sales-route fallout checkpoint on 2026-06-05: `apps/next/src/app/api/sales/bills/route.ts`, `apps/next/src/app/api/sales/po-sell/route.ts`, and `apps/next/src/app/api/sales/receipts/route.ts` now conform to the DB-first bigint schema for `sales_bills`, `po_sells`, and `receipts`. The routes no longer seed manual string ids such as `SB-*`, `POS-*`, or `RCP-*`; channel/product/bill/account/receipt refs are parsed to bigint before query/write; and outward JSON now stringifies only contract ids while keeping customer/branch/warehouse display fields on business code or display text only. Sales bill and PO Sell item snapshots no longer fall back to internal product ids for outward names, and customer receipt bank-statement linkage now uses the persisted bigint receipt id only as an internal string reference instead of pretending the receipt PK itself is a string. Scoped verification passed: `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false 2>&1 | rg "sales/bills|sales/po-sell|sales/receipts"` returned no matches, and `git diff --check -- apps/next/src/app/api/sales/bills/route.ts apps/next/src/app/api/sales/po-sell/route.ts apps/next/src/app/api/sales/receipts/route.ts` passed.
Runtime PO Buy event-log checkpoint on 2026-06-05: `po_buy_status_logs` has now been upgraded in-place from a minimal append-only status table to a structured event log. `apps/next/prisma/schema.prisma` and `supabase/migrations/20260605153000_upgrade_po_buy_status_logs_to_event_log.sql` add `event_key`, `action`, `from_status`, `to_status`, and `po_buy_doc_no` on the existing table and backfill legacy rows. `apps/next/src/lib/server/po-buy-reconciliation.ts` now writes PO Buy history through the structured event-log contract, and `/api/purchase/po-buy` now records create, edit, reconcile/receive, short-close, and cancel events with transition metadata instead of exposing raw bigint log ids. `PoBuyPageClient` detail history now renders action labels plus `from -> to` transition text using `event_key` as the outward row key. This keeps `po_buys` as current state and `po_buy_status_logs` as append-only history in the same table family without fallback to internal ids.
Runtime B5 account-selector closure checkpoint on 2026-06-05: the remaining active account-reference leak in supplier advance payments is now closed. `apps/next/src/lib/server/advance-payments.ts` now returns `fundingAccountId = accounts.code`, and `apps/next/src/app/api/purchase/advance-payments/[id]/route.ts` resolves the edited funding account by `accounts.code` instead of trying to parse client input as an internal bigint id. Runtime master-data contract hardening checkpoint on 2026-06-06: shared `simple-master-tables` now correctly separates `code-backed` masters from `auto-bigint` masters. `account_subtypes`, `directors`, `expense_types`, and `production_output_categories` now use outward `id = code` and update by `code`, while `machine_types`, `machines`, `production_lines`, `vat_settings`, and `wht_settings` stringify internal bigint ids on the way out and coerce them back to bigint on save/status update.
