- Current PO Buy table sizing follow-up: `/purchase/po-buy` also locks the `à¸§à¸±à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸à¸³à¸«à¸™à¸”à¸ªà¹ˆà¸‡` column to fit `YYYY-MM-DD` delivery dates without wrapping.
- Current PO Buy table sizing follow-up: `/purchase/po-buy` locks `à¸ªà¸–à¸²à¸™à¸°` to fit status labels and `à¸­à¸±à¸žà¹€à¸”à¸•à¸¥à¹ˆà¸²à¸ªà¸¸à¸”` to a compact metadata width.
- Current master-data follow-up: business-facing master codes and matching business IDs are now canonical uppercase. `/master-data/salespersons` uses `SA001`-`SA999`; manual input like `sales1`/`s1`/`sa1` normalizes to `SA001`, and new records use the generated `SA...` value for both `id` and `code`. `/master-data/suppliers` uses `SU0001`-`SU99999`; legacy-style `SUP1`/`s1`/`su1` inputs normalize to `SU0001`, and new records use the generated `SU...` value for both `id` and `code`.
- Dev-target data migration `20260520143001_uppercase_master_party_codes.sql` has remapped existing salespersons and suppliers so `id = code` in uppercase, changed supplier foreign keys to `ON UPDATE CASCADE`, and updated known `sales_id`/`supplier_id` references with zero orphan references after verification.
- Current supplier import follow-up: `/master-data/suppliers` now has an Excel import path that accepts the same `.xlsx` workbook produced by the supplier export sheet `à¸œà¸¹à¹‰à¸‚à¸²à¸¢`. Import is all-or-nothing, validates rows before write, maps `à¸œà¸¹à¹‰à¸”à¸¹à¹à¸¥` back to active salespersons by id/code/name, uses uppercase `SU...` as both `id` and `code`, and keeps address data in form/export/import while hiding the address column from the on-screen supplier table.
- Current customer/supplier round-trip follow-up: `/master-data/customers` now has Import Excel UI and `/api/master-data/customers/import`, matching the supplier import pattern. Customer and supplier imports accept the workbook produced by their export sheets, validate through the shared form schema, upsert by canonical business code/id, and generate new `CUS...` or `SU...` codes when a template row leaves the code blank.
- Current reference master cleanup: `/master-data/bank-names` and `/master-data/channels` no longer expose business code fields in the table/form/API response surface. Dev-target `public.bank_names`, `public.purchase_channels`, and `public.sales_channels` no longer keep separate `code` columns; their hidden primary keys remain for FK stability. Backup tables were created as `maintenance.reference_code_removal_backup_20260520090736_*`.
- Current supplier international-address follow-up: `public.suppliers` now has additive international address fields (`country_code`, `address_line1`, `address_line2`, `address_city`, `address_state_region`, `address_postal_code_intl`). `/master-data/suppliers` moves the domestic/foreign selector into the address section, requires visible country + address line 1 + city for foreign suppliers, keeps `country_code` internal/export-only when available, clears Thai address hierarchy fields for foreign writes, keeps `à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸­à¸¢à¸¹à¹ˆà¹€à¸•à¹‡à¸¡/à¸«à¸¡à¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸«à¸•à¸¸à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸­à¸¢à¸¹à¹ˆ` as address metadata, removes the general supplier note field from the modal/export/import surface, and changes `à¸˜à¸™à¸²à¸„à¸²à¸£à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` to a dropdown from `/master-data/bank-names`.
- Current supplier CSV replacement follow-up: dev-target supplier master was replaced from `docs/tasks/assets/reference/nsscrap permission and master data   - ผู้ขาย.csv` as the source of truth. The import generated uppercase `SU0001...` ids/codes for 1,871 active CSV rows, preserved 17 referenced old rows as inactive to keep transaction FKs valid, added missing bank masters (`à¸žà¸£à¹‰à¸­à¸¡à¹€à¸žà¸¢à¹Œ`, `à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸ªà¸”`, `à¸˜à¸™à¸²à¸„à¸²à¸£à¸­à¸­à¸¡à¸ªà¸´à¸™`, `à¸˜à¸™à¸²à¸„à¸²à¸£à¹€à¸žà¸·à¹ˆà¸­à¸à¸²à¸£à¹€à¸à¸©à¸•à¸£à¹à¸¥à¸°à¸ªà¸«à¸à¸£à¸“à¹Œà¸à¸²à¸£à¹€à¸à¸©à¸•à¸£`), defaulted blank owners to `PLOY`, and backed up the old supplier/FK state in `maintenance.supplier_replace_backup_20260520072518` and `maintenance.supplier_replace_fk_backup_20260520072518`. Follow-up repair restored bank/account values from the backup where the CSV was blank and name matching was reliable, and cleared all supplier tax IDs so the exported workbook can be keyed by users.
- Current supplier receiving-account follow-up: supplier credit term/limit has been removed from the supplier UI/API/Prisma model and dropped from `public.suppliers` after backup. Supplier receiving accounts now live in `public.supplier_bank_accounts` with `payment_method`: `à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸ªà¸”` stores no bank/account detail, while `à¹‚à¸­à¸™à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` requires a bank and digit-only account number. Dev-target normalization split 1,470 transfer rows, 282 cash rows, and 8 multi-account suppliers; cash markers were stripped from stored bank/account fields.
- Current supplier cleanup follow-up: unused `public.suppliers.notes` was dropped after backup `maintenance.supplier_notes_drop_backup_20260520121005`; supplier address metadata remains in `address` as `à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸­à¸¢à¸¹à¹ˆà¹€à¸•à¹‡à¸¡/à¸«à¸¡à¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸«à¸•à¸¸à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸­à¸¢à¸¹à¹ˆ`.
- Current supplier cleanup follow-up: unused `public.suppliers.version` was dropped after backup `maintenance.supplier_version_drop_backup_20260520121147`; supplier saves do not currently implement row-version optimistic locking.
- Current supplier receiving-account follow-up: account-level `branch_code` was added to `public.supplier_bank_accounts` after backup `maintenance.supplier_bank_accounts_branch_code_backup_20260520122022`; supplier modal now stores `à¸£à¸«à¸±à¸ªà¸ªà¸²à¸‚à¸²` inside each receiving account row.
- Current master-data follow-up: `/master-data/salespersons` form now auto-generates code and no longer exposes note, commission, or base salary fields. The target `salespersons` DB model no longer keeps `note`, `commission_pct`, or `base_salary`.
- Current master-data cleanup follow-up: customer master now follows the supplier-style party/address form and no longer uses contact person, bank account, or general notes. Product units/types no longer expose/store code; channels is now `à¸Šà¹ˆà¸­à¸‡à¸—à¸²à¸‡à¸‚à¸²à¸¢` over sales channels only; expense categories no longer use code or parent category; machines no longer use maintenance status and now use `/master-data/machine-types` as the DB-backed type source. Topbar branch selector now reads active `branches` through `/api/branches` instead of a placeholder/warehouse source, respecting current user branch scope where available. Supplier account copy uses a small inline SVG icon button with no CDN/dependency.
- i18n is recorded as a future separate architecture batch, not part of the current master-data cleanup, because it affects app-wide copy, validation messages, routing, and locale fallback.
- Current parity slice: `/sales/stock-issue` was revised after Transaction Bills. It restores the legacy amber/orange Pending Sale hero, four KPI cards, status filter, weight/cost/estimated-sale columns, status wording, and disabled convert/edit/cancel action shell. `/api/sales/stock-issue` only gained read status filtering and derived item quantity; no create/edit/convert/cancel/delete write behavior, stock-ledger reversal, or sales-bill generation was added.
- Current parity slice: `/purchase/payments` and `/sales/receipts` were revised after Pending Sale. The shared money-movement component now restores the first legacy Payment/Receipt Voucher shells with rose/emerald heroes, create CTAs, KPI cards, date/account/search filters, outstanding bill selector with party/amount prefill, voucher net summary, dense history table, footer totals, and disabled view/print action shell. Existing single-voucher POST behavior remains unchanged; multi-bill voucher, split accounts, print document generation, edit/delete/reverse, and allocation writes remain deferred.
- Current layout task: sidebar navigation scroll preservation was revised after user feedback. `AppNavigation` now restores saved sidebar scroll only once, avoids treating missing session storage as `0`, and suppresses programmatic scroll saves while still scrolling the selected route into view when needed.
- Current parity slice: `/purchase/receipt-vouchers` was revised after the sidebar task. It restores the first legacy Receipt Voucher read/print surface: blue info banner, compact search/create toolbar, KPI strip, seller/tax ID/purchase bill/weight/amount table columns, print/edit/delete action cluster with edit/delete disabled, legacy empty state, footer totals, and read-only print preview from existing DB fields. The API only exposes existing receipt voucher print/detail fields; create/edit/delete/save, purchase-bill prefill, company-profile print header wiring, and sync/write semantics remain deferred.
- Current receipt voucher design follow-up on 2026-05-31: `/purchase/receipt-vouchers` moved its explanatory Receipt Voucher copy to the app topbar and removed the in-page info banner. The list page now follows the active design shell more closely with summary KPI cards, one filter row (`à¸„à¹‰à¸™à¸«à¸²`, `à¸§à¸±à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆ`, clear, disabled create action), count/page-size/pagination above the table, and a denser white table shell while keeping the existing read-only print preview and disabled edit/delete actions unchanged.
- Current parity slice: `/daily/petty-advance` was revised after Receipt Vouchers. It restores the legacy purple/pink hero, five KPI cards, Top 10 recipient panel, compact filter/action bar, type/status chips, used/returned/remaining/action table shape, return modal date/account/notes fields, and read-only detail modal with summary plus return history. Existing create/edit/return writes are preserved; delete, expense allocation, linked-expense schema assumptions, and destructive cleanup remain deferred.
- Current parity slice: `/daily/bill-swap-history` was revised after Petty Advance. It restores the legacy rose/pink before-VAT report shell, KPI strip, supplier/product/bill search, before/after supplier names, derived weight, old/new price columns, old/new before-VAT amount columns, diff coloring, footer totals, and legacy empty state. The API only adds read joins for supplier names and purchase bill doc numbers; full legacy oldItems/newItems parity remains deferred because the target schema stores compact row fields.
- Current bill swap history design follow-up on 2026-05-31: `/daily/bill-swap-history` keeps the existing read-only report data but now follows the active list-page shell more closely with a standard filter row (`à¸„à¹‰à¸™à¸«à¸²`, `à¸§à¸±à¸™à¸—à¸µà¹ˆ`, clear), count/page-size/pagination row above the table, and denser KPI/table spacing per `docs/design.md`. Browser verification for this route is currently masked by an unrelated existing Next build error from `/app/stock/transfer/page.tsx` importing a missing `@/components/daily/StockTransferPageClient`.
- Current parity slice: Production report polish was revised after Bill Swap History. `/production/wip-report` now has age-days sorting/highlighting, `/production/report` has the legacy product output summary block, `/production/yield-loss-report` has the amber explanation and P&L impact card trio, and `/production/machine-utilization` has the formula banner plus Est.Hrs/à¸•à¹‰à¸™à¸—à¸¸à¸™ columns. All changes are read-only/report polish; branch/machine/status server filters, exact multi-output aggregation, loss reason wiring, and production mutations remain deferred.
- Current parity slice: `/daily/transfer` was revised after Production report polish. It restores the legacy compact filter card, direction account placeholders, clear-filter button, all/today/7-days/month period chips, inline count/total summary, rounded table shell, and explicit edit/delete action column while preserving the existing create/edit transfer POST flow. Delete remains disabled until a reviewed DELETE/tombstone/bank-statement cleanup path exists in the active Next API.
- Current transfer UI cleanup on 2026-05-31: `/daily/transfer` no longer shows the extra blue info banner (`à¹‚à¸­à¸™à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸£à¸°à¸«à¸§à¹ˆà¸²à¸‡à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µ â€” à¸ªà¸£à¹‰à¸²à¸‡ Bank Statement 2 à¸à¸±à¹ˆà¸‡`) above the filter card. The page now opens directly into the working filter/list surface instead of repeating explanatory copy.
- Current transfer design-alignment follow-up on 2026-05-31: `/daily/transfer` now leans on the active shared UI baseline for list pages. The search field, account filters, clear/create actions, summary row, table shell, numeric alignment, and modal field sizing were normalized to the `docs/design.md` filter/table/form tokens instead of continuing with page-local spacing/button/input variants.
- Current expense dashboard cleanup on 2026-06-04: `/daily/expense-dashboard` no longer shows the duplicate in-page gradient hero (`Dashboard à¸„à¹ˆà¸²à¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢`) above the working dashboard content. The route now opens directly into the period filter, KPI cards, anomaly section, and heatmap table, while the same title/subtitle now come from the global topbar via `PageTitleOverride`. Existing read-only dashboard behavior remains unchanged.
- Current expense-category follow-up on 2026-06-05: `/master-data/expense-categories` now exposes `à¸›à¸£à¸°à¹€à¸ à¸—à¸„à¹ˆà¸²à¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` again in the active app. The master list adds the new column, the create/edit form can store it as optional text, and additive schema support was restored via `expense_categories.type` without bringing back the removed parent-category field.
- Current expense-type master follow-up on 2026-06-05: `à¸›à¸£à¸°à¹€à¸ à¸—à¸„à¹ˆà¸²à¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` is no longer a free-text stopgap. The active app now has `/master-data/expense-types` as a dedicated master page, `/master-data/expense-categories` selects from that master via dropdown, and `expense_categories` stores the linked expense-type id while still showing the readable type name in the list.
- Current group-A master code follow-up on 2026-06-05: `bank_names`, `payment_methods`, `product_units`, `product_types`, `expense_types`, and `expense_categories` now all have dedicated `code` columns in the active schema/Prisma model. The shared simple-master pages for `/master-data/bank-names`, `/master-data/payment-methods`, `/master-data/product-units`, `/master-data/product-types`, and `/master-data/expense-types` now show/edit `code`, while `/master-data/expense-categories` also shows its own `code` and sends `expense_types.code` through the dropdown/UI layer before mapping back to `expense_type_id` internally. Dev-target/local DB migration `20260605005110_add_codes_to_group_a_masters.sql` was applied locally and backfilled existing group-A rows (`expense_categories.code` became `EXC-###`; existing bank/payment/unit/type ids were copied into `code`).
- Current master-data navigation follow-up on 2026-06-05: sidebar master-data navigation now groups expense masters under one parent menu `à¸„à¹ˆà¸²à¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢`. `/master-data/expense-categories` and `/master-data/expense-types` remain the same routes, but they now appear as sub menu items `à¸«à¸¡à¸§à¸”à¸„à¹ˆà¸²à¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` and `à¸›à¸£à¸°à¹€à¸ à¸—à¸„à¹ˆà¸²à¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` under the shared expense parent instead of two separate top-level master-data entries.
- Current expense master bigint pilot on 2026-06-05: `expense_types.id` and `expense_categories.id` were migrated from legacy `text` PKs to internal `bigint` PKs, and `expenses.category_id` was migrated to `bigint` FK in dev/local through `20260605014935_convert_expense_master_ids_to_bigint.sql`. Follow-up cleanup `20260605021000_drop_expenses_category_legacy_id.sql` has now removed the temporary legacy text trace columns (`expense_types.legacy_id`, `expense_categories.legacy_id`, and `expenses.category_legacy_id`) from local/dev-target DB because the active app no longer uses them. Master-data and daily-expense flows continue to use `code` at the API/UI boundary and map to internal bigint ids inside the server. Project rule was also tightened in `docs/agent-rules/database.md`: new target-schema tables should default to `bigint id` plus separate business `code` / `doc_no` fields unless a documented exception is approved.
- Current expense status follow-up: `/daily/expense` now uses the same business status vocabulary as ADV (`pending_approval`, `approved`, `paid`, `cancelled`) instead of the old `pending/paid` pair. The expense list/filter/detail/export flow now reads `expenses.status` as the main business state and mirrors `paid_status` only for compatibility with older downstream queries.
- Current expense modal follow-up on 2026-05-31: `/daily/expense` create/edit modal now shows both `à¸£à¸²à¸¢à¸¥à¸°à¹€à¸­à¸µà¸¢à¸”` and `à¸«à¸¡à¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸«à¸•à¸¸` as multi-line text areas instead of a single-line detail input. The modal writes to the existing `description` and `notes` fields only; no schema/API shape change was introduced.
- Current expense mutation follow-up on 2026-05-31: `/daily/expense` now allows `à¹à¸à¹‰à¹„à¸‚` and `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸` only while the document is still `pending_approval`. The list shows both actions only for pending rows, the edit modal no longer exposes a writable status dropdown, `POST /api/daily/expenses` refuses updates once the row has moved past pending approval, and `PATCH /api/daily/expenses/[id]` handles pending-row cancellation by marking the document `cancelled`.
- Current purchase bill ADV follow-up on 2026-05-31: `/purchase/bills` create/edit modal now includes a dropdown for `à¹€à¸­à¸à¸ªà¸²à¸£à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸¥à¹ˆà¸§à¸‡à¸«à¸™à¹‰à¸²/à¸¡à¸±à¸”à¸ˆà¸³` so office can select one ADV document to deduct before payment. The bill summary now shows `à¸¢à¸­à¸”à¸ªà¸¸à¸—à¸˜à¸´à¸à¹ˆà¸­à¸™à¸«à¸±à¸ ADV` and `à¸¢à¸­à¸”à¸ªà¸¸à¸—à¸˜à¸´à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸•à¹‰à¸­à¸‡à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢`. The API persists one active `supplier_advance_allocations` row per purchase bill for this flow, voids/replaces it on bill edit or bill cancel, refreshes `supplier_advance_payments.allocated_amount/remaining_amount/status`, and recalculates `purchase_bills.paid_amount/payable_balance/status` from both active ADV allocations and non-cancelled PMT rows so payment approval / supplier payment queues stay in sync.
- Current parity slice: `/stock/transfer` was revised after Daily Transfer. It restores the legacy compact filter bar with search/date/from-branch/to-branch controls, all/today/7-days/month period chips, inline count/weight summary, blue create CTA, seven-column table with disabled cancel shell, and red/emerald source-destination modal panels with sender/receiver/notes fields. Existing stock-ledger POST behavior is preserved; cancel/tombstone and sender/receiver persistence remain deferred until schema/write semantics are reviewed.
- Current stock transfer design-alignment follow-up on 2026-05-31: `/stock/transfer` now follows the active `docs/design.md` list/form baseline more closely instead of keeping page-local UI primitives. The page uses the shared filter shell (`Input`, `DatePickerInput`, shared buttons), a separate count/page-size/pagination row above the table, the shared lined table component, and a sectioned create modal (`à¸‚à¹‰à¸­à¸¡à¸¹à¸¥à¹€à¸­à¸à¸ªà¸²à¸£`, `à¸•à¹‰à¸™à¸—à¸²à¸‡`, `à¸›à¸¥à¸²à¸¢à¸—à¸²à¸‡`, `à¸£à¸²à¸¢à¸à¸²à¸£à¸ªà¸´à¸™à¸„à¹‰à¸²`, `à¸œà¸¹à¹‰à¸£à¸±à¸šà¸œà¸´à¸”à¸Šà¸­à¸šà¹à¸¥à¸°à¸«à¸¡à¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸«à¸•à¸¸`). Save validation now uses the central red-field pattern too: required/invalid fields show inline errors, red highlight, and auto-focus to the first invalid control.
- Current parity slice: removed legacy Pending Sales report from the target route set. LME persistence, matching/allocation writes, and sales-plan locks remain deferred to dedicated target schemas rather than the removed report.
- Current parity slice: `/anomaly-detector` was revised after the report parity batch. It restores category count tags, severity-colored group badges/titles/counts, legacy-like hero-to-KPI ordering, compact As-of read-only toolbar, and honest checklist copy for the 18 active rule groups. Legacy 40-check coverage and record-level jump/highlight remain deferred until missing read rules and target route highlight contracts are designed.
- Current parity slice: `/dashboard` data parity follow-up was revised after Anomaly Detector. The dashboard date range controls now request `/api/dashboard?from=&to=`, AR/AP aging buckets are computed server-side, cash composition includes FCD/AR/AP/OD/net cash, stock-by-branch and stock-by-group use stock ledger balances, and monthly trend rows drive dashboard chart panels. Branch/supplier/customer/product/group server filters and historical monthly baseline merge remain follow-up work.
- Current parity slice: `/dashboard` filter parity follow-up was revised after the data payload pass. Dashboard branch/supplier/customer/product/group controls now reload `/api/dashboard` with query parameters and the server filters purchase/sales KPIs and analysis rows read-only. The API now exposes active branches/groups/products as filter options; historical monthly baseline merge and fuller supplier/customer option lists beyond visible in-period rows remain follow-up work.
- Current parity slice: `/dashboard` historical data follow-up was revised after server filters. The dashboard now merges `historical_monthly` rows into revenue/COGS/expense totals and monthly trend rows (`pnl/revenue`, `pnl/cogs`, and `expense`) and shows actual merged amounts/row count in the historical indicator. Supplier/customer filter options now come from active master data rather than only top in-period rows. Remaining dashboard work is design-level statutory/GL reconciliation, not a safe read-only UI parity patch.
- Current unmapped legacy slice: remote-legacy-only views are being restored after the sidebar fix. `/dual-costing/waiting-allocations`, `/dual-costing/cost-allocation-ledger`, and `/dual-costing/report` now have dedicated read-only Next pages/APIs and sidebar entries. `/finance-accounting/asset-overview` now restores `trackAssetOverview` / Net Worth Track Asset as a management/read dark-shell baseline from existing Financial Dashboard + Cash & Others sources. No allocation write, reverse, stock mutation, GL posting, statutory P&L, or statutory balance-sheet behavior is added. The current remote-snapshot unmapped list is now closed; remaining items are design-level follow-ups.
- Current parity slice: Batch D Group C was revised after Company Profile and pushed as `dcfa1c1`. It restores Match Log's legacy `ðŸ“‹` info box, PO Sell filter shell, visible-row summary cards, and disabled Reverse action column; Deal Margin's match-status donut/legend, `PO Sell`/`Sell Qty` table shape, and legacy empty state; and Compare Margin's legacy first-screen order with date filters/row stats reduced after the core cards. No reverse/allocation mutation is enabled; Deal Margin `sellQty` is exposed as current `trading_deals.matched_qty` and documented in OpenAPI until normalized PO Sell allocation logs are designed.
- Current dual-costing design alignment follow-up on 2026-05-31: `/dual-costing/cost-pool`, `/dual-costing/cost-allocator`, `/dual-costing/match-log`, `/dual-costing/deal-margin`, `/dual-costing/compare-margin`, `/dual-costing/waiting-allocations`, `/dual-costing/cost-allocation-ledger`, and `/dual-costing/report` now share the active Next list/report shell pattern instead of page-local card/filter/table wrappers. All eight routes move explanatory copy into the app top bar via `PageTitleOverride`, use consistent white filter cards and KPI cards, and keep the dual-costing-specific color accents only where the legacy flow depends on them. `Cost Pool` now restores search + date filters in the active shell; `Cost Allocator` restores the missing step `â“ª` source selector shell before product selection; ledger/waiting/report management pages now use the same summary/filter/table rhythm as the other dual-costing pages. - Current follow-up slice: `/stock/ledger` row detail modal was implemented after UI-D3 and pushed as `181a2e5`. It turns the legacy `à¸­à¹ˆà¸²à¸™` action into a read-only modal using the existing ledger row payload; duplicate/orphan cleanup stays disabled and no stock write behavior is added.
- Current follow-up slice: PO Buy branch/warehouse cleanup is implemented. `/purchase/po-buy` create flow now uses required active `branches` instead of `warehouses`, generates new document numbers as `POB{branchCode}{YYMM}-NNNN`, writes `branch_id` directly, and leaves `warehouse_id`/`channel_id` null for new PO Buy rows. The active `/master-data/warehouses` page/API/config has been removed; `/master-data/branches` is the business-facing `à¸ªà¸²à¸‚à¸² / à¸„à¸¥à¸±à¸‡` master. The physical `warehouses` table is retained only for existing stock/purchase-bill history until a separate stock-location migration is designed.
- Current master-data cleanup slice: supplier receiving-account payment methods now come from `/master-data/payment-methods` instead of hardcoded options. Dev-target `bank_names` no longer contains `à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸ªà¸”`/`à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¹‚à¸­à¸™`, `payment_methods` no longer stores a code column, payment method names are bilingual such as `à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸ªà¸” (Cash)`, and supplier account rows use canonical `à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¹‚à¸­à¸™` while still accepting legacy `à¹‚à¸­à¸™à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` import text.
- Current purchase-bill modal follow-up: `/purchase/bills` now shows `à¸œà¸¹à¹‰à¸”à¸¹à¹à¸¥` in the blue WTI summary strip after selecting supplier + receipt, sourcing the name from the supplier master (`sales_rep`, with salesperson master fallback by `sales_id`) so office users can verify the responsible owner before pricing the bill.

- `à¸Šà¸™à¸´à¸”à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µ` à¸šà¸™à¸«à¸™à¹‰à¸² `/master-data/accounts` à¸–à¸¹à¸à¸œà¸¹à¸à¹€à¸›à¹‡à¸™à¸•à¸±à¸§à¹€à¸¥à¸·à¸­à¸à¸‚à¸­à¸‡ `à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¹‚à¸­à¸™` à¸­à¸¢à¹ˆà¸²à¸‡à¹€à¸”à¸µà¸¢à¸§à¹ƒà¸™ UI à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§; à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µ `à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸ªà¸”` à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¹à¸ªà¸”à¸‡/à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸•à¹‰à¸­à¸‡à¹€à¸¥à¸·à¸­à¸ subtype à¹à¸¡à¹‰à¸£à¸°à¸šà¸šà¸ˆà¸°à¸¢à¸±à¸‡ normalize à¸ à¸²à¸¢à¹ƒà¸™à¹€à¸›à¹‡à¸™ `cash` à¹€à¸žà¸·à¹ˆà¸­à¸„à¸¸à¸¡ validation à¹à¸¥à¸° backward compatibility.
- Current cash-account behavior follow-up: `/master-data/accounts` now hides `à¸›à¸£à¸°à¹€à¸ à¸—à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µ` until `à¸§à¸´à¸˜à¸µà¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢/à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` is selected, and keeps it hidden when the chosen payment method is `à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸ªà¸”`. Cash accounts still require `à¸ªà¸à¸¸à¸¥à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` because cash may exist in multiple currencies, while `à¸˜à¸™à¸²à¸„à¸²à¸£`, `à¸ªà¸²à¸‚à¸²à¸˜à¸™à¸²à¸„à¸²à¸£`, `à¹€à¸¥à¸‚à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µ`, and `à¸§à¸‡à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™ OD` remain hidden for cash. Save flow still auto-writes internal subtype `cash`.
- Current master-data cleanup slice: `currencies.code` and `overseas_remittance_purposes.code` were removed from dev-target after maintenance backups. Currency now uses internal `id` derived from uppercase `symbol`, and foreign-finance APIs still return a compatibility `code` value from the currency symbol for existing form consumers. Remittance purpose UI/API now exposes name/status only.
- Current system cleanup slice: Audit Log / Activity Log redesign was implemented and pushed as `7a07c7d`. Dev-target now has separate append-only `app_audit_logs` and `app_activity_logs` tables with RLS, `system.activity.view`, and append-only update/delete guards. Next writes user-management audit events to `app_audit_logs`, records page/branch activity through `/api/activity`, and `/admin/audit` reads a unified feed from the two new tables through the existing `/api/admin/auth-events` compatibility endpoint. Legacy `app_auth_events`, `audit_logs`, and deletion log tables are retained as history/compatibility sources, not the new source of truth.
- Current local runtime hardening follow-up on 2026-05-26: `/api/purchase/po-buy` and the PO options path inside `/api/purchase/bills` no longer run full `reconcilePoBuys()` passes across the whole PO set during ordinary GET/options page loads. Reconciliation remains on create/edit/cancel/short-close/write flows and on targeted PO validation before purchase-bill save, but read paths now trust stored `po_buys.remaining_*` / `status` values instead of recalculating thousands of rows per request. This removes the local `500` cascade where slow PO read-path reconciliation was causing `/api/auth/me`, `/api/activity`, and `/api/purchase/po-buy` to fail around the 5-second mark under concurrent dev-page loads. Remaining note: PO list reads are still heavier than ideal, but they now return successfully without the previous timeout/failure pattern.
- Current payment-approval bank-account selector follow-up on 2026-05-27: `/daily/payment-approval` AP rows now include supplier bank-account options from `supplier_bank_accounts`. When a supplier has more than one active bank account, the `à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µà¸˜à¸™à¸²à¸„à¸²à¸£` column can switch the destination account directly from the table via a local selector. This is a UI-only choice for now; approval write flow/audit persistence has not been added yet.
- Current payment-approval bank-account fallback follow-up on 2026-05-27: `/api/daily/payment-approval` now synthesizes a fallback bank-account option from legacy `suppliers.bank_account` / `suppliers.bank_name` when `supplier_bank_accounts` has no matching row yet. This keeps the table-side dropdown usable during master-data transition instead of collapsing back to plain text because the normalized bank-account child rows are still incomplete.
- Current payment-approval workflow decision on 2026-05-27/28: the business flow is locked as `à¸šà¸´à¸¥à¸£à¸±à¸šà¸‹à¸·à¹‰à¸­ -> à¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´à¹‚à¸­à¸™à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™ -> à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™ Supplier`. A newly created purchase bill with `payable_balance > 0` must appear in `/daily/payment-approval` first. Approval creates a persistent snapshot record, freezes the approved amount and destination bank account, and feeds `/purchase/payments` from approved snapshots only. `/purchase/payments` is the active queue surface: each approved row can either `à¸—à¸³à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` or `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸` before payment. The resulting `à¸£à¸²à¸¢à¸à¸²à¸£à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` must be treated as one lifecycle with statuses `à¸£à¸­à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢`, `à¹€à¸ªà¸£à¹‡à¸ˆà¸ªà¸´à¹‰à¸™`, and `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§`; `/purchase/payment-history` is the read-only snapshot history of rows that have already left the queue.
- Current payment-approval implementation follow-up on 2026-05-27: dev-target now has `public.payment_approvals` plus `payments.payment_approval_id`, applied locally from `supabase/migrations/20260527103000_add_payment_approvals.sql`. `/api/daily/payment-approval` now writes persistent AP approval snapshots for `purchase_bills`, freezes the selected destination bank account and approved amount, and returns both `pending` and `approved` rows from persisted state instead of UI-only selection. `/purchase/payments` now reads only approved snapshots, links each payment row back through `payment_approval_id`, and marks the approval `paid` when its approved amount is fully settled. Important settlement rule: `approved_amount` is the AP settlement amount, so on payment save the sum of `cash amount + withholding tax + discount` must not exceed the approved amount. Local browser/API smoke passed with `PB012605-0035 -> PMA012605-0001 -> PMT012605-0291`; after payment, the approval disappeared from both `/daily/payment-approval` and `/purchase/payments`, and DB verification showed `payment_approvals.status='paid'` plus `purchase_bills.payable_balance=0`.
- Current supplier-payment queue rule on 2026-05-28: `/purchase/payments` top table is the only active queue surface for approved supplier payments. The row action pair is now `à¸—à¸³à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` + `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸`; this `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸` means cancel the queued approval item before payment exists, using the approval snapshot id, and it returns the purchase bill to `/daily/payment-approval` as `à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´`.
- Current supplier-payment snapshot rule on 2026-05-28: a queued supplier-payment item cancelled from `/purchase/payments` is still part of the same payment lifecycle and should be represented in `/purchase/payment-history` as a cancelled snapshot row, not treated as â€œno historyâ€.
- Current supplier-payment history implementation follow-up on 2026-05-28: `/api/purchase/payment-history` now merges `payment_approvals.status='voided'` rows that never created a payment into the history read model as `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§` snapshot rows. Local browser/API verification passed with `PB012605-0417 / PMA012605-0004`: cancelling the queue item from `/purchase/payments` removed it from the payment queue, returned the bill to `/daily/payment-approval` as `pending`, and added a history row with doc no `PMA012605-0004`, amount `2689.00`, destination bank snapshot `à¸˜à¸™à¸²à¸„à¸²à¸£à¸à¸£à¸¸à¸‡à¹„à¸—à¸¢ Â· 555555`, and status `cancelled`.
- Current approval doc-number follow-up on 2026-05-28: `payment_approvals` now has user-facing `doc_no` separate from the UUID `id`. Approval rows use `PMA{branchCode}{YYMM}-NNNN`, and each approval cycle must create a fresh snapshot row with a fresh `PMA` number. `payment_approvals.doc_no` should therefore remain unique. When a queued approval is cancelled, the old approval snapshot stays in history with status `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§`; when the bill is approved again later, the new approval snapshot must get a new `PMA` number instead of reusing the old one.
- Current re-approval source-of-truth clarification on 2026-05-28: when a queued payment item is cancelled from `/purchase/payments` and later approved again, the new `payment_approvals` row must be rebuilt from the current `purchase_bills` state plus the bank account the user selects at that time. The cancelled `payment_approvals` row is history only; it must not be cloned as the source of truth for the next approval cycle.
- Current approval doc-number repair checkpoint on 2026-05-28: `supabase/migrations/20260528190000_restore_payment_approval_doc_no_unique.sql` has now been applied to local dev-target. It renumbered the old duplicate PMA snapshot safely, restored `uq_payment_approvals_doc_no`, and verified the `PB012605-0417` approval chain as `PMA012605-0004` = cancelled, `PMA012605-0005` = cancelled, `PMA012605-0006` = current approved row.
- Current pending-approval queue clarification on 2026-05-28: rows still in `à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´` are not snapshots. `/daily/payment-approval` must keep reading them from the current `purchase_bills` state, so if a purchase bill is edited before approval, the queue must immediately reflect the latest payable amount, supplier details, and selectable destination bank accounts.
- Current purchase-bill lock rule on 2026-05-28: once a purchase bill has an active `payment_approvals.status='approved'`, the bill must be locked against edit and cancel. It becomes editable again only after the queued payment item is cancelled and the bill returns to the `à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´` queue. Re-approval after that must create a fresh `PMA` snapshot.
- Current purchase-bill list follow-up on 2026-05-28: `/purchase/bills` now needs an explicit payment-workflow status surface separate from ordinary payment status. The list/detail should make `à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´` vs `à¸£à¸­à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` visible to users, and the same workflow state is the source for disabling `à¹à¸à¹‰à¹„à¸‚/à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸` actions on the bill.
- Current purchase-bill filter follow-up on 2026-05-28: the purchase-bill segmented status filter must be aligned to the payment-workflow state set, not only the raw bill settlement status. Required visible states are `à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´`, `à¸£à¸­à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢`, `à¸Šà¸³à¸£à¸°à¸šà¸²à¸‡à¸ªà¹ˆà¸§à¸™`, `à¹€à¸ªà¸£à¹‡à¸ˆà¸ªà¸´à¹‰à¸™`, and `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸`.
- Current purchase-bill segmented-filter batch on 2026-05-28: implementation still needs to finish `/purchase/bills` end-to-end so the API, segmented filter values, labels, and browser behavior all use the same derived payment-workflow state. Required derive order is `cancelled -> paid -> partial_paid -> pending_payment -> pending_approval`. This batch is not complete until the page can filter each of the 5 states correctly and validation passes again.
- New purchase-flow requirement on 2026-05-28: the target system must support `à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸¥à¹ˆà¸§à¸‡à¸«à¸™à¹‰à¸² / à¸¡à¸±à¸”à¸ˆà¸³` before final purchase billing. Example case: supplier sends one truck weighed only as a gross total on a large scale, detailed inbound weighing is not finalized yet, but the business still pays an advance first. Later, when creating the purchase bill, the form must allow selecting advance-payment entries so the bill can settle against them before sending only the remaining net payable into payment approval.
- Clarified on 2026-05-28: the advance-payment form needs real large-scale source fields, not just finance fields. Required minimum input now includes large-scale doc no, in/out dates, plate, optional vehicle photo, customer name, product name, in/out/net weights, price per kg, remark, scale operator, sender, driver, and the advance amount to pay, plus finance fields such as branch, supplier, payment date, payment method, and funding account.
- Clarified on 2026-05-28: after an advance-payment record is created, it must enter the same payment-approval queue (`à¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™`) as ordinary payable items, because it still requires approval before actual payout.
- Clarified on 2026-05-28: this queue should now be treated as business-level `à¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™`, not only `à¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´à¹‚à¸­à¸™à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™`, because it must accept at least `à¸šà¸´à¸¥à¸£à¸±à¸šà¸‹à¸·à¹‰à¸­`, `à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸¥à¹ˆà¸§à¸‡à¸«à¸™à¹‰à¸² / à¸¡à¸±à¸”à¸ˆà¸³`, and `à¸„à¹ˆà¸²à¹ƒà¸Šà¹‰à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` in one approval surface.
- Clarified on 2026-05-28: once a purchase bill is created with allocated advance payments, its initial workflow state depends on the remaining net payable after deducting those advances. If net payable remains, the bill starts at `à¸¢à¸±à¸‡à¹„à¸¡à¹ˆà¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´`; if the advances fully settle the bill, the bill goes straight to `à¹€à¸ªà¸£à¹‡à¸ˆà¸ªà¸´à¹‰à¸™` and must not enter payment approval.
- Clarified on 2026-05-28: if allocated advance payments exceed the final bill amount, the excess must go through an advance-refund flow first. The target system should not automatically carry that excess forward as supplier credit yet. Using the excess as a discount/offset against another bill is explicitly out of system for now.
- Clarified on 2026-05-28: advance-refund for overpayment belongs to a dedicated `Supplier` flow/menu. It should not reuse the refund surface from sales/customer receipt flows because the business direction and accounting semantics differ.
- New approval-queue requirement on 2026-05-28: approval must support `split payment approval`. A single payable source may be split into multiple approval items by payment method/account. Example target behavior: after a 1,000,000 bill has 800,000 advance already settled, the remaining 200,000 can be approved as 50,000 cash + 100,000 transfer account A + 50,000 transfer account B. Each split becomes its own `payment_approvals` row and its own `à¸£à¸­à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` row.
- New implementation batch on 2026-05-28: convert approval from one-amount-per-document behavior into `approval item` behavior. `/daily/payment-approval` must allow mixed split items in one approval cycle, persist one `payment_approvals` row per split item, and make `/purchase/payments` show each split as its own queued row.
- Documentation split on 2026-05-28: payment/approval/advance/refund rules are now separated into `docs/notes/Payment Flow.md`. `Purchase Flow.md` remains canonical for PO/WTI/Purchase Bill/stock-side rules, while `Payment Flow.md` is canonical for `PMA/PMT`, approval queue, split approval items, advance payment, and supplier-side advance refund.
- Open task batch on 2026-05-28 for split approval / advance payment:
  1. rename queue/menu wording from `à¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´à¹‚à¸­à¸™à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` to `à¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™` in active Next surfaces
  2. design additive schema for advance-payment header + later allocation-to-bill facts
  3. redesign `/daily/payment-approval` UI from single amount editor to split-item editor
  4. extend `/api/daily/payment-approval` write path so one approval cycle can create many `payment_approvals` rows
  5. adapt `/purchase/payments` to list and pay each split approval item independently
  6. preserve bill/workflow locking while any split item remains `approved`
  7. add browser smoke for mixed split methods: cash + two transfer accounts
- Current advance-payment implementation slice on 2026-05-28: `/purchase/advance-payments` is being added as the create/list surface for `à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸¥à¹ˆà¸§à¸‡à¸«à¸™à¹‰à¸² / à¸¡à¸±à¸”à¸ˆà¸³`. The additive schema starts with `supplier_advance_payments` for `ADV...` documents and `supplier_advance_allocations` for later many-to-many allocation into `purchase_bills`. This first slice stores source large-scale fields and finance intent only; it does not post bank statement rows and does not yet feed purchase-bill allocation.

- Current payment-approval/ADV queue follow-up on 2026-05-29: the active Next queue/menu wording is now `à¸­à¸™à¸¸à¸¡à¸±à¸•à¸´à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™`. - Current tax config follow-up: dev-target now has configurable `vat_settings` and `wht_settings` master/config tables. VAT remains seeded with VAT 7%. WHT now keeps the standard active rates `1% (à¸‚à¸™à¸ªà¹ˆà¸‡/à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸«à¸¡à¸²)`, `2% (à¹‚à¸†à¸©à¸“à¸²)`, `3% (à¸šà¸£à¸´à¸à¸²à¸£)`, `5% (à¸„à¹ˆà¸²à¹€à¸Šà¹ˆà¸²)`, `10% (à¸•à¹ˆà¸²à¸‡à¸Šà¸²à¸•à¸´)`, and `15% (à¸”à¸­à¸à¹€à¸šà¸µà¹‰à¸¢/à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸›à¸±à¸™à¸œà¸¥)`, with `WHT 3% (à¸šà¸£à¸´à¸à¸²à¸£)` as the active default calculation row. `/admin/system-settings` manages VAT as the primary active-rate card and WHT as an editable per-rate table under the System module. `/purchase/bills` reads the active VAT percent from DB, displays that percent in the modal summary, calculates VAT from that rate instead of hardcoded `7`, and stores the rate used in `purchase_bills.vat_rate_percent`. `/purchase/payments` now reads the active default WHT config for supplier payments, displays WHT as read-only, and recomputes WHT in the API before save.
- Current UI pattern note: shared list/filter/table/pagination/button/wording conventions now live in `docs/design.md`. Add or change reusable UI rules there instead of recording them only in this worklog.
- Current UI foundation follow-up: the active Next app now has a shadcn-style component foundation under `apps/next/` (`components.json`, `src/lib/utils.ts`, `src/components/ui/Button.tsx`, `Input.tsx`, `Select.tsx`, `Table.tsx`, `Tooltip.tsx`, `Dialog.tsx`, and upgraded `Card.tsx`) while keeping NS Scrap ERP colors and density. `/purchase/bills` and `/sales/bills` now consume shared UI components for filter/search/date/page-size/actions, the main list table shell, export tooltip, and the cancel-bill confirmation dialog instead of page-local class strings.
- Current UI foundation follow-up 2: `/purchase/payments`, `/purchase/payment-history`, and `/purchase/po-buy` now also consume the shared shadcn-style UI layer for high-reuse shells: search/date/account filters, page-size selectors, previous/next pagination actions, primary/secondary/export buttons, main list table containers, and amber tooltip behavior. `PoBuyCancelModal` now uses the shared dialog primitive; larger data-entry/detail modals still keep their existing flow for now to avoid widening the change set.
- Current UI foundation follow-up 3: modal/form adoption is now partially expanded too. `MoneyMovementPageClient` local form helpers (`BillSelect`, `Field`, summary select fields, modal action buttons) now sit on top of shared `Button/Input/Select`. `PoBuyFormModal` and `PoBuyDetailModal` now use the shared `Dialog` + `Table` + `Button` + `Input/Select` primitives instead of page-local modal shells, while keeping the existing wording, colors, and dense ERP spacing.
- Current UI foundation follow-up 4: `MoneyMovementPageClient` payment entry modal itself now sits inside the shared `Dialog` primitive, and its inner line-item grid/split controls use shared `Input/Select/Table/Button` much more consistently. `SupplierSearchCombobox` in `PoBuyPageClient` now uses the shared `Input` base too, so both list pages and modal entry surfaces are converging on one primitive set instead of only the outer page chrome.
- Current UI foundation follow-up 5: `MoneyMovementPageClient` now has the payment modal split into smaller feature-level sections (`PaymentSplitsSection`, `PaymentLinesSection`) instead of one giant render block, and `apps/next/src/components/ui/SearchCombobox.tsx` now exists as a reusable base primitive. `PoBuyPageClient` supplier search now consumes that shared combobox base rather than maintaining its own dropdown/input behavior inline.
- Current UI foundation follow-up 6: the payment modal section extraction is now a real file-level split under `apps/next/src/components/daily/MoneyMovementFormSections.tsx` rather than local helper functions, and numeric tooltip table cells now have a shared primitive at `apps/next/src/components/ui/TableNumberCell.tsx`. `PoBuyPageClient` uses that shared numeric cell instead of its own page-local implementation.
- Current UI foundation follow-up 7: the remaining `MoneyMovementPageClient` helper controls are now also split into `apps/next/src/components/daily/MoneyMovementFieldHelpers.tsx` (`BillSelect`, `Field`, `SelectField`, `SummaryPill`). At this point the page file is mostly orchestration/state/render composition, while reusable UI pieces have moved to feature/UI helper files.
- Current UI foundation follow-up 8: shared primitives are now used across another major transaction surface too. `TransactionBillsPageClient` supplier/product searchable dropdowns now sit on `apps/next/src/components/ui/SearchCombobox.tsx`, and its main numeric cells use `apps/next/src/components/ui/TableNumberCell.tsx` for consistent tooltip/width behavior instead of page-local implementations.
- Current UI foundation follow-up 9: `TransactionBillsPageClient` now also moves its bill-form field wrappers into `apps/next/src/components/daily/TransactionBillsFieldHelpers.tsx`. Supplier/product search, select fields, summary lines, and common input wrappers are no longer defined inline in the page file, and several bill/sales form inputs now sit directly on shared `Input`/`Select` wrappers while keeping the same labels, uppercase handling, and dense modal layout.

- Superseded AP payment-history wording clarification on 2026-05-28: purchase payment history remains a read-only snapshot surface for the full `à¸£à¸²à¸¢à¸à¸²à¸£à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢` lifecycle that has left the queue. The original wording used `à¹€à¸ªà¸£à¹‡à¸ˆà¸ªà¸´à¹‰à¸™` / `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§`; the current user-facing wording is now `à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§` / `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸` per the 2026-06-09 checkpoint above. If a cancelled item later goes through `approve again -> pay again`, the system must create a new payment/voucher row and keep the old cancelled row for audit. Downstream accounting/report posting must consume only successful payment rows.
- Superseded AP payment-history hardening slice on 2026-05-28: `/purchase/payment-history` stays GET-only as the read model route. The history UI now exposes status filtering (`à¸—à¸±à¹‰à¸‡à¸«à¸¡à¸”`, `à¸ˆà¹ˆà¸²à¸¢à¹à¸¥à¹‰à¸§`, `à¸¢à¸à¹€à¸¥à¸´à¸`), keeps cancelled rows visible for audit, and uses row-click detail modal instead of an in-page action button.
- Current master-data accounts wording follow-up on 2026-05-28: `/master-data/accounts` now treats bank-based company accounts as `à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¹‚à¸­à¸™` in the UI instead of `à¸˜à¸™à¸²à¸„à¸²à¸£`, while keeping DB values (`cash`, `bank`, `other`) unchanged. `à¸šà¸±à¸à¸Šà¸µà¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¸šà¸£à¸´à¸©à¸±à¸—` table display now uses a Thai label field so FCD/OD accounts can stay under the `à¹€à¸‡à¸´à¸™à¹‚à¸­à¸™` type without changing stored data.
- Superseded purchase stock-routing follow-up: product master no longer exposes `products.item_status` / `à¸›à¸£à¸°à¹€à¸ à¸—à¸„à¸¥à¸±à¸‡à¸—à¸µà¹ˆà¸ˆà¸°à¸£à¸±à¸šà¹€à¸‚à¹‰à¸²`. Warehouse master still stores `warehouses.type` as metadata, but purchase bill `STOCK` routing is controlled by the selected branch's active RM warehouse, not product metadata or user manual selection. Current rule is `/purchase/bills` auto-selects the active RM warehouse by selected branch, locks the warehouse field read-only, writes that warehouse to `purchase_bills.warehouse_id` and Stock `stock_ledger.warehouse_id`, and keeps `stock_ledger.output_category` from the transaction write path. No runtime fallback from warehouse name/code/type/hint is allowed.
- Current purchase bill modal UI follow-up: the `à¸‚à¹‰à¸­à¸¡à¸¹à¸¥à¸šà¸´à¸¥ > à¸ªà¸²à¸‚à¸²` field in `/purchase/bills` now keeps the shared combobox behavior but renders with an input-group control and chevron trigger so the modal field matches the newer searchable selector pattern without changing topbar branch selection.
- Current branch field UI foundation follow-up: the modal-style branch selector has been extracted to reusable `@/components/ui/BranchSelectCombobox` for non-topbar forms. `/purchase/bills` purchase and sales bill modals now consume the shared branch field instead of inlining branch combobox markup.
- Current branch preference follow-up: `/purchase/bills` now reads the saved branch selection from local storage on page mount and uses it as the default branch when opening create purchase/sales bill modals, while still validating that the saved branch exists in the active branch master list before applying it.

This installs the composition required by the official Radix date-picker docs without replacing the existing `Button.tsx`.

- Current date-picker action follow-up: shared shadcn-style `DatePicker` and `DatePickerInput` wrappers now include optional `Clear` and `Today` actions in the popover footer so filter/form date fields can match the desired basic date-picker behavior without custom per-page markup.
- Current date display follow-up: shared `DatePickerInput` now uses `dd/MM/yyyy` display (`24/05/2026`) while keeping API-facing values normalized as `YYYY-MM-DD`. Active transaction/purchase/sales filters and form date fields moved onto the shared date picker where they previously still used native `type="date"` inputs, and key list/detail tables in purchase bills, PO buy/sell, AP/payment approval, stock ledger/transfer, petty advance, daily expense, receipt vouchers, and trading matching now render dates through `formatDateDisplay` so read paths show the same `dd/MM/yyyy` pattern as the inputs.

Product master no longer shows, imports, exports, filters, or saves `item_status`; `stock_ledger.output_category` must come from transaction context instead of product master metadata.
