- 2026-07-05: Report/list tab-filter layout sweep checkpoint
  - UI-only sweep applied the multi-table layout convention from `docs/design.md` across active report/list surfaces: `/dual-costing/waiting-allocations`, `/finance-accounting/depreciation`, `/trading/matching`, `/reports`, `/finance/ar`, `/finance/ap`, `/trading/dashboard`, `/profit-cost-analysis`, and `/po-reports/outstanding`.
  - What is what: line tabs are the active data surface selector, filter cards are the search/date/status controls for the current report, and pagination/reset-width/tool actions belong to the active table surface.
  - Why it stays this way: users should choose the table context first, then see the filter controls directly before the rows they control. This prevents shared filters from floating above the tab selector and looking detached from the active table.
  - `/profit-cost-analysis` keeps its page-level KPI and dashboard summary panels above the table tabs, then renders line tabs, filter card, and active table. `/po-reports/outstanding` keeps tab-specific remaining KPI cards under the PO buy/sell tabs because those totals depend on the selected PO direction.
  - Runtime API behavior, filter state, formulas, exports, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.
  - Browser/computer-use QA was not run per the current instruction to avoid browser verification unless explicitly requested.

- 2026-07-05: WTI/WTO dashboard page checkpoint
  - Added `/daily/weight-ticket-dashboard` under the same Daily Transactions category as `/daily/weight-ticket-list`, with exact permission `daily.weight_tickets.view` and menu label `Dashboard ใบรับ-ส่งของ`.
  - Added read-only `GET /api/daily/weight-ticket-dashboard` using `weight_tickets`, `weight_ticket_product_summaries`, and active `stock_holds` to show total documents, WTI received weight, WTI remaining/waiting PB, WTO shipped weight, WTO pending out, status summary, branch summary, product summary, and attention rows.
  - What is what: the dashboard is an operational monitor; WTI remaining comes from summary `remaining_weight`, while WTO pending out comes from active stock holds. The list/detail page remains the working surface for document search, print/share, edit/cancel, and downstream handoff.
  - Why it stays this way: users asked for a separate page instead of overloading the list. Keeping this dashboard read-only avoids changing WTI/WTO write flows and keeps cost-sensitive data out of the high-level scan page.
  - Runtime write behavior, WTI/WTO save/edit/cancel/confirm, print/share, LINE notification, stock ledger writes, purchase bill, sales bill, Supabase schema, and DB state were not changed.
  - Validation passed: targeted ESLint for the new API/client/page/navigation files and `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`.
  - Browser/computer-use QA was not run per the current instruction to avoid browser verification unless explicitly requested.

- 2026-07-06: Supplier ADV VAT dropdown follow-up
  - `/purchase/advance-payments` now shows the `VAT` dropdown for both ADV types: `มัดจำส่งของรอคัดแยก` and `มัดจำล่วงหน้ายังไม่ส่งของ`.
  - Switching the ADV type back to `มัดจำส่งของรอคัดแยก` no longer forces `vatType` back to `ไม่มี VAT`; it only clears `invoice no`, because invoice is specific to the invoice-advance case.
  - The form summary now shows the selected VAT mode for all ADV types and shows subtotal/VAT breakdown whenever `มี VAT` is selected.
  - Runtime DB/API contract was already VAT-aware from the previous ADV batch; this follow-up is UI and documentation alignment only.

- 2026-07-06: Dashboard Overview UI checkpoint
  - `/dashboard` was renamed in the active shell from `Financial Dashboard` to `Dashboard Overview`.
  - The dashboard UI was aligned to the legacy/prototype Dashboard reference from `https://sirimasth.github.io/ns-scrap-erp/`: desktop now uses a white/neutral global filter card with visible branch/group/supplier/customer/product filters, mobile uses a compact quick-range card plus `MobileFilterSheet`, then a gradient hero, calmer white/accent KPI cards, and the same management-read section order.
  - Follow-up polish from full-page review: desktop filter controls now show visible labels for branch/group/Supplier/Customer/product, the global filter surface is no longer a heavy black strip, date inputs expose readable text, the clear-filter action is neutral instead of orange, the hero badge no longer repeats the page title, empty Historical notices are hidden, KPI cards under the hero are calmer white/accent cards without misleading status dots, the Aging table card spans wider, Top Suppliers/Customers are grouped under a `Top Ranking` section, and duplicate purchase/sales totals were removed from the lower detail metric sections.
  - What is what: `/dashboard` is the main management overview for purchase, sales, cash/bank, AR/AP, stock, trends, aging, top suppliers/customers, and section metrics.
  - Why it stays this way: users need to see the dashboard scope/filter first, then the overview numbers, before reading detailed widgets. The page remains a read-only report surface and must not become a finance/accounting posting page.
  - Runtime formulas, `GET /api/dashboard`, permissions, database schema, source tables, and DB state were not changed.
  - Validation passed: `npm exec --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- eslint src/components/main/MainDashboardsPageClient.tsx src/lib/navigation.ts`, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false`, `npm run build --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next`, and targeted `git diff --check` (only existing LF/CRLF warnings).

- 2026-07-07: Dashboard Overview table-density follow-up
  - `/dashboard` now keeps the overview/risk panels visible first, then moves lower detail-heavy surfaces into tabs: `อันดับคู่ค้า`, `Stock`, and `ตัวชี้วัดย่อย`.
  - Top Supplier and Top Customer ranked tables now show 5 rows by default and expose a blue expand/collapse action to reveal up to 10 rows only when needed.
  - The hero color was softened to a muted slate-blue/teal gradient and the visible title is now `Dashboard Overview` with Thai context below, reducing both the dark/heavy feel and the too-bright neon-blue glare while preserving the read-only management overview purpose.
  - Runtime formulas, `GET /api/dashboard`, filters, permissions, database schema, source tables, and DB state were not changed.

- 2026-07-07: Tracking 360 filter-card alignment follow-up
  - Superseded by the tab semantics follow-up below: view selectors that switch tables/data surfaces belong in shared line tabs, not filter-card segmented controls.
  - What stays: `/tracking/customer`, `/tracking/product`, and `/tracking/supplier` keep the shared global filter card for search/date range/master-data filters and export action.
  - Why it changes: `รายการ`, `Top 10`, `รายปี`, and Supplier `สรุปตามสินค้า` are data surfaces, not filter conditions.
  - Runtime API behavior, filter query state, formulas, exports, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.

- 2026-07-07: Weight Ticket Dashboard filter-card alignment follow-up
  - Superseded by the tab semantics follow-up below: `/daily/weight-ticket-dashboard` now uses line tabs for `ภาพรวม`, `WTI รับเข้า`, `WTO ส่งออก`, and `สรุปสินค้า`.
  - What stays: the filter card keeps only real date/branch scope controls, quick range, and navigation/create/refresh actions.
  - Why it changes: those dashboard choices switch data surfaces, while quick range/date/branch are actual filters.
  - Runtime API behavior, filter query state, WTI/WTO row split, formulas, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.

- 2026-07-07: Multi-table tab semantics follow-up
  - Controls that switch between multiple tables/report surfaces are line tabs again, not filter chips. Applied this to `/tracking/customer`, `/tracking/product`, `/tracking/supplier`, and `/daily/weight-ticket-dashboard`.
  - What is what: tabs choose the active data surface; filter cards hold search/date range/master-data/quick-range/status controls plus page actions.
  - Why it stays this way: users need to distinguish “which table am I viewing” from “how is this table filtered,” and the shared line-tab pattern already exists for that job.
  - Runtime API behavior, query state, formulas, exports, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.

- 2026-07-08: Daily Report visual-first table/filter follow-up
  - `/daily-report` was inspected in the rendered browser first, then the implementation in `MainDashboardsPageClient` was adjusted as a UI-only follow-up.
  - What is what: the date row is the report scope filter, the purchase/sales KPI cards show daily weight/amount/average/GP, the bill tables show the actual document rows and count, and cash movement remains the daily cash-flow breakdown.
  - Why it stays this way: the date filter should use the shared neutral filter-control treatment; bill tables should use the single neutral table shell/header; KPI cards should not repeat the bill count already visible in the bill table title; table headers should not repeat totals already visible in KPI cards.
  - Fixed visible issues: removed the black `วันนี้` button treatment, removed duplicate bill-count pills from KPI cards, removed duplicate bill totals from purchase/sales table headers, normalized purchase/sales/cash table headers to slate, tightened empty states, and guarded daily report numeric display so missing values render `0.00` instead of `NaN`.
  - Follow-up visual alignment: the purchase average and sales GP helper text now renders as a full-width KPI card footer instead of sitting under only the amount column, so the two KPI cards stay visually balanced on desktop and mobile.
  - Runtime API behavior, daily-report formulas, filters, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.
  - Validation passed during the follow-up: targeted ESLint for `MainDashboardsPageClient.tsx`, workspace type-check, workspace build, and authenticated Playwright screenshots for desktop/mobile `/daily-report`.

- 2026-07-08: Analytics Dashboard bar-label readability follow-up
  - `/analytics-dashboard` daily purchase vs sales bar chart now separates the colored fill bar from the numeric label. Values render centered on the full progress/donate-style track instead of being clipped inside the variable-width fill, and the separate white pill around the number was removed after visual review.
  - What is what: the fill bar communicates relative size, while the centered text on the track/fill communicates the exact money amount.
  - Why it stays this way: small or zero values must remain readable and the two chart columns should not look cramped or uneven just because the fill width is narrow. The chart should look like a progress bar, not a label chip placed inside a chart.
  - Follow-up mobile QA also found the fixed share/print action buttons covering chart rows on mobile. They now float only on large screens and render in normal content flow on smaller screens so actions do not cover charts, tables, cards, or bottom navigation.
  - Runtime API behavior, analytics formulas, date filters, exports/share actions, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.
  - Validation passed: targeted ESLint, workspace type-check, authenticated desktop/mobile Playwright screenshots for `/analytics-dashboard`, and scoped `git diff --check`.

- 2026-07-08: Analytics Dashboard table-density follow-up
  - `/analytics-dashboard` was visually reviewed after the bar-label change and had too many table surfaces stacked vertically. The top flow now keeps filters, KPI cards, the daily purchase/sales progress chart, and group-value breakdown visible first, then moves the row-level detail tables into shared line tabs.
  - What is what: `คู่ค้า` shows Top suppliers and Top customers, `สินค้า` shows Top inbound and outbound products, and `Sale` shows purchase totals by salesperson. These are detail contexts, not separate always-visible dashboard panels.
  - Why it stays this way: the page should read as an analytics dashboard, not a long report made of many tables. Users can choose the detail context they need while still keeping the primary overview and key breakdown available.
  - Runtime API behavior, analytics formulas, date filters, exports/share actions, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.

- 2026-07-10: UAT `/purchase/bills` supplier-advance schema alignment
  - Investigated `https://ns-erp-uat.vercel.app/purchase/bills` after Vercel request logs showed `GET /api/purchase/bills 500` on the UAT deployment that serves branch `uat`.
  - Verified the deployed code expects the newer supplier-advance VAT breakdown fields in `apps/next/src/app/api/purchase/bills/route.ts`, specifically:
    - `public.supplier_advance_payments.advance_type`, `invoice_no`, `vat_type`, `vat_rate_percent`, `subtotal_amount`, `vat_amount`, `total_amount`
    - `public.supplier_advance_allocations.allocated_subtotal_amount`, `allocated_vat_amount`, `allocated_total_amount`
  - Verified the UAT Supabase database was still missing those columns, so the route could not read supplier-advance references for purchase bills and crashed at runtime.
  - Applied `supabase/migrations/20260706093000_add_supplier_advance_type_vat_breakdown.sql` directly to the UAT database to align schema with the already-deployed code.
  - What is what: the `supplier_advance_payments` / `supplier_advance_allocations` pair is now the source of truth for advance-payment type, VAT/subtotal/total breakdown, and per-bill allocated subtotal/VAT/total values shown in purchase-bill ADV flows.
  - Why it has to be like this: `/purchase/bills` now computes ADV options and remaining values from subtotal/VAT-aware amounts, so deploying the code without the matching schema guarantees runtime failure. This is a schema-environment issue, not a page-layout issue.
  - No application code or runtime fallback was added in this checkpoint; the fix was to bring UAT schema up to the contract already required by the deployed code.

- 2026-07-10: Sales Commission drilldown table-density follow-up
  - `/sales-commission` salesperson drilldown keeps all 4 verification tables, but no longer stacks them as one long table wall and no longer renders two table panels inside one tab.
  - What is what: `ยอดรวมตามหมวด` shows Table 1, `ยอดได้คอม` shows Table 2, `ผู้ขาย` shows Table 3, and `รายการสินค้า` shows Table 4.
  - Why it stays this way: when a drilldown has many table surfaces, users should choose the decision context first through shared line tabs; each business verification table gets its own tab so no table disappears and no tab becomes a two-table wall.
  - Runtime API behavior, commission formulas, filters, CSV export data, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.
- 2026-07-11: WTI/WTO draft lifecycle completion
  - WTI/WTO detail page and detail modal now expose the matching confirmation action (`ยืนยันรับของ` / `ยืนยันส่งของ`) while status is `draft`.
  - Draft WTI is not counted as `รอเปิด PB`; draft WTO is not counted as `รอออก SB` or pending-out follow-up. Downstream dashboard KPIs start only after confirmation.
  - LINE auto-send no longer runs on create/edit. It runs after the successful `draft -> received/delivered` transition, and manual LINE send rejects draft documents.
  - Printed draft documents remain available for checking but carry a visible draft/unconfirmed badge.
  - Added persisted action permissions for create, update, confirm, cancel, and share. Existing roles that had `daily.weight_tickets.view` receive the action permissions through migration `20260711133000_add_weight_ticket_action_permissions.sql`; runtime APIs do not fall back to view permission.
  - Applied the permission migration to dev-target `fhglqymcdmrgbsbadnwr`; verification found 6 active weight-ticket permission codes, no view-enabled role missing the confirm action, and migration history version `20260711133000` recorded.
  - What is what: Draft is a saved, editable work document; confirmed WTI/WTO is the business event that may enter PB/SB follow-up and notification flows.

- 2026-07-11: WTI/WTO form type-switch safety
  - Switching between WTI and WTO now resets the complete form after explicit confirmation when any business data has been entered. Branch, supplier/customer, header warehouse, line warehouses, products, weights, notes, and attachments are no longer carried across document types.
  - Draft WTO detail now states `ยังไม่จอง stock` beside the confirmation action. The reservation remains confirmation-owned; saving a draft does not reserve inventory.
  - What is what: WTI and WTO are separate document contracts even though they share one creation surface. WTI uses Supplier and a required physical Godown text; WTO uses Customer and a Warehouse ID per line. WTI stock Warehouse is resolved automatically downstream.
  - Why it has to be like this: carrying values across document types can create a visually valid but semantically invalid document. A full reset makes the type boundary explicit and prevents stale party/warehouse data from crossing the contract.

- 2026-07-11: WTI/WTO attachment payload boundary
  - New vehicle and line evidence images are uploaded to the configured WTI/WTO Supabase Storage bucket through authenticated `POST /api/daily/weight-tickets/attachments` before the document payload is saved.
  - The write payload now stores only compact file metadata (`fileName`, public `url`, and `storageKey`) in the existing image reference arrays. It no longer stores new image binary Data URLs in transaction rows.
  - Upload accepts only JPEG, PNG, and WebP up to 10 MB and requires WTI/WTO create or edit permission. Missing Storage configuration fails explicitly; there is no bucket-name fallback in the upload route.
  - Existing Data URL rows remain readable as migration-boundary legacy data so historical WTI/WTO evidence and PDF/LINE output do not break. New uploads do not use that legacy encoding.
  - What is what: Supabase Storage owns image bytes; WTI/WTO transaction rows own references to evidence used by the document.
  - Why it has to be like this: embedding image bytes in PostgreSQL arrays inflates request bodies, transaction rows, backups, and notification work. A Storage reference keeps the transaction contract small while preserving the current document/PDF/LINE read flow.
  - The reusable attachment grid and its attachment preview contract were extracted from the large create/edit page into `WeightTicketAttachmentGrid.tsx`. This is a behavior-preserving first split that keeps image UI and accepted file types owned by the attachment module.
  - Why it has to be like this: saving incomplete field data must not create billing workload, snapshot outbound cost, or notify external LINE recipients before the responsible user confirms the physical receipt/delivery.

- 2026-07-13: Financial LINE notification checkpoint
  - New bills saved successfully through `POST /api/purchase/bills` and `POST /api/sales/bills` now enter the existing LINE job/retry flow as `purchase_bill/PB` and `sales_bill/SB`. LINE failures are isolated after the bill transaction and do not change the successful bill response.
  - PB/SB/PMT/RCP routing is fail-closed: only an active rule whose `documentTypes` explicitly contains the matching financial type can receive that document. Financial notifications do not use the default/all-active-target fallback.
  - `/admin/line-settings` now exposes constrained WTI/WTO/PB/SB/PMT/RCP selectors, and the admin API validates those document type values before saving a rule.
  - Added compact dark LINE Flex cards with at most five product rows, party, branch, warehouse, Sale/channel when available, total, actual paid/received balance status, and a bill-detail link. Tax IDs, bank accounts, COGS, and GP are intentionally excluded.
  - Added an aggregated PMT Flex card with PMA/source documents, payout-account snapshots, payment totals, masked destination account, and the payment-history link. Internal payment/voucher/source IDs and unnecessary tax/contact data are excluded.
  - Added an aggregated RCP Flex card with active SB allocations, receiving-account code/name/amount rows, receipt/discount/WHT/AR/fee/net totals, and the receipt-history link. Only active RCP/allocation facts are eligible; full account numbers, internal IDs, tax/contact/address data, pending RCPs, and cancelled RCPs are excluded.
  - What is what: `line_targets` owns registered LINE group IDs, `line_notification_rules` owns document-to-group mapping, and `line_notification_jobs`/`line_notification_attempts` own post-commit delivery and retry history.
  - Why it has to be like this: WTI/WTO are operational receipt/delivery documents while PB/SB/PMT/RCP are financial documents. Explicit document-type routing prevents a financial document from leaking to an unrelated group, and post-commit delivery prevents a LINE outage from rolling back ERP data.
  - Dev-target `fhglqymcdmrgbsbadnwr` now has five explicit temporary rules. `WTI,WTO`, `PB`, `PMT`, and `RCP` point to `ทดสอบ`; `SB` points to `Peach, NAMPEC Official, Meng Watcharathat🤖`. The RCP resolver returned only `ทดสอบ`; official LINE validation returned HTTP 200, and live job `168` reached `sent` on attempt 1 with no duplicate source-target job. Replace only the rule targets when dedicated customer groups are registered.
  - Latest validation passed: RCP notifier/Flex/routing/jobs/API coverage within full Vitest `65/65`, workspace type-check, full ESLint with zero errors (one pre-existing warning in `scripts/qa-thai-font.tsx`), npm audit with zero vulnerabilities, scoped diff check, real dev-target RCP reconciliation/privacy checks, official LINE push validation HTTP 200, and a production build with `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096` plus constrained workers completing all 306 static pages.
  - Remaining dev-target environment risk: `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` is not configured, so the delivered RCP Flex used the `http://localhost:3000` fallback for its open-system button. Delivery is verified, but that button remains unusable from a phone until this environment receives its real public app URL.
  - Customer readability follow-up: LINE Flex does not allow a custom font family, so the RCP card now uses LINE's native font with a dark header, light `#f8fafc` body, white footer, `md` spacing, and selective `md/bold` emphasis only for the customer and key receipt totals. Normal text/body contrast is 17.06:1, labels are 7.24:1, and the darker `#047857` financial/status green is 5.24:1 on the body and 5.48:1 on white. A full-width rectangular multicolor highlight treatment was trialed against the supplied reference, but the user selected the earlier light-green rounded tabs as the final layout: `SB / ตัดลูกหนี้`, `บัญชีบริษัทที่รับเงิน`, and `สรุปยอดรับเงิน`. These three tabs group the sections without adding background fills to the individual financial rows. The official validator and final direct preview push to `ทดสอบ` both returned HTTP 200; privacy fields and routing were unchanged.

- 2026-07-12: Dashboard/production visual consistency checkpoint
  - In Codex Browser, verified and corrected Thai-first visible copy in `/dashboard`, `/daily-report`, `/analytics-dashboard`, `/production/dashboard`, `/production/orders`, and `/production/report`; dashboard/report formulas, filter semantics, export behavior, APIs, permissions, schema, and business data were not changed.
  - Production dashboard now uses shared Lucide KPI icons and mobile line tabs. Production order rows keep the first index column left-aligned and every later data/status/action column right-aligned, with Thai labels and widened non-wrapping work-in-progress/yield columns.
  - Targeted ESLint and workspace type-check passed for `MainDashboardsPageClient.tsx`, `ProductionReportPageClient.tsx`, and `ProductionOrdersPageClient.tsx`.
  - Current browser audit blocker: the existing in-app Browser session began receiving 404 responses for authenticated `/api/auth/me`, `/api/activity`, and `/api/daily-report` calls after reload, even though unauthenticated shell probes confirm those local routes exist and return 401. Do not treat later page screenshots as authenticated QA until the user signs in again in Codex Browser or the session recovers.

- 2026-07-12: Production dashboard and order-table visual consistency checkpoint
  - Verified `/production/dashboard`, `/production/orders`, and `/production/report` in Codex Browser. Dashboard was checked in Desktop/Mobile and Light/Dark; the order list was checked in Desktop/Mobile with live rows.
  - `/production/dashboard` now uses Thai-first KPI/chart/machine wording, shared Lucide KPI icons, and shared mobile line tabs. `/production/orders` keeps the leading index column left-aligned and all later data/status/action columns right-aligned, with non-wrapping Thai headers for work-in-progress and yield.
  - No production formulas, filters, exports, APIs, permissions, database schema, or business data changed. Targeted ESLint and workspace type-check passed after the UI-only edits.

- 2026-07-13: Dashboard, calendar, cash summary, and Tracking 360 consistency checkpoint
  - UI-only Thai-first pass updated `/owner-daily`, `/daily-report`, `/profit-cost-analysis`, `/cash-flow-calendar`, `/business-calendar`, `/cash-others-summary`, `/tracking/customer`, `/tracking/supplier`, and `/tracking/product`.
  - What is what: working labels, line tabs, detail-section titles, table headers, and compact summary cards now use one Thai vocabulary; business acronyms such as AR, AP, COGS, GP, and WTI remain where they are the established business term.
  - Why it stays this way: table/filter/detail wording must remain Thai-first, and all list/data-surface switches must use the shared line-tab pattern. Cash & Others mini summaries now delegate to the shared `KpiCard` instead of maintaining a page-local card markup.
  - No formulas, filters, export URLs, APIs, permissions, database schema, or business data changed.
  - Validation passed: targeted ESLint for the seven touched components, `npm run type-check --workspace @ns-scrap-erp/next -- --pretty false` with `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096`, and scoped `git diff --check`.

The normal type-check then completed without TypeScript errors. Codex Browser still exposed a stale tab binding during this checkpoint, so browser visual revalidation remains required after the session is refreshed.

- 2026-07-13: App shell header-divider alignment checkpoint
  - The desktop sidebar brand header no longer exceeds the 72px main topbar: its vertical padding is reduced from `py-4` to `py-3.5`, removing the one-pixel intrinsic-height overflow caused by the 40px logo plus padding and border.
  - Dark mode now gives the sidebar divider the same `--ns-dark-border-strong` color used by the main topbar, so the shared boundary stays continuous across the sidebar/main junction. Light-mode divider treatment remains unchanged.
  - What is what: the sidebar brand header and main page topbar are separate flex columns that must terminate at the same shell row boundary.
  - Why it stays this way: equal 72px boxes and one dark-mode divider token prevent a visible step or color break without changing navigation, authentication, page content, or responsive behavior.
  - Validation passed: targeted ESLint, Next workspace type-check, production build (306 static pages), scoped diff check, and a Chromium geometry regression proving `73/72px` before versus `72/72px` with zero bottom delta after.

- 2026-07-13: Sales Commission overview-card regression recovery
  - Restored the user-approved 2026-07-10 salesperson card presentation on `/sales-commission`: a two-column inner metric grid, colored text without generic KPI icons, and a full-width `ค่าคอมเดือนนี้` card. The current drilldown-tab handler remains intact.
  - The eight overview KPI cards now follow the existing page-flow baseline of four cards per desktop row, producing two readable rows instead of one compressed eight-card row; tablet/mobile behavior remains responsive through the existing two-column base layout.
  - Filter usability follow-up adds `วันนี้ / 7 วัน / เดือนนี้` quick ranges and a reset action, formats bill/Supplier counts as integers, and lets users filter salesperson cards by `ทั้งหมด / มีรายการ / ได้คอม` without changing the API query contract or commission calculations.
  - Root cause: commit `64c6751e` (`Align sales plan client with Mind version`) replaced `MainSalesControlClients.tsx` broadly and changed the already-approved Sales Commission card from `SalesCardMetric` to five vertically stacked generic `Metric` cards; merge `f217727f` then carried that remote file result over the local layout.
  - What is what: the overview card is the salesperson comparison/entry surface; the later four-tab drilldown, filters, pagination, export, API payload, commission formulas, permissions, and database state remain separate and unchanged.
  - Why it stays this way: restoring only the two presentation hunks preserves the accepted visual hierarchy without checking out an old whole file and losing newer Sales Plan or Sales Commission behavior. `docs/design.md` now records this card as an explicit non-generic layout baseline.
  - Validation passed: red/green source-shape regression check, targeted ESLint, Next workspace type-check, production build (306 static pages), and scoped diff check. Authenticated browser comparison remains unavailable because the current browser test session redirects `/sales-commission` to login.

- 2026-07-14: Purchase payment sticky-action divider cleanup
  - `/purchase/payments` removes the adjacent resize handle from `อายุเอกสาร (วัน)` and the vertical border on the sticky `จัดการ` header/body cells, so the boundary no longer renders as a stray stroke when the queue is empty.
  - What is what: `อายุเอกสาร (วัน)` remains sortable and keeps its approved width; `จัดการ` remains sticky and visually separated from the payment data columns.
  - Why it stays this way: the sticky background already keeps the action column readable while scrolling; a resize indicator or persistent vertical border at the same edge adds visual noise and becomes a floating line in the empty state.
  - No payment data, sorting behavior, actions, APIs, permissions, LINE routing, or database state changed.
  - Validation passed: source contract check, targeted ESLint, Next workspace type-check, npm audit with zero vulnerabilities, and scoped diff check.

- 2026-07-14: Sales receipt history trust/readability follow-up
  - `/sales/receipts` history KPI totals now exclude cancelled RCP rows. With status `ทั้งหมด`, cancelled documents remain visible for audit but contribute zero to `ยอดรับแล้ว`, WHT, bank fee, and `เงินเข้าสุทธิ`.
  - Entry-tab KPI scope is now the outstanding queue (`ยอดค้างรับ` and `บิลค้างรับ`), while history KPI scope follows the active history filters only; the two business contexts are no longer mixed in one card row.
  - History account filtering now matches every persisted account split, so selecting a secondary receiving account no longer hides its RCP row or understates the filtered KPI totals.
  - Empty date fields now show `วว/ดด/ปปปป` instead of a realistic fixed date. The later user-approved naming override restores the page title/breadcrumb to `รับเงิน Customer`.
  - The history grid now uses one shared table shell, resets receipt-history saved widths to the new `v7` baseline, reserves readable widths for the full Thai WHT/bank-fee headers, puts `หมายเหตุ` before the rightmost `จัดการ` column, uses `text-sm` for primary row values, and changes edit/cancel row actions to light outlines.
  - What is what: active RCP rows represent settled receipt facts; cancelled RCP rows are retained audit evidence after their cash/AR effects were reversed; outstanding SB balances belong to the entry queue, not history totals.
  - Why it stays this way: reversed documents must not inflate cash-in summaries, an empty filter must not look selected, and a finance table should keep primary values readable while destructive actions remain visually secondary until confirmation.
  - No receipt create/edit/cancel API contract, LINE routing, permissions, database schema, or database state changed.
  - Validation passed: red/green KPI/account-filter regression tests, full Vitest `67/67`, full ESLint with zero errors (one pre-existing warning in `scripts/qa-thai-font.tsx`), Next workspace type-check, npm audit with zero vulnerabilities, source contract check, scoped diff check, production build with all 306 static pages generated, and the local navigation/login/RSC guard across 106 extracted routes with zero failures.
  - Visual browser UAT was not rerun for this implementation batch; the earlier audit attempt could not attach to the local authenticated browser session.

- 2026-07-14: Sales receipt naming and queue-spacing follow-up
  - Restored the official `/sales/receipts` menu, page title, and breadcrumb label to the user-approved `รับเงิน Customer`.
  - Kept task-level wording such as the `รับเงินลูกค้า` tab, buttons, and modal copy unchanged because those labels describe the receipt action rather than the page identity.
  - The desktop receipt queue now gives every column an explicit resizable width and a full-table width instead of letting the final `จัดการ` column absorb all widescreen remainder. Removed the customer cell's fixed `max-w-72` cap so available table space can show the full customer name before ellipsis is needed, sized `จัดการ` for its two valid buttons, and reset saved queue widths with storage key `v2`.
  - What is what: `ลูกค้า` is the primary variable-length business description column; `จัดการ` is a compact control column containing only the valid row actions.
  - Why it stays this way: remaining desktop width should improve business-data readability and balanced spacing, not render as an empty block inside the action column while customer names are truncated.
  - No receipt KPI, history-table layout, filters, account matching, create/edit/cancel flow, LINE routing, API contract, permissions, or database state changed.
  - Validation passed: red/green source contract, targeted ESLint, Next workspace type-check, npm audit with zero vulnerabilities, scoped diff check, and the local navigation/login/RSC guard across 106 extracted routes and 107 checks with zero failures.
  - Visual browser UAT was not run for this follow-up; the user-provided rendered screenshot was the visual defect evidence, and repository policy limits implementation requests to local compilation checks unless browser testing is explicitly requested.

- 2026-07-14: Active Next resizable-table balanced-width migration
  - A source-wide audit found 91 runtime `<colgroup>` blocks across 47 active Next TSX files that intentionally removed the final column width. The first contract scan caught 88 index-based exceptions; the manual follow-up found three key/dynamic-column variants in shared Master Data and Production Report, and the regression matcher was expanded to cover both forms. Because every affected table already renders through `table.ns-table` or the shared `Table` component at full container width, the old exception made the final status/action/numeric column absorb the widescreen remainder while earlier business names could still truncate.
  - Replaced the old final-column auto-stretch convention with one rule for every active resizable table: every `<col>` keeps the width returned by its existing `useResizableColumns().getColumnStyle()` call, including the final column. Existing per-table defaults, minimums, user-resized localStorage values, horizontal overflow, sorting, pagination, and responsive card alternatives remain intact.
  - Added a source-contract regression test that scans every active Next TSX `<colgroup>` and fails if a final-column width exception is reintroduced.
  - What is what: `defaultWidth`/`minWidth` describe each business column; `table.ns-table` fills the available shell; `tableMinWidth` preserves horizontal scrolling when the configured column sum is wider than the viewport.
  - Why it stays this way: remaining desktop width should improve the spacing of the whole table, not become one visually empty final cell—especially not an action/status cell—while customer, supplier, product, or document text is cut off earlier in the row.
  - This is a presentation-only migration. It does not change table data, calculations, filters, sorting keys, row actions, APIs, permissions, LINE routing, database schema, or database state.
  - Validation passed: the source contract was red on 88 index-based exceptions before the migration, the expanded audit covered all 91 variants and finished at zero, full ESLint completed with zero errors and one pre-existing warning in `scripts/qa-thai-font.tsx`, Next workspace type-check passed with a 4 GB Node heap after the default 2 GB run exhausted memory, full Vitest passed `69/69`, npm audit found zero vulnerabilities, full `git diff --check` passed, and the production build compiled and generated all 306 static pages.
  - Browser/DOM UAT was not run for this source-wide implementation batch; the contract test covers every active Next TSX `<colgroup>`, while rendered pixel-level comparison remains a separate explicitly requested browser QA step under the repository rules.

- 2026-07-14: Active Next subtle table-row divider follow-up
  - Added one global horizontal `1px` separator between consecutive body rows of every active `table.ns-table`. The first `slate-100` implementation passed the source contract but was visually too close to white; after the user reported that no line was visible, the rule moved to semantic `--color-scrap-line` (`#d9e2ec` in Light and `#334155` in Dark) so the divider remains thin but perceptible.
  - What is what: the row divider separates records horizontally; column spacing and alignment still separate fields vertically, so no vertical column borders were added.
  - Why it stays this way: long operational lists such as `/daily/weight-ticket-list` need a quiet visual guide for following one record across many columns, while heavy boxed grids would add noise around statuses and row actions.
  - Existing hover states, cancelled/status row backgrounds, table widths, resizing, sorting, pagination, mobile cards, data, actions, APIs, permissions, LINE routing, and database state remain unchanged.
  - Expanded the table source-contract test to require one shared divider rule plus the correct Light and Dark semantic-token mappings, and to prevent the generic Dark table-header rule from overriding divider color on `tbody th` cells.
  - Validation passed: the divider contract test was red while the rule still used `slate-100` and green after it moved to `--color-scrap-line`; targeted ESLint completed with zero issues, Next workspace type-check passed with a 4 GB Node heap, full Vitest passed `70/70`, npm audit found zero vulnerabilities, scoped and full `git diff --check` passed, and the production build compiled and generated all 306 static pages.
- Browser/DOM UAT was not run for this implementation batch; the user-provided `/daily/weight-ticket-list` screenshot is the visual requirement, while pixel-level review of the rendered divider remains a separate explicitly requested browser QA step under repository rules.

- 2026-07-14: Profit & Cost category filter checkpoint
  - `/profit-cost-analysis` now uses the shared searchable `SearchCombobox` for `หมวดสินค้า`, aligned with the supplier and customer filters.
  - What is what: the category filter selects one product group, while an empty value means `ทุกหมวดสินค้า`.
  - Why it stays this way: the old multi-select chips consumed filter-card space as the category list grew. The report workflow needs fast lookup of one category at a time, so the single-select searchable control is clearer and follows the closest existing filter pattern.
  - The UI sends one `metalGroup` query parameter. No report formula, data source, API calculation, permission, database schema, or DB state was changed.

- 2026-07-14: Profit & Cost product-table pagination checkpoint
  - The `สินค้า` tab now paginates the product report after applying the selected sort order. Users can select 10, 25, 50, or 100 rows per page, read the shown/total count, and navigate with `ก่อนหน้า` and `ถัดไป`.
  - What is what: sorting applies to the complete filtered result, then the current page renders its slice of that ordered result.
  - Why it stays this way: the product report can grow beyond a practical one-page table, while pagination preserves a predictable scan surface without changing report totals or filters.
  - Runtime formulas, data source, API contract, permissions, database schema, and DB state were not changed.

- 2026-07-14: Shared searchable-dropdown visual checkpoint
  - `SearchCombobox` now owns the shared dropdown-panel treatment: compact rows, a clear selected/focus state, and a soft slate dark surface matching the accepted supplier dropdown reference.
  - `/profit-cost-analysis` now also routes branch and purchase/sales channel filters through this shared searchable control, so all dropdown filters on that page have one interaction and visual treatment.
  - Native browser `select` panels remain unsuitable for this visual contract; future filter/master-data dropdown migrations must use `SearchCombobox` when search or consistent panel design is required.

- 2026-07-14: Profit & Cost ranking-card density checkpoint
  - The three Top 10 ranking cards now show the first 5 rows by default and each card can independently expand to show all 10 rows.
  - What is what: the ranking remains calculated from the complete Top 10 payload; the control only changes how many already-ranked rows are visible.
  - Why it stays this way: a five-row default keeps the dashboard/table boundary visible without losing access to the full Top 10 ranking when a user needs it.

- 2026-07-14: Profit & Cost receivable/payable KPI consolidation checkpoint
  - The four AP/AR and partner-count KPI cards were consolidated into two cards: `เจ้าหนี้คงเหลือ` includes the purchasing-supplier count, and `ลูกหนี้คงเหลือ` includes the selling-customer count.
  - What is what: the financial balance remains the primary KPI value; its related partner count is supporting context in the same card.
  - Why it stays this way: the count is useful context for an AP/AR balance but does not need an independent card, so the summary surface uses half the previous width without hiding information.

- 2026-07-14: Profit & Cost paired-metric consolidation checkpoint
  - `ซื้อรวม` and `ขายรวม` now share one two-column KPI card, retaining each amount and quantity. `ซื้อเฉลี่ย/กก.` and `ขายเฉลี่ย/กก.` likewise share one two-column KPI card.
  - What is what: each combined card preserves the two related business measures as separately labeled values; no value is concatenated into an ambiguous string.
  - Why it stays this way: purchase/sales and their average prices are comparison pairs. Showing them together reduces summary-card count while making the comparison faster to scan.

- 2026-07-14: Profit & Cost KPI hierarchy and dark-card checkpoint
  - The KPI area is now a two-row 12-column layout. The first row shows buy/sell, COGS, GP, stock quantity, and stock value; the second row shows average buy/sell price, AP, and AR.
  - Cards use a subdued shared dark surface. Neutral financial/stock metrics no longer receive arbitrary colored values; green is reserved for positive GP and red for negative GP.
  - What is what: AP/AR, stock, and COGS remain factual balances/costs, not warnings. GP is the only KPI whose color directly communicates positive or negative performance.
  - Why it stays this way: fewer saturated colors and a stable hierarchy reduce visual competition, so users can find profitability and balance information without scanning a rainbow of cards.

- 2026-07-14: Dark surface and table-border normalization checkpoint
  - Shared dark-theme tokens now use three calmer surface levels and transparent slate borders. Cards, panel boundaries, table shells, table headers, dividers, and hover states consume those tokens.
  - What is what: a normal border separates rows lightly; the stronger border is reserved for input, panel, and table boundaries where a structural separation is needed.
  - Why it stays this way: dark ERP surfaces should be organized by background hierarchy first. Reducing opaque borders prevents cards and dense tables from reading as a grid of competing outlines.
Sortable table-header baseline checkpoint on 2026-07-14: shared `ResizableTableHead` now renders stacked ascending/descending chevrons for every sortable column. Only the active sort-direction chevron uses blue while the inactive chevron and header label remain neutral; this makes the selected sort visible without adding a strong header background. This is a presentation/accessibility-only change: sorting state, ordering behavior, column resize, APIs, permissions, and data are unchanged.
